Upon arriving, they found the taverns dark and the doors unresponsive. It was already the third drum beat, late at night. They then invited Wang to their home, where they lit the lamp and drank together. As they laughed and chatted, the roosters began to crow chaotically. Wang stood up to leave, but Zhan Xing tried to hold him back. Wang said, "Drinking so heartily is delightful, but the government is dealing with a strange case, and I wish to investigate it. We cannot delay any further." Zhan Xing asked to go with him, but Wang refused. Zhan Xing insisted, and Wang replied, "Going is fine, but whatever you see, do not be startled, otherwise it will be of no benefit to either of us." Zhan Xing inquired about the reason, and Wang said, "I will tell you honestly, there will be a great change here. Today, the underworld lord is inspecting the spirits of robbery, which is a great ceremony in the underworld." Upon hearing this, Zhan Xing became even more eager to witness the event. Wang said, "If you persist in this, I will take you with me, but do not regret it if you become afraid." He took Zhan Xing's hand and led him to the threshold, where they stumbled. He pulled him up and continued walking together. Zhan Xing felt his body was as light as a leaf and flew to a place where the palace gates were majestic, and the beams and rafters were dark and sturdy.

    44

    入则院庭宏敞，地上密排利刃，矗立如笋，贯胸破腹者枕籍其上。中间搭一木板，仅容一人，张惧欲遁，王曳之，依其肘下，屏息而行。入重门，则甲士重重，斧钺林立。张惶恐，几不能步，乃杂众中，立殿檐下。俄闻堂鼓訇动，响若雷霆，披坚执锐人高声齐吼。见旌旗披拂中，一王者垂冕彩服，自屏后出，南面端坐，上下人众一齐拜舞。忽一吏虎首人身，奔上跪献方策，旋下堂传王旨。便闻门外哭声震地，断头缺臂者一拥而入，纷纷立阶下。王览册一阅，怒云：“人数尚少若干，何便持簿来？”弃簿于地，起立退屏后。于是万声号呼，乱如鼎沸，食顷始纷纷散去。张欲出而王已失所在，不觉失声一呼，旁一甲士以槌力挞其背，猛然而苏。盖死去已二日矣。时马贼往来于其处，动伤村人。次年遂有捻逆之变，人死如麻，始悟王者言人数尚少，为有因也。

    Explanation

    44

    Upon entering, the courtyard and hall were spacious, but the ground was densely lined with sharp blades, standing upright like bamboo shoots, with those who had been pierced through the chest and abdomen lying piled on top. In the middle, a narrow wooden board was placed, just wide enough for one person. Zhang, filled with fear, wanted to flee, but the king pulled him, having him lean against his elbow, and they walked cautiously. Upon entering the heavy gate, they found soldiers in armor in layers, with axes and halberds standing in rows. Zhang, in a state of extreme fear, almost unable to walk, stood among the crowd under the eaves of the hall. Soon, the hall drum boomed, sounding like thunder, and those wearing armor and holding weapons shouted loudly in unison. In the fluttering of banners, a king wearing a ceremonial crown and colorful robe emerged from behind the screen, sitting facing south in a dignified manner, while all the people above and below bowed and danced together. Suddenly, a figure with a tiger's head and a human body rushed up to kneel and present a square strategy, then descended from the hall to convey the king's command. Outside, the sound of weeping was heard, shaking the ground, as those with severed heads and missing arms rushed in, standing in disorder at the steps below. The king, after glancing through the book, angrily said, "There are still several fewer people, why did you bring the book?" He threw the book on the ground, stood up, and retreated behind the screen. Thus, ten thousand voices shouted, chaotic like boiling water, and after a meal's time, they scattered in disorder. Zhang wanted to leave, but the king had already disappeared. Unaware, he let out a cry, and a soldier beside him struck him with a club, and he suddenly woke up. It seemed he had been dead for two days. At that time, horse bandits were frequently passing through the area, causing harm to villagers. The following year, there was a rebellion led by the Nian, resulting in countless deaths, and one began to realize that the king's words about the number being insufficient had a reason.

    45

    颠僧
    　　同治壬戌夏，有颠僧不知何自来，坐邑城北野，蓝缕如丐，痴狂类颠。跛一足，自言能疗奇病，见者奇之。时瘟疫大作，邑城中每日死人无算，街巷纸幡披拂，比户相望。巫医奔走无停踵，病毙相继弗止。有闻僧名者，往求医治。僧殊不针灸药饵，撮土为丸与之。或施水，或拍病者肩顶身股，种种不一状。病者甘啖秽土浊浆，以为灵丹；忍受其拍打摩挲，以为施佛力。好事者传其灵异，云死者生，哑者语，跛者履，瞽者视。于是人挈瓶提壶乞其水，持篮携橐求其药。来者日众，传者日多。男妇老幼，摩肩接袂，缕缕然道左如行蚁。

    Explanation

    45

    Crazy Monk

    In the summer of 1862 during the reign of Emperor Tongzhi, there appeared a crazy monk whose origins were unknown. He sat in the northern outskirts of the county town, ragged and poor, resembling a beggar, and behaved in a manner similar to a lunatic. He had a lame foot and claimed he could cure strange diseases. Those who saw him found him remarkable. At that time, a severe epidemic broke out, and in the county town, people died by the hundreds every day. The streets were filled with paper banners and funeral flags, and households mourned one after another. Shamans and doctors ran about incessantly, but the deaths continued unabated. Those who heard of the monk's name went to seek his treatment. The monk did not use acupuncture, herbs, or medicine; instead, he formed soil into pills and gave them to the patients. Sometimes he gave them water, and sometimes he slapped the shoulders, tops, and limbs of the patients. His methods of treatment varied widely and were not uniform. The patients willingly consumed the dirty soil and murky water, believing it to be a miraculous elixir; they endured the monk's slapping and rubbing, believing it to be a form of divine intervention. Those who enjoyed spreading stories of miracles spread the monk's reputation, claiming that he had brought the dead back to life, cured the mute, restored the lame to walking, and given sight to the blind. Thus, people brought bottles and jugs to ask for the monk's water, and carried baskets and bags to ask for his medicine. The number of visitors and the spread of the monk's reputation increased daily. Men, women, old and young people crowded together, forming a continuous line along the road, like ants in a line.

    46

    病人艰于步履，扶杖至，肩舆至，路远者乘车至。无病者亦持器求永，兢兢然如获玉液。车马载道，人密如林，万口喧哗，万首攒动，乱如沸鼎。富室为扎席棚十余间，施瓮百十具，珍羞果饵，供给不绝，银钱钞票，不计其数。担夫不顾其业，日担水数桶以供人用。游手匪类相聚二百余人，供僧指使。僧令其灌水丸泥，以备施舍。邑素有火会，凡城市有火灾，即鸣钲为号，则聚集成伙，各持水具器械往扑灭之，乡俗然也。邑共百十余会，会各数百余人，水具等皆其预备。是时相聚担水，以注于瓮，不顾火灾。即有火灾，亦不暇顾。

    Explanation

    46

    Patients who find it difficult to walk arrive with the help of a cane, sedan chair, or carriage, depending on the distance. Even those without illness come with containers seeking the medicine, with great care as if they had obtained a precious elixir. The streets are filled with carriages and horses, and the crowds are dense like a forest, with thousands of voices shouting and heads moving, creating a chaotic scene like a boiling cauldron. Wealthy households set up over ten tent-like structures, placed jars in the hundreds, and provided delicacies and fruits without interruption, along with countless silver coins and paper money. The laborers, putting aside their usual work, carry several buckets of water each day to serve the crowd. Over two hundred idle and lawless people gather, following the orders of the monks. The monks instruct them to pour water and knead clay to make pills, preparing for distribution. The town has a traditional fire brigade; whenever there is a fire in the city, a bell is rung as a signal, and people gather in groups, each carrying water tools and equipment to extinguish the fire, a local custom. The town has over a hundred such groups, each with several hundred people, all equipped with water tools and other equipment. At that time, they gathered to carry water and pour it into the jars, without regard for fires. If there were a fire, they would have no time to attend to it.

    47

    水车来往道旁千万计，锣声人声相应，旌旗烂漫如云锦。香烟喷溢，高上青霄。夜则灯火遍野，远近繁杂如密星。华盖彩舆停道左，皆显贵之妻妾也。僧揭舆帘向美人洒土唾津，或摩面拭颐，笑云：“愈矣，愈矣。”即令舁归。有待半晌不得施治者，即闻轿内嘤嘤娇啼，泣曰“悔罪”，以为活佛不齿。僧一怒则抱首攒卧，众即跪前哀祷；喜则为病者拍肩摩顶一二状，则病者自以为幸甚荣甚。僧声价日高，衣亦华楚，行则人负之。后数百人拥挤相随，恐其或去。妇女粉汗淋漓，杂众中争曳僧足，云佛足也，摩之可以已灾，脱其履争擘足垢怀之。

    Explanation

    47

    Thousands of water wheels come and go by the roadside, the sound of gongs and the sound of people echoing each other, the colorful flags spread out like a brocade of clouds. The incense smoke rises high into the sky. At night, the lights spread across the fields, far and near, as dense as stars. Colorful carriages and umbrellas stop on the left side of the road, all belonging to the wives and concubines of the wealthy and noble. The monk lifts the curtain of a carriage, sprinkles dirt and spittle on a beautiful woman, or rubs her face and cheeks, smiling and saying, "Healed, healed." Then they are carried back. Those who wait for a long time without receiving treatment hear soft cries from inside the carriage, crying out "repentance," believing that the living Buddha disapproves of them. If the monk gets angry, he will hold his head and lie down, and the crowd will kneel in front of him and pray; if he is pleased, he will pat the shoulder and stroke the head of the sick person a few times, and the sick person will consider it a great honor. The monk's reputation grows daily, and his clothing is also luxurious, and when he walks, people carry him. Later, hundreds of people crowd around him, afraid that he might leave. Women with sweaty faces and bodies compete in the crowd to pull the monk's feet, saying they are the Buddha's feet, and rubbing them to ward off disasters, even tearing off their shoes to collect the dirt.

    48

    已而共碎其履，各持一片归。前面则男妇老弱数百辈，持香长跪以迎。街巷人口谈耳昕，手之指画，神之张皇，无不说僧灵异、言僧神奇者，且不敢出一亵语，恐有以达佛听而佛加罪也。邑之富户及上宪邑侯，均拈香，朝衣朝冠，如大典礼。于是谓其处曰“募安寺”。其地本荒冢，尽平之，宽约数亩。骸骨朽棺，狼藉当途，人马奔驰蹴踏成齑粉，令人不忍侧目视。将建寺于其处，募得金钱无数，砖瓦堆集如小山。一旦，僧不知何往。众失望曰：“呜呼！我佛飞升，我辈焉得从之？”后半载，得于缧绁之中。见者奇之，询其故，知与诸匪徒争钱相殴，兴讼被执也。于是神异如扫，而蓝缕如初。
    　　醉茶子曰：提壶灌顶，佛之真也。颠狂离奇，佛之似也。僧不能率其真，更不能窃其似，何也？贪得无厌，见利忘害，卒致故态依然，岂非无术甚乎？若当极盛之时，怀金遁去，人乌知其真伪哉！

    Explanation

    48

    After a while, they all broke their shoes, each holding a piece and returning. In front of them were hundreds of men, women, old, and weak people, holding incense and kneeling long to welcome. People in the streets and alleys talked and listened, pointing with their hands, and the gods were alarmed. No one did not say the monk was miraculous, and those who said the monk was miraculous. They dared not utter a single impure word, fearing that something might reach the Buddha's ears and the Buddha would punish. The wealthy households and the county magistrate all offered incense, in official robes and hats, as if in a grand ceremony. Thus they called the place "Mou'an Temple." The place was originally a field of graves, completely leveled, about several acres wide. Bones and rotten coffins were scattered on the road, trampled by people and horses into powder, making people unable to look away. Planning to build a temple there, they raised countless amounts of money, bricks and tiles piled up like small mountains. One day, the monk did not know where he went. The crowd disappointedly said, "Alas! Our Buddha has ascended, how can we follow him?" Half a year later, he was found in prison. Those who saw it found it strange, asked the reason, and knew he was fighting with bandits over money and was arrested for a lawsuit. Thus the miraculous aura vanished, and he returned to his original ragged state.

    Zuitchazi said: Pouring water on the head from a pot is the true Buddha. Crazy and strange is the semblance of the Buddha. The monk could not maintain his true nature, nor could he imitate his semblance, why? Greedy and insatiable, seeing profit and forgetting danger, eventually returning to his old state, isn't this a great lack of skill? If at the height of his success, carrying gold and fleeing, how could people know the truth?

    49

    信都翁
    　　信都翁，年已七旬，娶继室于氏，少艾，风流佻达，顾影自怜。翁老迈于思，屡向床头弃甲。氏与村中恶少通，遂与私奔。行十余里，妇足弱不能履。至一村，欲投宿处。村中有邵姓者，居临野寺。有学究授徒寺中，徒十余人，率皆远近村者。中有林生，乃邵姓之婿，时尚未婚，故与邵不通来往。适塾师他出，众生拟共饮，因难林曰：“汝能往邵家取下酒物来，不拘何物，不计多寡，我等即备东道，否则酒债汝自偿。”生踟蹰不愿往，众强之，不得已，诣岳家。邵翁媪皆往于田，家中惟邵女在。见生至，惊问故。生告以所求。女捡咸卵数枚付之，且速之行。生视家中无人，遽拥求欢。女云：“身既许汝，有何不从？但父母即归，两俱无颜。”生犹纠缠，女指空室云：“夜候于此，君其夜来。”盖托词以拒之也。生匆匆遂去。邵翁媪自田归，时已薄暮，门前有少年夫妇，哀求寄宿。

    Explanation

    49

    Mr. Xindu
    　　Mr. Xindu was already seventy years old when he married his second wife, Lady Yu, who was young and beautiful, free-spirited and unrestrained, and often looked at her reflection with self-pity. Due to his advanced age, Mr. Xindu frequently felt weak in bed. Lady Yu had an affair with a young man from the village and eloped with him. After traveling for more than ten miles, the woman's feet were weak and she could not continue. They arrived at a village and wanted to find a place to stay. There was a family surnamed Shao in the village, living near a temple. A scholar was teaching students in the temple, with about ten students from nearby villages. Among them was Mr. Lin, who was set to marry into the Shao family but was still unmarried, so he did not have contact with the Shao family. Just then, the private teacher left, and the students planned to drink together. They challenged Mr. Lin, saying, "If you can go to the Shao family and bring us some food for the feast, no matter what or how much, we will prepare the feast. Otherwise, you will have to repay the debt of wine." Mr. Lin hesitated but reluctantly agreed, and went to his father-in-law's house. Old Master and Old Mistress Shao were out working in the fields, and only the Shao family's daughter was at home. When she saw Mr. Lin, she was surprised and asked what he was doing there. Mr. Lin told her what he needed. The girl gave him several salted eggs and urged him to leave quickly. Mr. Lin saw that no one was at home and hastily tried to have sex with her. The girl said, "I have already given myself to you, what is there not to agree to? But if my parents return, both of us will be embarrassed." Mr. Lin still tried to persuade her, and the girl pointed to an empty room and said, "Wait for me here tonight, and you should come at night." This was an excuse to reject him. Mr. Lin hurriedly left. Old Master and Old Mistress Shao returned from the fields, and it was already dusk. There were a young couple at their doorstep, crying out for shelter.

    50

    翁怜之，遽纳入，使息于空室。生归塾，与众欢饮。席终，乘醉往邵家，直至空室，双扉虚掩。才上阶，闻室中低语，细审之，尽床笫狎亵词。疑女有他约，忿火中炽，摸砌下得利刀，遽持之，尽力猛斫，双头并落，呼叟至而责之。叟云：“小女伴老妻宿，安眠已久，汝所杀者何人？”生云：“痴老翁，尚佯作不知，尔女与奸夫耳。”烛之，乃昨寄宿之夫妇也。翁大惊，怼生曰：“此寄宿之人，尚未审其姓氏，尔遽杀之，重累老夫也。”生亦愕然，惊悔欲遁。翁挽其衣，两相争辩不休，而邻人满墙头矣。翁神色惶恐，并未询生因何而来。生肢体战栗，亦自忘其来因何事。媪与翁又相诟谇，邻人嘈杂，互为之解纷。正喧哗间，天已曙，一叟自外至。审视其尸，大称快事。盖寄宿者即于氏与恶少，叟即于氏之夫也。于是邵翁与生俱哀求叟，叟慨然曰：“是我所欲为而未能者。”重劳生焉，赴官自任，生得免。此道光戊申四月事。

    Explanation

    50

    The elderly man felt pity for him and hurriedly took him in, having him rest in an empty room. The young man returned to the school and drank merrily with others. When the banquet ended, he went to Mr. Shao's home drunk, up to the empty room, where the double doors were slightly ajar. As he stepped onto the threshold, he heard low whispers coming from inside the room. Carefully listening, he heard all indecent words being spoken. Suspecting that the daughter had another appointment, his anger flared up. Feeling for under the wall, he found a sharp knife and quickly took it. With all his strength, he stabbed fiercely, and both heads fell off. He called the old man to come and rebuked him. The old man said, "My daughter has been staying with my old wife and has been sleeping soundly for a long time. Who did you kill?" The young man said, "You foolish old man, pretending not to know, your daughter is with her adulterer." Lighting the candle to see, it turned out to be the couple who stayed here last night. The old man was greatly shocked and scolded the young man, saying, "This person who stayed here, we have not yet identified their surname, and you killed them so quickly, adding to my burden." The young man was also startled, regretting in shock and wanting to escape. The old man grabbed his clothes, and both parties argued without stopping, while neighbors filled the tops of the walls. The old man looked terrified and did not ask why the young man had come. The young man shivered all over and also forgot why he had come. The old woman and the old man argued again, and the neighbors were noisy, mediating between them. While they were arguing, the sky had already dawned, and an old man came from outside. Examining the bodies, he greatly praised the event. It turned out that the person who stayed here was Lady Yu and a ruffian, and the old man was Lady Yu's husband. Therefore, Mr. Shao and the young man both pleaded with the old man. The old man resolutely said, "This is what I wanted to do but could not." He troubled the young man to go to the authorities and take responsibility, and the young man was spared. This was an event in April of the Wuxin year of Daoguang.

    51

    醉茶子曰：报施之巧，至于如此之奇哉！是虽人为，未必无鬼神主持之也。顾人当老夫髦矣，尚不自知其无能为，势必至于燕尔之后，即便抡元。奉劝皤皤之老，毋娶袅袅之妻。设无越俎之林生，恐于飞之乐终让他人，而此耻不能雪矣。

    Explanation

    51

    醉茶子曰：The skill of reciprocating favors is so extraordinary! Though this may be human-made, it is not impossible that it is influenced by divine intervention. But when one is an old man, still unaware of his own limitations, he will inevitably achieve success after a period of youthful marriage. I advise the elderly not to marry young and graceful women. If there is no interference from someone who should not meddle, the joy of married life might be enjoyed by others, leaving this shame unavenged.

    52

    如意
    　　严生恩渥，豫人，将北上，途间遇雨，投一村舍。有颁白叟出迎，云：“舍宇无多，后院有草庐数椽，乃小女所居，前月物故，柩尚在堂，客如不嫌，室内可以下榻。”生思去此无宿处，不得已诺之。叟导入登堂，则素帏高悬，灵寝居中。复室内床幛光泽，器具精洁，洵为静女闺阁。叟为生具黍杀鸡，剉刍秣马，情极殷渥。自言刘姓，耄年无子，惟此女名如意，年十九，性敏心灵，精于刺绣，因瘵疾亡。语次唏嘘不已。生慰藉之。叟去后，生取几上书籍披阅，率皆野史稗编，知为女所素览者。再视他物，多半尘封。然窗上残绒，奁中剩粉，手迹犹宛然也，不觉心动，颇涉遐想。莲漏沉沉，伏枕未寝，忽堂中爆然作响，声自棺出。静听移时，室门又响，佳人已入室矣。

    Explanation

    52

    Ru Yi
    　　Yan Sheng, a man from the state of Yu, received great favor and was about to travel north. On his journey, he encountered rain and sought shelter in a rural house. An elderly man with white hair came out to greet him, saying, "There are not many rooms here, but there are a few thatched huts in the back courtyard, which were once my daughter's. She passed away last month, and her coffin is still in the hall. If you do not mind, you can stay in the room." Yan Sheng considered that he had no place to stay elsewhere and reluctantly agreed. The elderly man led him inside, where a white curtain hung high and the coffin was placed in the center. Inside the room, the bed curtains were shiny, and the utensils were clean and well-maintained, truly a quiet and refined lady's chamber. The elderly man prepared millet and killed chickens, cut hay, and fed the horse, showing his extreme warmth and graciousness. He introduced himself as Liu, an old man without children, and mentioned that his only daughter was named Ru Yi, who was nineteen years old, intelligent, and skilled in embroidery, but died of a wasting disease. As they spoke, they both sighed deeply. Yan Sheng comforted him. After the elderly man left, Yan Sheng picked up books from the table and read them, which were mostly historical and miscellaneous writings, knowing they were once read by the deceased daughter. He noticed that many other items were dusty, indicating neglect. However, remnants of the deceased daughter's presence, such as the remains of her embroidery on the window and the leftover powder in her cosmetics box, were still visible, stirring his emotions and thoughts. The water clock ticked away, and Yan Sheng lay down but was disturbed by a sudden noise from the coffin. He listened quietly for some time, and then the room door made a sound again, and a beautiful lady entered the room.

    53

    灯下视之，桃靥流丹，柳眉横翠，盈盈秋水，顾盼生波，谓生曰：“何物狂生，浼人床席！岂锦茵之上容汝酣睡邪？”生云：“香榻拂拭净洁，敬以待卿。仆何敢高卧，愿侍床下。”女曰：“卿是何称，汝敢卿我？如此轻薄，不如早杀之。”乃扪席底，取鸾刀，将刺其喉。生惊欲号，女掷刀笑曰：“懦夫，胆怯哉！侬非杀人者，所以操刀欲试，殆压尔狂悖耳。子远来疲倦，寄宿固不妨，何初会面，遽出游词？佳士不应尔尔。”生长揖谢过。女解颜欢笑云：“温存如侬，竟遭夭逝。命如纸薄，谁复见怜？”生云：“睹遗物已觉心酸，既见仙容，倍觉神荡。恨小生无福，不相逢于未故时也。”女云：“是不难。侬虽离形，魂气未散，尚足以侍君子。倘蒙垂怜，愿备小星。

    53

    By the light of the lamp, she looked at him. Her cheeks were flushed like peach blossoms, her brows arched like willow leaves, her eyes clear as autumn waters, and her glances full of charm. She said to him, "What kind of madman are you, disturbing my bed! Could it be proper for you to sleep soundly on such a luxurious bed?" He replied, "The fragrant bed has been cleaned and kept clean, I respectfully await you. How dare I lie down comfortably? I wish to serve at your feet." She said, "What title do you use for yourself? Dare you address me as an equal? Such disrespect, it would be better to kill you now." She then reached under the bed, took a phoenix knife, and was about to stab his throat. The man was shocked and wanted to scream, but she threw the knife and laughed, "Coward, you are afraid! I am not a killer; I was just trying the knife, to suppress your outrageous behavior. You have traveled far and are tired; staying here is not a problem. Why, just meeting for the first time, do you speak so casually? A good man should not act like this." He bowed deeply to apologize. She smiled and laughed, "Gentle and kind like me, who ended up dying young. A life as thin as paper, who else will pity me?" He said, "Seeing her belongings already makes me feel sad; having seen her divine appearance, I feel even more enchanted. I regret that I was not fortunate enough to meet her before she died." She said, "It's not difficult. Although I am no longer in my physical form, my spirit has not dispersed; I can still serve a gentleman. If you show mercy, I wish to be your companion."

    54

    ”生踌躇有难色，女不怿，曰：“侬含羞自荐，意殊不恶。君何相拒之深？岂览物冥想者，非子也耶？”生曰：“佳偶如卿，夫复何憾？第仙凡路隔，恐不利于生人。”女云：“不妨，请明日同行，伪为娶他乡者，谁复知之？”生辞以逆旅往还，诸多窒碍，俟归途相迓。女许之，出室门而没。生秉烛待旦。及晓，叟来，尽以情告，且求祝免。叟闻言惊异，云：“弱息死后，从无怪异，岂其魂果归来？”乃奔告媪。媪泣曰：“适梦女来，言严生将聘为妻，乞予为之择吉。事虽幻妄，然已死之蚕，丝狁未断，情殊可悯也。”言次大痛，泪落如雨。生窥其状，心亦悲楚，然计无所出，辞翁欲行。时疏雨初歇，湿云尚浓，翁挽留，生不可，跨马遂去。

    Explanation

    54

    The protagonist hesitated, showing signs of difficulty. The girl was displeased and said, "I, in my shyness, offer myself, and the intention is not at all bad. Why do you reject me so deeply? Is it not you who meditates on things?" The protagonist replied, "If you are such a good match, what more could I desire? However, there is a great divide between the divine and the mortal, which may not be good for a mortal." The girl said, "It does not matter. Let us pretend to marry someone from another place, and who would know?" The protagonist cited the many obstacles in traveling back and forth to an inn and suggested meeting on the way back. The girl agreed, and she left through the door and disappeared. The protagonist waited by the candle until dawn. By morning, the old man came and told him everything, and he also asked for a blessing to avoid any misfortune. The old man was surprised and said, "After my daughter's death, there has been no strange occurrence. Could her spirit have truly returned?" He then rushed to tell the old woman. The old woman cried and said, "Just now I dreamed that the girl came and said that Yan Sheng will marry her, asking me to choose a good day. Although it is a dream, the dead silkworm still has its silk thread unbroken, which is quite pitiful." She was greatly grieved, and tears fell like rain. The protagonist saw her state and also felt sad, but having no plan, he bid farewell to the old man and wanted to leave. At that time, light rain had just stopped, and the wet clouds were still thick. The old man tried to persuade him to stay, but he could not, so he mounted his horse and left.

    55

    行五十余里，雷声轰轰，暴雨又作，急投前途旅舍，脱湿衣灯前火熨。忽竹帘钩响，女探身入，谓生曰：“自揣尚不甚陋，何深恶而痛绝之，致予贻老亲羞？敢问其故。”生云：“婚姻不可苟合，必告父母。予归禀命而行，事无不济。若学相如、文君，虽死不愿也。”女云：“钻穴逾垣，妾亦素鄙。昨曾示梦于母，君岂未之闻耶？烦备一肩舆，妾随君至家，父母国人，谁或贱之？惟一事奉恳，行时需待薄暮，白昼日光照耀，妾实难堪。”生云：“俟予归时，即便奉行。”女云：“妾候君于青陵台畔，此处去君家不远，谅不为君累也。”生唯唯，女遂去。生入都，羁留数月，不敢归。或教以迂途而返，生从之。将至家，日已暮，见女候于道左，责其爽约。生下骑，对以诡词，女怒稍解。扶女上马，相将至逆旅，与生同食寝，不异生人。

    Explanation

    55

    After traveling more than fifty miles, the thunder rumbled loudly, and the heavy rain resumed. Hurriedly seeking shelter at a nearby inn, the man removed his wet clothes and ironed them by the lamp. Suddenly, the bamboo curtain hook made a noise, and the woman leaned in, saying to the man, "I thought I was not very ugly. Why do you deeply hate and utterly despise me, bringing shame to my elderly parents? I dare to ask the reason." The man replied, "Marriage cannot be hasty or casual; one must inform one's parents. I will return home to seek permission and then proceed, and nothing will go wrong. If you learn from Sima Xiangru and Zhuo Wenjun, even if it means death, I would not agree to it." The woman said, "Digging holes and climbing walls, I have always despised such behavior. Yesterday, I showed my mother a dream; did you not hear about it? Please prepare a sedan chair, and I will follow you to your home. Our parents, countrymen, and anyone would not look down on us. I have one request to make: when traveling, it must be late afternoon. In broad daylight with the sun shining, I truly cannot bear it." The man said, "When I return home, I will immediately comply." The woman said, "I will wait for you at the foot of Qingling Terrace. This place is not far from your home; I am sure it will not be a burden for you." The man nodded affirmatively, and the woman left. The man went to the capital and stayed there for several months, reluctant to return. Someone advised him to take a roundabout route back, and he followed the advice. As he approached home, the day was already evening. He saw the woman waiting on the side of the road and reproached her for breaking the agreement. The man dismounted and responded with a deceptive excuse, and the woman's anger somewhat subsided. He helped the woman onto the horse and escorted her to an inn, where they ate and slept together, no different from ordinary people.

    56

    时生尚未娶，计亦良得。次日薄暮，备肩舆夜行。未晓舁至家，拜姑与二嫂，礼仪肃然。生托云：“女病羞明，常避烈日。操作须待灯下。”母信之。居半载，夫妇情倍笃。女事姑孝，姑亦嬖爱过于常情。二嫂自愧勿如，因忌生，思谋害女，饮以鸩酒，女宴如。二嫂私议曰：“是女踪迹诡异，非妖即鬼，半载从不归宁，且不见有母家人至，真可疑也。”令两兄向生苦诘，备得其实。乃白诸母，且云恐不利于生。母大恐，遂遣术士符咒，卒无验。适有老道士须发皓白，形貌权奇，托钵于门外。或问道士何能，答云：“能除鬼魅，疗奇疾。”二嫂闻之，急延入。道士作法于庭，禹步念咒，置钵于地。旋见女从室中奔出，状甚狼狈，至钵前扑地化为轻烟而灭。生大痛。

    Explanation

    56

    At that time, the man had not yet married, and the plan was also quite good. The following evening, just before dusk, they prepared a sedan chair for a night journey. Before dawn, they arrived at the house, where the man knelt to pay respects to his mother-in-law and two sisters-in-law, and the ceremony was solemn and respectful. The man explained, "The daughter-in-law is ill and afraid of bright light, often avoiding the intense sun. She must work under the lamp." The mother believed him. After half a year, the relationship between husband and wife became even more intimate. The daughter-in-law was filial to her mother-in-law, and the mother-in-law also loved her more than usual. The two sisters-in-law felt ashamed and could not compare, so they envied the husband and thought of harming the daughter-in-law. They gave her poison, but she remained unaffected as if nothing happened. The sisters-in-law privately discussed and said, "This woman has strange and mysterious behavior, either a demon or a ghost. For half a year, she never returned home, and no relatives from her mother's side came. It is indeed suspicious." They made their brothers-in-law interrogate the husband harshly and obtained the truth. They reported to the mother-in-law, saying they feared it would be harmful to the husband. The mother was greatly alarmed and sent a sorcerer to perform talismans and spells, but finally, there was no effect. Just then, an old Taoist with white hair and beard, and a strange appearance, was begging outside the door. Someone asked the old Taoist what he could do, and he replied, "I can expel ghosts and demons, treat strange diseases." The sisters-in-law heard this and hurriedly invited him in. The old Taoist performed rituals in the courtyard, stepping in the manner of the Great Yu and reciting incantations, placing his begging bowl on the ground. Soon, the daughter-in-law rushed out of the room, looking very disheveled, and when she reached the begging bowl, she fell to the ground and transformed into a light mist, disappearing. The husband was deeply grieved.

    57

    道士曰：“公子勿忧，鬼偶何可以长？贫衲自有善法。”生不答。道士持钵欲行，二嫂酬以钱，不受，顾谓二嫂曰：“党邪陷正，鬼神难欺。尔辈好自为之。”出门竟去。生从此怏怏不乐。有为生议婚者，低昂均不当意。生之姨有一女，貌美而患颠疾，居常裸衣披发，目不识人。举家患之，以故及笄犹无问字者。一日，其母立门外，遇一老道士，托钵而前，自云能疗诸疾。母告以所苦，求为施治。道士曰：“疗颠不难，但女公子愈后，宜速为议婚，不然，疾终不能瘳。”母云：“婚嫁所以迟迟者，以儿病颠也。颠果愈矣，迟之何为？”乃导入女室。道士以朱笔书符，形如蚯蚓，烧灰令女吞之。复举钵咒诅，向女一挥，有黑烟自体出，绕女三匝，忽氤氲入女口中而没。道士曰：“愈矣。”辞母而去。

    Explanation

    57

    The Taoist said, "Young master, do not worry. How can a ghostly figure last long? The humble monk has his own good method." The young man did not reply. The Taoist held his begging bowl and was about to leave when the younger sister-in-law offered him money, but he refused. Turning to her, he said, "Favoring evil and harming righteousness, the ghosts and gods cannot be deceived. You people should do as you please." Then he bid farewell and left. The young man was discontented and unhappy ever since. Those who proposed marriage for him found neither high nor low satisfactory. The young man's aunt had a daughter who was beautiful but suffered from a mental illness. She usually appeared naked with disheveled hair and was unable to recognize people. The whole family was troubled by it, and thus, even after reaching the age of marriage, she still had no suitor. One day, her mother stood outside the door and encountered an old Taoist who held a begging bowl and approached her. He said he could cure all diseases. The mother told him of her daughter's condition and asked for treatment. The Taoist said, "Treating the mental illness is not difficult, but after the young lady recovers, it is advisable to quickly arrange her marriage. Otherwise, the illness will never be cured." The mother replied, "The reason we have delayed the marriage is because of the daughter's illness. If she truly recovers, why delay further?" The Taoist then led her into the girl's room. He wrote a talisman with red ink, shaped like a worm, burned it to ashes, and made the girl swallow it. Then he lifted the begging bowl and chanted incantations, waving it toward the girl. Black smoke emerged from her body, circled around her three times, and suddenly became misty, entering her mouth and disappearing. The Taoist said, "She is cured." He bid farewell to the mother and left.

    58

    母见女奄奄如睡，俄而欠呻如初醒，张目四顾，颠疾若失，索汤水自盥浴，整衣理发，登床抛绣，剪烛观书，较昔年转增聪慧。生母闻女甥疾愈，来视，见其举止端凝，真无痴态，大悦，为生聘焉。女母亦愿以生为婿，遂许之。择吉亲迎入门，贤慧无殊于刘女。生喜甚。一日，女谓生曰：“君以我为何人？”生笑曰：“尔我姨之女，夫何待言。得毋又犯颠耶？”女曰：“非也。侬乃君之鬼妻如意也。道士乃仙人，悯妾无罪，摄妾魂去，纳诸斯人躯壳之中，所谓借躯而生也。彼颠女之数当终，我两人之缘未断，皆真人之法术为之。此后当金铸仙象，以答其恩。”生如其言，铸道士小像供诸内室，夫妇朝夕虔拜。二嫂知其故，惧女报怨，谮于母，言其妖异，粉饰多词。

    Explanation

    58

    The mother saw her daughter lying there, as if asleep, but soon she yawned and groaned as if just waking up, opened her eyes and looked around, and suddenly changed her manner, as if losing her usual self. She asked for water and washed herself, tidied her clothes and combed her hair, got into bed and threw away her embroidery, trimmed the candle and read a book. Compared to before, she seemed even more intelligent. When the mother-in-law heard that her daughter-in-law had recovered, she came to see her. Seeing her daughter-in-law's dignified and composed behavior, she was very pleased and agreed to the marriage. The mother of the daughter was also willing to have the son-in-law as her son-in-law, so they agreed. They chose a good day for the wedding and brought the bride into the home. She was as virtuous and wise as Liu's daughter. The son was very happy. One day, the daughter said to him, "What do you think I am?" The son laughed and said, "You are my aunt's daughter. What else do you need to say? Are you going to be crazy again?" The daughter said, "No. I am your ghost wife, Ru Yi. The Taoist priest is a deity, and he felt sorry for me, having done nothing wrong, and took my soul away, placing it in this person's body. This is what is called 'borrowing a body to live.' The mad girl's fate was supposed to end, but our two people's fate was not yet complete. This is all thanks to the real person's magic. In the future, we should cast a golden statue of the deity to repay his kindness." The son followed her words and cast a small statue of the Taoist priest, which they placed in the inner room, and they worshipped it devoutly morning and evening. The sister-in-law knew the reason, feared that the daughter would seek revenge, and slandered her to the mother-in-law, saying that she was strange and unusual, embellishing with many words.

    59

    母怒曰：“噫嘻！我姊之女岂亦谓之鬼物耶？彼数年前病颠，诸医罔效。适有道士为之治愈，事诚有之。渠不忘其德，铸像供奉，于理亦当。何可谓之为妖异！若云借躯而生，事近荒诞，有何证据？”二女词穷，又谓：“道士恐非真仙，不可敬信。”母曰：“阿姨延治疾之道士，即故等延驱鬼之道士。果妖道也，汝辈胡为招之来？”二人惭退。母益宠女，而二妇动遭白眼矣，于是衔怨益深。一日早炊，置砒于饼饵，将毒女。适二妇出，为童子颠倒而二妇不知也，归而取饼各啖其子，遂并毙。二妇悔恨无及，谮二兄与生析箸。生本中牟人，兹移居于河阴广武山南，买薄田，创庐屋，迎母终养。言其情于刘翁媪，两家往来如翁婿。乞得如意之柩，葬诸吹台之侧。每春秋佳节，夫妇同造其墓，登高瞻望，悲喜交集焉。

    Explanation

    59

    The mother became furious and exclaimed, "Ah! Could my sister's daughter also be called a ghostly being? She fell ill with madness several years ago, and all the doctors were ineffective. Just then, a Taoist priest cured her, and it is indeed true. She did not forget his kindness and cast an image to worship him, which is reasonable according to the principles. How can she be called an evil spirit! If one says she was reborn in another body, the matter is nearly absurd, what evidence is there?" The two women were at a loss for words and said, "The Taoist priest might not be a true immortal, and he should not be revered." The mother replied, "The aunt invited the Taoist priest who treated her illness, and he is the same one we invited to exorcise the ghost. If he is an evil Taoist, why did you invite him?" The two women were ashamed and retreated. The mother grew even more fond of her daughter, while the two women were often treated with disdain, and thus their resentment deepened. One day, during the early morning cooking, they placed arsenic in the cakes, intending to poison the daughter. Just then, the two women left, and the servant boy made a mistake, which the two women did not know. They returned and each took a cake to feed their child, and thus both children died. The two women were filled with regret and slandered the two brothers and the son-in-law. The son-in-law was originally from Zhongmou and had moved to the south of Guangwu Mountain in Heyin, where he bought a small piece of land and built a house, bringing his mother to live with them in filial piety. He explained the situation to the elderly couple Liu, and the two families interacted like father-in-law and son-in-law. They obtained the coffin of their wish and buried it beside the Chuitai. On every spring and autumn festival, the couple would visit her grave together, climb high, and look out, feeling a mixture of sorrow and joy.

    60

    醉茶子曰：离魂倩女，信有之耶？而一念钟情，百端魔障，遂使砒毒鸠酒，几杀其身。设非贤能孝姑，何以得吉神拥护哉！
    柳儿
    　　季生子野，燕南人。父荣，燕之名秀才。庭训綦严，生又性敏，以故才捷能文，尤工词翰。弱冠入泮。世家争婚之，父俱未许。适狂盗犯境，兵甲如林。土枭乘势扰乱，白昼御人，举县老弱奔窜。生家积粮为村人掠去，荣携眷避于上谷。有中表亲朱某，是处富绅，为生家赁庑分廪，供给勿衰，留荣于其家，襄办村团，月给薪水，荣甚感之。生因客居仓卒，诵读辄疏，日惟散步村外释闷。村中有王姓缝工，与生对门居。王妻三十许，风致姣丽，不类村妇。有女名柳儿，貌美尤过其母，尝随母碾米于比邻，日凡三四往，必过生门。生立门外，女携箕帚自对面来，裙布荆钗，殊无艳饰，而发盘高髻，秀眉在骨。生不禁神荡，目送女去远，始返身归。由是冥想梦寐都萦，早起不暇洗漱，即俟诸门外。

    60

    The Tea Drinker said: Is it truly the case that there are beautiful maidens who have left their souls? And yet, a sudden infatuation, countless obstacles, led to the use of poison and toxic wine, nearly killing her. Had it not been for the virtuous and filial daughter-in-law, how could she have been protected by auspicious spirits?

    Liu'er

    Ji Shengzi, a native of the southern part of Yan, had a father named Rong, who was a famous scholar of Yan. The family's education was extremely strict, and Ji was naturally intelligent, so he excelled in writing and was particularly skilled in literature. At the age of twenty, he entered the school. Families competed to marry him, but his father refused them all. Just as bandits invaded the territory, with an army like a forest. Local bandits took advantage of the chaos to cause disturbances, attacking people in broad daylight, causing the entire county's elderly and weak to flee. Ji's family's stored grain was taken by villagers, and Rong took his family to Upper Valley to seek refuge. A relative-in-law named Zhu, a wealthy and influential person in the area, rented a room and shared his granary with the family, continuing to provide without faltering. He left Rong in his home, assisting in organizing the village militia, paying a monthly salary, for which Rong was greatly grateful. Ji, living in a foreign place in haste, found that his reading became less frequent, so he would only take walks outside the village to relieve his boredom. In the village, there was a tailor named Wang who lived opposite Ji's house. Wang's wife, in her thirties, was graceful and beautiful, unlike a typical village woman. They had a daughter named Liu'er, who was even more beautiful than her mother. She would often accompany her mother to grind rice at a neighboring house, going there three or four times a day, always passing by Ji's house. Ji would stand outside his door, watching as the girl came from the opposite side, carrying a winnowing basket and broom, wearing a simple skirt and plain hairpins, without any elaborate decoration, but with her hair coiled in a high bun and her beautiful eyebrows prominent. Ji could not help but be captivated, watching her until she was far away, then turning and returning. From then on, he would think of her constantly, even in his dreams, waking up so early that he didn't have time to wash, and waiting outside his door.

    61

    将午女来，细瞻裙下，双翘细锐如笋，益觉爱慕，伫立多时，睛不能转。女母来，生始退入门内。女母已察其意，从此不令女出，日惟自己操作。生大失所望，咏忆柳诗百首，辗转思量，情辞悱恻。一日踟蹰门外，负手听蝉，忽足下锵然落一物，视之，银指环也。骇而四顾，见女在门外，冁然微笑，见生返身遽入，行数步又回顾，笑指指环，欲生收藏者。生会意，急捡起纳诸袖中。再视柳儿，已掩扉入。归而秘藏于箧，人无知者，遂赋诗曰：“银指环，如月弯。向疑在天上，端自落人间。银指环，白如雪。欲去问青娥，幽情无人说。”未几，贼氛已退，荣议还乡，买一巨舟，装载行李，待风顺起程。生不怿，终日立门外，俟女出，示以意旨，而女杳无见期。适布帆翩翩作响，荣命家人登舟，中流击楫，片刻已十余里。

    Explanation

    61

    As noon approached, the girl came out, and the boy looked carefully at the hem of her skirt, noticing her feet, which were rounded, slender, and sharp like bamboo shoots. He felt even more deeply admiring of her, standing still for a long time, unable to move his eyes. When the girl's mother arrived, the boy finally retreated inside. The girl's mother had already noticed his feelings and decided not to let her daughter go out, so the girl spent her days doing her own tasks. The boy was greatly disappointed and composed a hundred poems about Liu'er, pondering their feelings and words, which were filled with sorrow. One day, he hesitated outside the door, standing with his hands behind his back, listening to the cicadas. Suddenly, something fell with a ringing sound at his feet. He looked down and saw a silver finger ring. Startled, he looked around and saw the girl outside the door, smiling with joy. She saw the boy turn and hurriedly go inside, walked several steps, and then looked back, smiling and pointing at the ring, indicating that he should keep it. The boy understood her meaning and quickly picked it up, putting it in his sleeve. He looked at Liu'er again, who had closed the door and gone inside. He returned and secretly stored the ring in a chest, no one knowing about it. He then composed a poem saying: "A silver finger ring, like a crescent moon. Previously thought to be in heaven, it actually fell to earth. A silver finger ring, white as snow. Wanting to go ask the beautiful girl, deep feelings have no one to tell." Not long after, the bandit threat had subsided, and Rong discussed returning home, buying a large boat, loading it with luggage, and waiting for favorable winds to set sail. The boy was unhappy and stood outside the door all day, waiting for the girl to come out, to show his intention, but the girl never showed up. Just as the sailcloth fluttered and made a sound, Rong ordered his family members to board the boat, paddling in the middle of the stream, and in a short while, they had traveled more than ten miles.

    62

    生望洋兴叹，无可奈何，恨不即生双翼，飞过长河。而一作此想，便觉身轻如叶，倏忽飘到北岸。信步前行，所经并非故道，林木蓊郁，间杂荆榛，有数椽茅屋，四围遮以豆篱，疑是村舍，急趋问路。至篱边，寂寂无人，直至檐下，闻屋中嘤嘤悲泣。怪而审顾，一女子红绡掩面，呜呜娇啼。方欲退步，闻屋女子云：“庭前季郎耶？弃我而去，胡为复来？”生视之，柳儿也。不觉悲哽自剖，声泪俱堕。女出以红巾，为生拭泪，谓生曰：“父母之前可以婉言示意，愁恩何为？君之戚朱某，若作媒，事无不成，何不归而谋之？妾为阿母禁制，不敢轻出户庭，今而后惟有守死以待，弃否一听于君。”语毕，退入室中。生欲随之，忽村尨乱吠，一惊而寤，身仍倦卧舟中。归里后以梦私告诸母，母商诸父。

    Explanation

    62

    The protagonist, overwhelmed by the vastness of the river, sighed in despair, feeling utterly helpless. He wished he could grow wings to fly over the long river. As soon as he entertained this thought, he felt his body as light as a leaf, suddenly drifting to the northern bank. Walking leisurely forward, he found himself on a path unfamiliar to him, surrounded by dense forests and wild thorns. There stood a small thatched cottage, enclosed by bean fences, which he suspected was a rural dwelling. He hurried to ask for directions. Upon reaching the fence, he found the place quiet and deserted. He walked up to the eaves and heard a soft, sorrowful weeping coming from inside the house. Curious, he carefully looked around and saw a woman covering her face with red silk, sobbing gently. Just as he was about to step back, he heard the woman in the house ask, "Is it you, my dear Season? You abandoned me and left, why have you come back?" The protagonist recognized her as Liu'er. Overcome with sorrow, he confessed his feelings, his voice choked with tears. The woman came out with a red cloth to wipe his tears and said to him, "You can hint gently with soft words before my parents, why worry about this? Your relative Zhu, if he acts as a matchmaker, nothing will be impossible. Why not return home and discuss it? I am restricted by my mother and dare not leave the house lightly. From now on, I will only wait until death, whether to abandon or not is up to you." After speaking, she retreated into the room. The protagonist wanted to follow her, but suddenly, village dogs began to bark wildly, and he was startled awake, still tiredly lying in the boat. After returning home, he privately told his mothers about the dream, and they discussed it with his fathers.

    63

    父以其缝工女贱之，又以路远娶聘非易，遂寝其事。生知计不行，愁郁成疾，日惟啜薄粥盏许。冉冉光阴，又至春日，拂檐垂柳，才黄欲匀，生书一绝于纸云：“云鬟雾鬓本多姿，记得相逢一笑时。转盼韶华浑似梦，独怜春柳挂情丝。”书毕倦卧，睡去。稿为生父见之，甚厌其事，而又怜子病，含怒而未之发。会清明节，村中游女如云，好事者随诸郊外，生亦杂众中。日将暮，人渐稀，途次遇一老妪立道左，顾生久之，谓生曰：“若个好男儿，眉目清扬，神色一何郁郁！倘有心事，老身愿效绵薄。”生叹云：“心事诚有，但恐姥姥无能为力耳。”妪云：“恐郎无甚心事，果有之，某无不能为。”生以其言异，尽以情告。妪笑云：“是何难哉！使今日不遇老身，则郎终当以情死。”生固求援手。妪云：“去此半里许有小庄，彼王氏母女寄居于其间。如不信，请偕往。”生欣然从之。

    Explanation

    63

    The father, regarding his daughter as lowly due to her skill in sewing, and considering the distant journey and the difficulty of marriage arrangements, abandoned the matter. The young man, knowing his plan was unfeasible, fell ill from worry and depression, only drinking a little thin porridge each day. Time passed slowly, and another spring arrived. The willow branches by the eaves were just turning yellow and looking uniform. The young man wrote a quatrain on a piece of paper: "Her hair is like clouds and mist, naturally beautiful. Remembering the moment we met and smiled. Turning to look, the beautiful years seem like a dream. Only love the spring willows hanging with emotional threads." After finishing writing, he tiredly lay down and fell asleep. The draft was seen by his father, who greatly disliked the matter but also pitied his son's illness. He held his anger but did not express it. On the Qingming Festival, the village girls were like clouds. Those who enjoy it followed them to the outskirts, and the young man also joined the crowd. As the day was ending, people gradually became fewer. On the way, he met an old woman standing on the side of the road. She looked at him for a long time and said, "What a good young man! With clear and bright eyebrows and eyes, his expression was so gloomy. If you have something on your mind, I am willing to help." The young man sighed and said, "I do have something on my mind, but I fear you are powerless." The old woman replied, "I fear you have nothing on your mind, but if you do, I can help." The young man, finding her words unusual, told her everything. The old woman laughed and said, "What is so difficult! If I had not met you today, you would have died of your emotions." The young man firmly requested her help. The old woman said, "About half a li away, there is a small village. The Wang family mother and daughter are staying there. If you don't believe me, please go with me." The young man happily followed.

    64

    至一处，茅屋数椽，豆篱环绕，芳草古树，明翳甚浓，景物与梦中无异，怪而问曰：“得毋梦中耶？”妪云：“分明我引郎来，何得云梦？”生云：“向梦此境，今固疑之。”妪云：“真境，何必多疑？”生云：“清明时节，篱上豆花何蓓蕾也？”妪云：“生醉乎？请细觇之。”生再视，则竹编麂眼，并无豆花，惟细草葺葺而已。相将入室，王氏含笑出迎，见生云：“年余不见，憔悴竟如此矣。”生泣诉其故。妇云：“尊翁自高门阀，痛绝姻好，岂我女如道旁苦李，无人拾者？我固知郎君至诚，故烦俞姆招郎来，一谈胸臆。联姻诚愿，但须尊翁诚意而求，不然谓我缝工女，岂真不能占凤于清门耶？”生婉辞谢过，俞姆又代为之说词。妇沉吟云：“若欲附为婚姻，当赘诸吾家。如不愿，请郎即行。

    Explanation

    64

    To the place, there were a few thatched cottages, surrounded by bean trellises, with fragrant grass and ancient trees, the shade being very dense. The scenery was no different from what was seen in a dream. The man was surprised and asked, "Isn't this a dream?" The woman said, "I clearly led you here, why ask if it's a dream?" The man said, "I once dreamed of this place, now I doubt it." The woman said, "It's a real place, why be so doubtful?" The man asked, "During the Qingming Festival, why are the bean flowers on the trellis still blooming?" The woman said, "Are you drunk? Please look closely." The man looked again and saw that the screen was made of woven bamboo, resembling a deer's eye, with no bean flowers, only lush and dense grass. Together they entered the room, and the woman greeted him with a smile. Seeing the man, she said, "It's been over a year since we last met, and you look so thin and worn out." The man wept and told her the story. The woman said, "Your father has high social standing and has rejected all marriage proposals. Is my daughter like a bitter plum by the roadside, no one wants her? I knew you were sincere, so I asked Mrs. Yu to invite you here to discuss our feelings. We truly wish to marry our daughter to you, but your father must be sincere in his request. Otherwise, he's treating my daughter as a seamstress, and she truly can't marry into a noble family." The man politely declined and apologized. Mrs. Yu again tried to persuade him. The woman hesitated and said, "If you want to marry our daughter, you must come to live in our family. If you don't want to, please leave immediately."

    65

    ”生急云：“愿愿！”于是扫除内室，铺设床幛，遣俞姆妆女而出，上堂交拜，即夕成礼。生视女光艳倍胜曩日，遂相欢悦。询女胡为来此，云：“妾为俞姥将来，不料妾母先在此，遂侨居焉。顾妾日在闺中，不知此名何里，询诸俞姥，谓此为俞氏庄云。”生信之，缱绻月余，情同胶漆。忽念大事已定，当告父母，或可携妻归，不然，淹留岳家，胡可长也？乃商之于女，女未决。自念一去即返，何必斟酌，不白女而出村。甫行数武，回顾并无村落，垒垒高冢，环以松柏。大骇，寻途而归。至家，则父母方以寻生不得，相对悲泣，泪痕犹未干。见生来，大悦。询其故，生以实对，遂相惊为遇妖，生亦深恐。父母禁生不令其出游，急为生择婚。数家均未就，因有结姻王氏之心，乃修书致朱。书未发而朱自上谷来，荣述其事，且言所求。朱大称怪事。

    Explanation

    65

    The man hurriedly said, "Certainly!" Then he swept the inner room and laid out the bed curtains, sent the maid to dress the woman and send her out, conducted the formal bowing ceremony in the hall, and completed the wedding rites that evening. The man found the woman's beauty and radiance far surpassing what he had seen before, and they were soon filled with joy. He asked the woman why she had come there, and she replied, "I was brought here by Old Lady Yu, but I did not expect my mother to already be here, so I have been residing temporarily. However, I was confined to the inner quarters every day, unaware of the name of this place. I asked Old Lady Yu, and she said this is Yu's estate." The man believed her, and they spent more than a month in deep affection, their relationship as close as glue and lacquer. Suddenly, he thought of the major matter that had been settled, and he should inform his parents, perhaps to take his wife back home, or otherwise, staying at his father-in-law's house indefinitely was not a good idea. He discussed this with the woman, but she was undecided. He thought to himself that if he went away and returned immediately, there was no need to deliberate, so without telling her, he left the village. After only a few steps, he looked back and saw no villages, only pile upon pile of high tombs surrounded by pines and cypresses. Greatly frightened, he found his way back. When he arrived home, his parents were just then searching for him and were crying together, their tears still not dried. Seeing him come, they were greatly pleased. When they asked him why he had gone, he told them the truth, and they were shocked, thinking he had met a demon. The man was also deeply afraid. His parents forbade him from going out and hurriedly arranged a marriage for him. Several families did not agree, but there was a plan to marry him to a Wang family. So, he wrote a letter to Zhu. Before the letter was sent, Zhu came from Shanggu, recounted the events, and mentioned his request. Zhu greatly called it a strange matter.

    66

    荣问故，朱云：“自君家归后，王氏女奄奄抱病，察其意，似为生而病者。春时扑蝶村外，忽不返，家人寻访殆遍，踪影全杳。月余忽自归，问之，云：扑蝶时遇一老妪，自云俞姓，邀登车上，其行迅驶，片刻至一村。入门，母先在室，询来此何为，则语殊含浑。次日，妪携生来，为之赘婿。居月余，生出不妇。母谓女曰：『尔可同俞姥先行。予则继至。』遂随姥乘飞车至一处。姥令女下车，日；『尔家不远，可自归。予从此别矣。』女欲致问，见车尘拂拂，如风飞行而去。细视其处，乃旧时扑蝶处也。乘月色至家，见母固在室中，怪而尽以情告，举家骇异。女始悟所遇者非母，深悔为妖所误，致使谤言沸腾，愧怒欲死。王夫妇忧闷无计，思嫁女以息众议，而门第人品如生者，亦殊寥寥。故某此来玉成其事。”生父母闻言大悦，为朱备聘仪还上谷。

    Explanation

    66

    Rong asked about the matter, and Zhu said, "After returning from your home, the Wang family girl lay ill and weak. Observing her condition, it seemed she was ill because of her husband. In spring, when she chased butterflies outside the village, she suddenly did not return. The family searched everywhere, but her trace was completely lost. After a month, she suddenly returned on her own. When asked, she said: 'When chasing butterflies, I met an old woman who introduced herself as surnamed Yu. She invited me to get on her carriage, which traveled swiftly, and in a moment, we arrived at a village. Upon entering, her mother was already in the room. When asked why I came here, her words were unusually vague. The next day, the old woman brought her husband and made him a son-in-law. After staying for over a month, her husband left without her. The mother told the daughter, "You may go ahead with the old woman Yu; I will follow later." So she followed the old woman on a flying carriage to a place. The old woman made her get off the carriage and said, 'Your home is not far; you may return on your own. I will part ways here.' The girl wanted to ask further, but saw the carriage dust rising and flying away like the wind. Upon closer inspection, it was the same place where she had chased butterflies before. Traveling by moonlight back home, she saw her mother still in the room. Finding this strange, she told them everything, and the whole family was shocked. The girl then realized that the person she had met was not her mother and deeply regretted being deceived by a demon. Causing slander to spread, she was filled with shame and anger, almost wanting to die. The Wang couple was distressed and had no plan, so they thought of marrying off their daughter to stop the gossip. But finding a match of his social status and character was very rare. So I came here to help make this arrangement.' The parents of the husband were very pleased upon hearing this and prepared the betrothal gifts for Zhu, sending them back to Shanggu."

    67

    朱归访王，王得朱作冰斧，喜甚，早备妆奁，送女至燕。朱助奁物丰厚，季氏洒扫青庐，为合卺。远近惊为奇异，无瓜葛者赤具仪来贺，争觏新人。夫妇华服登堂交拜，见者皆惊为神仙中人。此往彼来，门庭如市，五日始休。两家深感俞母，但不知为何许人。一日荣醉归，天色已晚，遇老妪，止宿于其家。屋仅三楹，中堂设榻款客，寒暄一二语，即入复室，闭扉寝。天微明，起促客云：“邻鸡喔喔，客宜早行，我家固无男子，惟母女二人，恐好事者造黑白也。”荣起，妪送诸门外，感其义，询姥姓氏，妪云：“老身姓胡，因与弱息僦居俞氏屋，人疑我亦其宗派，其实非也。身与令郎相识，烦寄一言：舟中好梦，洞里良缘，皆予母女之力。”荣唯唯，意未深解。行数武，始悟为俞姥，返顾人物惧杳，但见松柏夹路，乃同里俞氏旧茔。因知俞姥乃冢中之狐仙，于是修墙垣，栽树木，父子焚香虔祷，冀俞姥再来，而终渺然。

    Explanation

    67

    Zhu returned to visit Wang, who got Zhu to make an ice axe. Wang was very happy and prepared the dowry early, sending his daughter to Yan. Zhu generously contributed to the dowry, and the Ji family cleaned the green pavilion for the wedding ceremony. People far and near were surprised by the unusual event, and those without any connection came to congratulate, competing to see the new couple. The newlyweds dressed in fine clothes and ascended the hall to bow to each other, and those who saw them were all surprised that they looked like people from the celestial realm. People came and went, the courtyard was bustling like a market, and they rested after five days. The two families deeply appreciated Yu's mother, but they did not know who she was. One day, Rong returned drunk, and it was already late. He encountered an old woman and stayed overnight at her house. The house had only three rooms, and in the central hall, a bed was set up to entertain guests. They exchanged a few words of greeting, and then immediately entered the inner room and closed the door to sleep. It was just getting light when Rong got up and urged the guest to leave. The old woman said, "The neighbor's rooster is crowing, the guest should leave early. My family does not have any men, only the mother and daughter. I am afraid that those who like to gossip will spread rumors." Rong got up, and the old woman saw him off at the door. Rong felt grateful for her kindness and asked her surname. The old woman said, "I am surnamed Hu. Because of my weak daughter, we rented a house from the Yu family. People suspect that I am also part of their family, but that is not true. I have met your son and ask you to deliver a message: the good dreams on the boat and the good match in the cave were all due to the efforts of my mother and daughter." Rong nodded, but he did not fully understand. After walking a few steps, he realized it was Yu's old woman. Looking back, the people and the old woman were gone, but he saw pine and cypress trees lining the road, which was the old burial ground of the Yu family in the same village. He then knew that Yu's old woman was the fox spirit in the tomb. Therefore, he repaired the walls, planted trees, and with his father, burned incense and prayed devoutly, hoping that Yu's old woman would come again, but she never returned.

    68

    醉茶子曰：千里姻缘，红绳系足，未必真钟情也。钟情者相对咫尺而宛如山岳。不无室近人远之慨，恨无好事之俞姥，为之宛转撮合也。苟广其术以行之，则情天欲海中永无怨旷之苦矣。彼月下翁，正嫌其无用耳。
    云素秋
    　　云素秋，都中之优伶也，貌艺双绝，一时名重。以故富绅公侯家贵公子多踵其门，招饮者日数十处，秋率不往，而惑之者愈众。西贾王本通者，贩茶于都，颇称豪富，耳其名，以厚贽往。秋献一茶，略通一二语，匆匆入内。王大恚，不辞而返，誓与优人绝。有友人李月华，邀王饮，言及素秋，犹带余忿。李云：“卯金儿何太狂悖，请招来，吾为君泄此愤。”乃遣伻招之。素秋至，筵间媚态百端，王盛气都平，转有不能自持之势，求李代通殷勤。李固风流客，向秋宛转说辞，极言王诚心倾慕。

    Explanation

    68

    The Tea-Drunk One remarked: A thousand-mile marriage alliance, bound by a red thread, is not necessarily true love. Those who are truly in love are like mountains apart even when close by. There is no lack of the feeling that one's home is near but the person is far, and one regrets not having a kind matchmaker like Yu to mediate and bring them together. If one were to expand this method and apply it, there would be no more suffering of unfulfilled love in the realms of emotion and desire. The Moonlit Old Man, who is precisely annoyed by its uselessness.

    Yun Suhui
    Yun Suhui was a courtesan in the capital, whose beauty and artistry were both unparalleled, and she was famous for a time. Because of this, wealthy gentry, princes, and noble young men often came to her door, and she was invited to drink at dozens of places daily, but she usually did not go, and the number of those who were captivated by her grew. A Western merchant named Wang Wentong, who traded tea in the capital, was quite wealthy and prosperous. He heard of her name and went with a generous gift. Yun offered him a cup of tea and spoke a few words, and she hurriedly went inside. Wang was greatly angered and left without saying goodbye, swearing to break off relations with courtesans. A friend named Li Yuehua invited Wang to drink, and when they talked about Yun, he still harbored lingering resentment. Li said, "How arrogant and disrespectful is the gold child, let me invite her, and I will help you vent your anger." He then sent a messenger to invite her. When Yun arrived, she displayed a hundred charming gestures at the banquet. Wang's anger subsided, and he began to feel unable to control himself, and he asked Li to convey his sincerity. Li is a man of elegance and charm, and he spoke to Yun with gentle and persuasive words, speaking highly of Wang's sincere admiration.

    69

    秋大悦，邀二人至其寓，重开筵宴，水陆并陈，豹胎鲂腴，穷极奢侈，饮至三更始返。次日访李，思欲再往。李云：“素秋都中闻人，所交者皆侯王公卿，我辈何如人，而欲令彼青眼耶？如必欲强致之，非以利动不可。”王乃出白镪三百两送李，烦为介绍，然后随李往。李先怀二十金与秋，私约曰：“王某巨富，幸勿轻售。倘从我计，千金不难得也。”秋然之，乃邀王至秋寓。秋自内迎出，引至别业，雕梁画栋，松竹萧疏，院中嵌石成池，满引清水，朱鱼队队游泳其中。池上异花奇草，芳洌扑人。南望有竹篱一带，女萝蔓草掩映其间。

    Explanation

    69

    Autumn was favorable, and he invited the two of them to his residence, where he hosted a grand banquet again. Sea and land dishes were served, including rare delicacies such as "leopard fetus" and "mature carp," symbolizing extreme luxury. They drank until the third watch of the night before returning. The next day, he visited Li, thinking of going back again. Li said, "Su Qiu is a famous person in the capital, and those he associates with are all nobles, kings, high officials, and other high-ranking individuals. How are we, mere commoners, to gain his favor? If you insist on gaining his attention, you cannot do so without offering money." Wang then gave Li 300 taels of silver, asking him to introduce them. Afterward, they went with Li to Su Qiu's residence. Li first gave Su Qiu 20 taels of silver, privately agreeing, "Wang is extremely wealthy. Please do not sell him short. If you follow my advice, it is not difficult to earn a thousand gold." Su Qiu agreed, and then invited Wang to his residence. Su Qiu welcomed them from inside, leading them to a separate estate. The beams and pillars were intricately carved, and the courtyard was adorned with sparse pines and bamboos. In the yard, stones were embedded to form a pool, filled with clear water. Red fish swam in teams within it. Exotic flowers and strange grasses grew around the pool, their fragrance and freshness captivating. To the south, there was a stretch of bamboo fence, with moss and creeping plants intertwined among them.

    70

    有小瓦亭，红窗半启，碧槛弯环，袅袅茶烟，出自廊下。甫至亭前，一小僮揭帘延客入。片刻以玉碗献茶，瑙盘盛果，红菱白藕，堆积盈盈。三人谈笑方浓，忽有白某侯爷至。秋即随出，半晌不返。僮云：“主人今日恐不能归，二公盍明日再来惠顾？”二人自觉无趣，踟蹰庭前，与僮絮语。僮云：“素秋，徽人，有妹年二八，美丽无匹，名红亭，往往亦见客，然非与素秋莫逆不能觏也。”王闻僮言，便询李曾见之否。李云：“铁杵磨针，但须功到耳。”王会意，因俱出。王归，冥想素秋，而更欲一睹倾城，俾阔眼界。

    Explanation

    70

    There is a small tile pavilion with half-open red windows and green railings that curve around, and curling tea smoke emerges from the corridor. Just as they arrived at the pavilion, a young servant unfolded the curtain to invite the guests in. In a moment, tea was served in a jade bowl, and a tray of precious stone held fruits, red lotus seeds, and white lotus roots, piled up abundantly. The three were engaged in lively conversation and laughter when suddenly a nobleman named Bai arrived. Autumn followed him out, and he did not return for a long while. The servant said, "The host probably cannot return today. Why don't you two come again tomorrow?" The two felt a bit awkward and hesitated in the courtyard, chatting casually with the servant. The servant said, "Suqiu is from Anhui and has a sister who is sixteen years old, beautiful beyond compare, named Hongting. She often sees guests, but you can only meet her if you are close friends with Suqiu." Wang heard the servant's words and asked Li if he had ever seen her. Li replied, "A steel rod can be ground into a needle, as long as the effort is put in." Wang understood and they all left. Wang returned home, deeply thinking about Suqiu, and even more desiring to see the beautiful Hongting to broaden his horizons.

    71

    次日，邀李至，备厚贽，将约同访。忽僮自外入，则素秋来矣。王喜出望外，设筵痛饮。酒酣，秋歌《乔醋》一阕，心声手语，娓娓动人。王颠倒之态不可言状。秋故作媚容，蔼蔼与王语，且求其常至寓所。王唯唯，如奉丹诏。筵终，怀金偕往，秋引入卧室，拂榻展衾，香流床障，为王缓带解履。王骨软欲酥，相将俱寝，其狎昵不待言矣。由是在秋寓恒流连数十日不返，渐渐与秋亲近。乘间步入后院，见庭中有丽人立数花朵，粉晕朝霞，眉弯弱柳，洵属无双。见王来，笑问云：“公得非王官人？妾素秋之妹红亭也，向慕久矣。”嫣然启齿，落雁沉鱼。

    Explanation

    71

    The next day, Wang invited Li to come, prepared a generous gift, and planned to visit together. Suddenly, a servant entered from outside, and Su Qiu arrived. Wang was overjoyed, and they arranged a feast, drinking heavily. When the wine flowed freely, Su Qiu sang a verse of "Qiao Cu," her heartfelt words and gestures moving in a gentle and charming way. Wang was utterly captivated, his state beyond description. Su Qiu deliberately made a flattering appearance, speaking kindly to Wang and asking him to come to her residence often. Wang replied affirmatively, as if receiving an imperial edict. After the feast, Wang took gold with him and went together with Su Qiu, who led him to her bedroom. She smoothed the bed and spread the quilt, and the fragrance wafted from the bed curtain. She unfastened Wang's belt and removed his shoes. Wang's bones were so soft they almost melted, and they lay down together, their intimate and affectionate relationship needing no further explanation. Thus, Wang stayed at Su Qiu's residence for dozens of days without returning, gradually becoming close to her. One day, he took the opportunity to enter the back garden and saw a beautiful woman standing among several flowers, her face blushing like the morning glow, her brows arched like weak willows, truly unmatched. Seeing Wang, she smiled and asked, "Are you not Mr. Wang? I am Hong Ting, the younger sister of Su Qiu, and I have long admired you." She smiled charmingly, making even geese fall from the sky and fish sink to the bottom.

    72

    王疑为仙，魄荡魂摇，几乎颠踬。回首素秋在侧，邀王出。王目夺心移，语无伦次，终夜不能交睫。谓秋曰：“红娘红娘，你真个令我消魂，区区阿堵物断不靳也。”秋笑而不答。次日谋诸李，李云：“荡妇岂能贞洁自守，所以迟迟者，不过为孔方耳。君不吝，彼何难。”王言：“向以利动，彼竟不答，何也？”李云：“投其所好，自无不成。”王云：“安知其所好乎？”李言：“俟予细询素秋。”王乃赠李金，使谋厥事。李受金数日，来谓王曰：“事不济矣。彼自以才妓居，平时作画吟诗，好读《南华·秋水》。

    Explanation

    72

    Wang suspected he had met a fairy, his soul and spirit swaying, almost stumbling. Turning back, he saw Suqiu by his side, inviting him out. Wang's eyes were captivated, his heart moved, and his speech was incoherent; he could not close his eyes all night. He said to Suqiu, "Hongniang, Hongniang, truly you make me lose my soul. For such a small thing, I will not hesitate to give." Suqiu laughed without answering. The next day, Wang consulted Li, who said, "A loose woman cannot remain chaste on her own. The delay is only because of money. If you are not stingy, what difficulty is there?" Wang said, "I previously tried to motivate her with profit, but she still did not answer. Why is that?" Li replied, "Appeal to her interests, and it will surely succeed." Wang asked, "How can I know her interests?" Li said, "I will inquire in detail about Suqiu." Wang then gave Li gold to plot the matter. After receiving the gold for several days, Li came to Wang and said, "The matter did not succeed. She considers herself a talented courtesan, often painting and reciting poetry, and enjoys reading the Nanhua Qiu Shui."

    73

    凡与往来者，必亲与唱和。不然，金高北斗勿顾也。顾吾知君非长于此者。”王云：“且为奈何？”李云：“仆有一良友，乃燕赵名士刘生名杰。烦渠代成数诗，伪为君作，用以诱之，何如？”王从其教。红亭大悦，乃出己作求和。王茫然不解。红亭嘲曰：“吾固知其诈也。”王惭而退，使李持百金赠刘，将买《长门赋》以媚佳人。而刘固辞不受，云：“仆非贷诗者。前书俚句，怜王痴而闻红美也。如再索诗，非面对丽人不能得只字。”李归白王，王不得已，引刘先至，以情达素秋，云：“为蚕种桑，非为桑也；采桑饲蚕，非为蚕也。

    Explanation

    73

    In all interactions, one must personally engage in mutual composition. Otherwise, even if the gold is as high as the Big Dipper, it is not worth considering. But I know you are not skilled in this." Wang asked, "What should I do then?" Li replied, "I have a good friend, who is the famous scholar Liu Sheng from Yan and Zhao. I ask him to compose several poems on your behalf, pretending they are yours, to attract her. How about that?" Wang followed his advice. Hongting was very pleased and showed his own works for mutual composition. Wang was completely at a loss and did not understand. Hongting mocked, "I knew it was a trick all along." Wang felt ashamed and retreated, sending Li to deliver a hundred gold pieces to Liu, intending to buy the Changmen Fu to please the beautiful woman. However, Liu firmly refused to accept, saying, "I am not one who lends out poems. My previous crude verses were out of pity for Wang's foolishness and admiration for Hongting's beauty. If you seek more poems, I will not give you a single word unless I face the beautiful woman myself." Li returned to report to Wang, who had no choice but to bring Liu to Hongting first, explaining the situation to her, saying, "To raise silkworms, one plants mulberry trees, not for the mulberry itself; to feed the silkworms, one picks mulberry leaves, not for the silkworms themselves."

    74

    卿识之乎？”秋请闻其说，王曰：“予引刘来，非为刘也；遣刘饵红，非为红也。”秋笑应之，于是设宴中堂，履舄交错。刘赠红一绝云：“蛾眉凝翠遣谁描，目转秋波态更娇。如此风姿真绝世，却怜行踪等萍漂。”红得诗，反复沉吟，似有所思。已而泪下，亦题句：“飘泊谁怜堕溷花，一朝青眼幸君加。幽闺自此抛脂粉，孤影窗前对碧纱。”王睹其状，知红属意于刘，急引客去，自以珊瑚镜台一座，蜀锦绵茵赠红。而红尽屏却，杜门谢客，虽贵公子亦不接见也。

    Explanation

    74

    "Do you understand?" Qiu asked, requesting to hear the explanation. The king replied, "I brought Liu here, not for Liu's sake, but to use Liu to win Hong over, not for Hong's sake." Qiu laughed in response. Then, a banquet was set up in the central hall, with guests' shoes and socks scattered everywhere. Liu presented Hong with a poem: "Who can describe the greenish blackness of her eyebrows? Her eyes like autumn waves make her even more charming. Such grace is truly unparalleled in the world, yet I pity her wandering life like drifting duckweed." Upon receiving the poem, Hong recited it repeatedly, as if deep in thought. Soon, tears welled up in her eyes, and she wrote a response: "Who pities the fallen flower that drifts? One day you bestowed your favor upon me. From now on, I will abandon my makeup and life in seclusion, alone facing the green gauze before the window." The king, seeing her state, knew that Hong had feelings for Liu. He quickly escorted the guests away and presented Hong with a coral mirror stand and a silk mat made of Shu brocade. However, Hong rejected everything, closed her doors, and refused to see any guests, even the most distinguished nobles.

    75

    王意大窘，屡以金帛簪钏相赠，而红意不转，使婢言于王曰：“相见有日，幸勿相迫。”王遂寄宿秋寓，恒不返。适有家书至，云母病垂危，妻将临褥。王惊欲归，访李月华，告以故。李云：“焉有事巧如此者，是诳君也。”王怃然曰：“危言嚇我，我便插翼飞回耶？是信必众商伙所为，微君言，仆中其术矣。”遂决意不归，即以别筵招刘生，与素秋共饮，云：“家书未必是实，吾不信也。此友人赠我之别筵，恰好我辈痛饮。”刘索书览毕，叹曰：“母子夫妇，夭伦也。倘尊堂一有不测，岂产妇所能办理？不早归，何以准备？”王不听。众友共相劝诫，卒勿改，乃与王核算帐目，不与共厥事。

    Explanation

    75

    Wang Yi was in a difficult situation and repeatedly offered valuable gifts such as gold, silk, and jewelry to Hong Yi, but Hong Yi remained unmoved. She sent a maid to convey a message to Wang Yi: "We will meet again soon. Please do not press the matter." Wang Yi then decided to stay temporarily at a seasonal lodging and did not return home. Just then, a letter arrived from home, reporting that his mother was critically ill and his wife was about to give birth. Wang Yi was shocked and wanted to return home. He visited Li Yuehua and informed him of the situation. Li Yuehua said, "How could things be so coincidental? This must be a deception." Wang Yi sighed and said, "They are scaring me with dire warnings. Should I fly back instantly? This letter must be the work of many merchants. Without your words, I would have fallen into their trap." He then decided not to return and immediately invited Liu Sheng and Su Qiu for a farewell banquet. Liu Sheng read the letter and sighed, saying, "The mother, son, husband, and wife are all part of the natural order. If your mother has any mishap, how can a pregnant woman handle it? If you do not return early, how can you prepare?" The friends all tried to persuade and warn Wang Yi, but he finally did not change his mind. They then settled accounts with him and did not involve him in further matters.

    76

    王应得数千金，运至秋寓，将与秋小权子母作长久策。而秋挥金如土，殊无吝惜。刘生闻其事，诣王谏曰：“花街柳巷之中，岂有能货殖者，不过借子金银以遂其暴殄耳。如欲经营，请仍寻故友中之练达者。不然，怀金作归计，庶使倚闾人稍解愁颜也。我不知尊堂望子何如矣。”王笑曰：“君数劝我归，意欲独占花魁也。”刘不言，浩叹而去。不数月，王囊金罄尽，大为素秋白眼。然质衣典物，犹可作买花之费。无何，钱物萧然，遂为僮仆辈逐出。往寻故友，佥以其无行而不齿。

    Explanation

    76

    Wang should have received tens of thousands of gold coins, which were transported to Autumn's residence, with the intention of entrusting Autumn with a small amount of capital to make a long-term plan. However, Autumn spent money like dirt, showing no sign of frugality. Liu, upon hearing of this, went to Wang and advised him, saying, "In the red-light district, how can anyone truly engage in trade? They are merely borrowing your gold and silver to squander it. If you wish to manage your affairs, seek out experienced friends from your past. Otherwise, carry gold as a plan to return home, so that the person waiting at the door might be somewhat relieved of worry. I wonder what expectations your father has for you." Wang laughed and said, "You have repeatedly urged me to return, implying that you wish to monopolize the role of the top beauty." Liu remained silent, sighed deeply, and left. Within a few months, Wang had spent all his gold, and Autumn gave him a cold look. Despite this, he still pawned his clothes and sold his possessions to continue his extravagant lifestyle. Soon, he was left with nothing, and he was driven out by the servants. He went to seek help from his old friends, but they all held him in contempt due to his poor conduct.

    77

    无奈投李，李不纳，使老妪以钱一串授之云：“此非常策，后宜勿来。”王悻悻去，半日钱尽，丐食于市，人多不恤，且痛诋之。王饥肠雷鸣，败裳风刺，恐葬诸异地犬腹，乞食西行。途遇乡人，知母已病死，妻已产亡，家产尽为族人分散。王闻言痛绝，始恍然悟李之误己也。乃怀利刃入都，思杀李以泄愤。而朝城暮郭，终未相逢。丐食年余，筋皮殆尽。一日于古寺墙下，枕块斜卧，炷油破盏，烧食烟粪。举头见刘生来，惭愧欲遁，刘止之，羞极俯首。刘慰藉多辞，倾囊厚赠之。乃诣李曰：“君之景况，某所素知。今日得有余赢者，皆王某力。彼一贫如洗，君宜周之。

    Explanation

    77

    Wang, having no choice, sought help from Li, but Li refused. An old woman was then sent to give him a string of coins and said, "This is not an ordinary plan; you should not come again in the future." Wang left in anger, and in half a day, the coins were gone. He began to beg for food in the market, but most people did not care, and some even severely criticized him. His stomach growled like thunder, his tattered clothes were pierced by the wind, and he feared he would be buried in a foreign land by a dog's stomach. He begged for food and traveled westward, meeting a fellow villager on the road who told him that his mother had died of illness, his wife had died in childbirth, and his family property had been completely divided among his clan members. Wang was utterly devastated by the news and suddenly realized that Li had deceived him. He then carried a sharp blade into the capital, intending to kill Li to vent his anger. But from morning in the city to evening in the suburbs, they never met. He begged for food for more than a year, and his muscles and skin were almost completely worn away. One day, he leaned against the wall of an ancient temple and lay down with his head on a pile of earth. He lit a broken oil lamp and burned incense and feces for food. He raised his head and saw Liu approaching, feeling ashamed and wanting to flee. Liu stopped him, and he was so ashamed that he bowed his head. Liu comforted him with many words and gave him a generous gift from his own purse. Wang then went to Li and said, "Your situation, I have always known. Today, if I have any surplus, it is all due to Wang's efforts. He is as poor as a beggar; you should help him."

    78

    ”李勃然曰：“彼自无行，荡产败家，皆其自取，于我何尤？吾今稍享安乐者，乃数年经营之力，谁向彼手中强夺耶？况昔曾周之，彼自不返，吾安能填无底壑乎？”刘出叹曰：“不仁之人，至此已极，吾知必有惨报。”遂与李绝交。李嫉之。一日遇于隘巷，势不能避，遂与语。言次，李问：“尚忆红亭否？闻渠为君憔悴矣。”刘笑曰：“妓女焉知钟情，不过迎新送旧而已。”李庄容以信之，刘云：“如此奈何？”李云：“请往探之，当证仆言非妄。”盖欲趁此以败其行。

    Explanation

    78

    Li Bo ran said, "He himself is without conduct, wasting his property and ruining his family, all of which are his own doing. What fault do I have? I now enjoy a little peace and comfort, which is the result of several years of effort. Who is taking it away from him by force? Moreover, I once helped him, but he did not return it. How can I fill an abyss with no bottom?" Liu sighed and said, "An unrighteous person has reached this extreme. I know there will be a terrible retribution." So he broke off relations with Li. Li envied him. One day, they met in a narrow alley, and the situation did not allow avoidance, so they spoke to each other. During the conversation, Li asked, "Do you still remember Red Pavilion? I heard that she has been withered for you." Liu laughed and said, "How can a courtesan know true love? It is just welcoming new clients and seeing off old ones." Li calmly believed it. Liu said, "What should we do about this?" Li said, "Let us go investigate, to prove that my words are not false." He intended to take advantage of this opportunity to discredit Liu's behavior.

    79

    生云：“无论红亭自高声价，即素秋岂俯首寒酸哉！”李云：“素秋近日疮症极危，车马迹稀，后庭花不堪重问矣。”刘云：“若然我能医之。”遂偕往。素秋鞠躬出迎，呻吟颇苦，门庭冷落，大与昔殊。刘云：“闻卿贵恙沉重，某有小术可医。倘脱然无累时，何以酬我？”秋以身许，怡然求治。刘使解衣视之，见尻际肿溃，下连尾闾，出囊中药屑，白如粉，敷之，秋觉痛顿减，急赞药良。刘云：“若人精调药，顷刻可愈。”秋愁难得，刘云：“卿蓄之久矣，何便言难？”秋笑云：“昔日暴弈此物太多，宜今日无处觅也。”刘云：“此药干敷，忍耐数日，可保无恙。”如其言，果大瘳。秋欣然曰：“吾报刘有日矣。

    Explanation

    79

    The student said, "No matter how high Red Pavilion raises its reputation, how could Suqiu bow her head in poverty!" Li replied, "Suqiu's condition of sores is extremely dangerous recently, with fewer carriages and horses, the flowers in the rear courtyard are no longer worth asking about." Liu said, "If so, I can treat it." They went together. Suqiu bowed and came out to greet, groaning in great pain, with a cold and deserted door and hall, greatly different from before. Liu said, "I heard that your illness is very serious. I have a small skill to treat it. If you are free of illness and worries, how will you repay me?" Suqiu offered herself, happily seeking treatment. Liu made her undress to examine it, seeing swollen and ulcerated at the buttocks, down to the tailbone, he took out powdered medicine from his bag, white like powder, applied it. Suqiu felt the pain immediately decreased, quickly praising the medicine as good. Liu said, "If one is skilled in making medicine, it can be cured in a moment." Suqiu was worried about not being able to get it, Liu said, "You have been accumulating it for a long time, why do you say it is difficult?" Suqiu laughed and said, "In the past, I gambled with this thing too much, so today it is hard to find." Liu said, "This medicine is applied dry, endure for a few days, and it can ensure no harm." Following his advice, indeed, Suqiu greatly improved. She happily said, "I will repay Liu someday."

    80

    ”使僮延生至，云：“蒙君整顿后庭，敢忘报德？兹特扫径相待。”生笑云：“仆非穿窬之盗，区区必就事恳者，以子居为奇货，故不得不相迫耳。君既慨然，仆惟心领。”遂结为忘形交，而终不及乱。由是秋甚德生，往往招生饮。偶酌于斗室，有美人来窥，视之，红亭也。生云：“不嫌盂浪，盍来同饮？”女不辞，即入座，为生把盏，凝眸弄带，妩媚非常。生乘醉云：“红粉多情，玉容无主，得非命乎？”女微笑不言，席终而去。素秋谓生曰：“君视红亭何如？”生曰：“佳人难再。

    Explanation

    80

    The servant Yan brought the message to me, saying, "I have been favored by your lordship to tidy up the inner chambers. How could I forget the favor? I have specially swept the path to welcome you." I replied with a smile, "I am not a thief who climbs through walls. I am a simple person who is earnest in my requests, regarding your residence as a precious commodity, hence I could not help but press you. Your lordship has kindly agreed, and I shall take it to heart." Thus, we became close friends, disregarding superficial differences, but we never went beyond propriety. From then on, Autumn greatly appreciated me and often invited me to drink together. Once we drank together in a small room, and a beautiful woman came to look in. I saw that it was Hong Ting. I said, "If you do not mind my boldness, why not join us for a drink?" She did not refuse and immediately sat down, pouring me a cup, fixing her gaze, and playing with her sash, displaying extraordinary charm. I, in my drunkenness, said, "The red powder (referring to a woman) is full of emotion, and her jade-like face lacks a master. Is this not fate?" She smiled and remained silent. When the banquet ended, she left. Autumn said to me, "What do you think of Hong Ting?" I replied, "A beautiful woman is hard to come by again."

    81

    ”秋云：“昔王本通呼为红娘，君以为相若否？”生云：“虽莺莺不是过也。若果如红娘，予亦愿他从良。”秋笑曰：“君果见怜，红亭固有心久矣。第恐以鄙贱见弃，故不敢明言。”生闻言肃然起谢。明日，秋备妆奁甚丰，自送红亭至，生妻余氏颇贤，一见大悦。红亦善事嫡，嫡庶相安，从无含酸之意。李闻其事，持仪来贺，饮于生家。大醉，晚辞归，选次为人所杀，捕凶未获。群疑生，拘生去。生言词真挚，县令不能诬。秋出金为之营脱，始得免。李族人又疑李妻谋杀，联名控之。官拘李妻，试以毒刑，遂诬服。盖李妻王氏，素不贞，与家人左禄通。众牵左禄，拘左至，榜掠甚苦，卒无词。

    Explanation

    81

    Autumn said: "In the past, Wang Bensong called her Hongniang. Do you think she is similar to her?"
    The listener replied: "She is not inferior to Cui Yingying. If she is indeed like Hongniang, I would also wish for her to find a good match."
    Autumn laughed and said: "If you truly care, Red Pavilion has had feelings for a long time. I am just afraid of being rejected due to my low status, so I dare not speak openly."
    The listener, upon hearing this, stood up and bowed in gratitude.
    The next day, Autumn prepared a rich dowry and accompanied Red Pavilion to the listener's home. The listener's wife, Yu Shi, was very virtuous, and she was delighted to see Red Pavilion. Red Pavilion was also good at serving the legitimate wife, and there was no resentment or jealousy between them.
    Li heard the news and brought gifts to congratulate, and they held a drinking party at the listener's home. Li got very drunk and left late in the evening. The next day, he was killed, and the perpetrator was not caught. People began to suspect the listener, and he was detained. The listener's words were sincere, and the county magistrate could not accuse him falsely. Autumn paid money to secure his release, and he was finally freed.
    Li's family members then suspected Li's wife of murder and filed a joint complaint. The officials detained Li's wife and subjected her to torture, and she was falsely accused and confessed. It turned out that Li's wife, Wang Shi, was not chaste and had an affair with a family member named Zuo Lu. People brought Zuo Lu, detained him, and severely tortured him, but he did not confess.

    82

    而王氏诬指为左杀，官不能决，并系于狱。忽传有丐人死于途，身有遗书云：“仆非人类，忘骨肉而溺娼优，遂使母死妻亡而终不顾。所以然者，皆李月华之误我也。其初阳为亲昵而阴劫吾财，其后显与决裂而暗幸吾祸，下井投石，斯人已极，不啖其内，何以甘心！国门之外杀李者我，万勿累及他人，以增吾罪。短刀一柄为证。某月日王本通血书。”地保报官往验之，袖底利刃如霜，血犹殷红，其尸乃自毒死。官询李妻，始知王之颠末。乃重责左禄，并王氏而释之，案遂结。刘生闻之，往视其尸，果王也，乃与素秋并力厚葬于野。

    Explanation

    82

    Wang falsely accused someone of left-handed murder, and the officials could not decide the case, so they imprisoned multiple people together. Suddenly, it was reported that a beggar had died on the road, and there was a will on his body that said: "I am not human; I have forgotten my family and indulged in the company of prostitutes and actors, causing my mother to die and my wife to perish, and I ultimately did not care. The reason for this is all due to Li Yuehua's mistake with me. At first, he pretended to be close and friendly but secretly robbed my wealth; later, he openly broke ties and secretly rejoiced in my misfortune. To push someone into a well and throw stones, this person has reached the extreme. Without eating his insides, how can I be satisfied? The one who killed Li outside the city gate is me. Do not involve others, to increase my guilt. A short knife serves as proof. On the date of the month, Wang Ben Tong wrote this with his own blood." The local security officer reported to the authorities for verification. A sharp blade hidden in the sleeve like frost, the blood was still bright red, and the body was found to have died by poison. The officials questioned Li's wife and learned the details of Wang's actions. They then severely punished Zuo Lu and released Wang and his family, concluding the case. Liu heard about it, went to see the body, and found it was indeed Wang. He then with Su Qiu's help gave him a proper burial in the wilderness.

    83

    生以素秋齿长，劝其改业。秋从其言，出金指捐豫省典史，出仕颇有政声。生喜曰：“吾不图汝迁善至于如是！”素秋曰：“官场本似戏场，昔日敷粉搓脂，装作美女，则居心以媚人为念，故群相颠倒。今日升堂放衙，装作官长，则居心以爱民为念，故人皆悦服。彼名之曰官，而上负君国，下误民生，惟知敛财者，诚我辈之不如矣。”刘以为然。秋益自勉，不数年退归林下，家中楼阁连亘，牛马纷繁，良田数百顷，居然称世家焉。而犹乐善不倦，有贫寒者周之，客死者瘗之，立义阡于东郭。乡人感其德，勒碑以表彰之。而刘生终未获登仕版焉。
