    哥悦之，而屡招不至，于是携厚赀往，才拒不纳，哥兴尽而返。适学宪案临，翁谓哥曰：“凡人读书多年，文则辎軿青紫。今值考期，不登文场，恐贻人笑，奈何？”哥慨然无难色。补县郡试，投卷应考，阴使干仆以财贿当路，又以他人冒代，竟补博士弟子员，声名赫濯。世家求婚者踵相接。有同里王御史之女，美而慧，父钟爱之。见哥大悦，托戚党关说。翁不敢违，竟许其盟。居无何，王氏催聘甚迫，哥知之，颇自馁，乃谓翁曰：“翁使我服女之服，行女之行，则是女而己矣。今使服男之服，且使行男之行，谓为男可乎？”翁错愕，莫知所对。

    311

    The elder brother was pleased with him, but despite repeated invitations, he did not come. The elder brother then carried a large sum of money to go, but he only refused to accept it, and the elder brother returned when the enthusiasm was exhausted. Not long after, the provincial examination was held, and the father said to the elder brother, "Those who have studied for many years, writing will lead to high official rank and purple robes. Now that the exam period has arrived, failing to take the examination will surely bring ridicule, what should we do?" The elder brother was determined and showed no difficulty. He took the county and prefectural exams by submitting a written response, secretly sending a servant with bribes to influential officials, and had someone else impersonate him. He eventually became a student of the Imperial Academy, gaining great fame. Wealthy families seeking his hand in marriage came one after another. There was a daughter of a magistrate from the same district, who was beautiful and intelligent, and her father doted on her. Seeing the elder brother, she was very pleased, and asked relatives to intercede on her behalf. The father dared not disobey, and finally agreed to the match. Not long after, the Wang family urgently pressed for the marriage. The elder brother knew this and felt quite disheartened. He then said to his father, "Father, if you make me wear women's clothes and act like a woman, then I am just a woman. Now, if you make me wear men's clothes and act like a man, can I be considered a man?" The father was taken aback and did not know what to say.

    312

    哥郁郁无所为计，日惟优游散闷，于教坊东遇才官，握手与语。才红晕于颊，相将入寓，势不能避，不得已周旋尽欢。哥又以厚利诱其师，师令侍公子寝。才颦蹙曰：“仰蒙高厚，侬岂不知。但生年十七，犹完璧也。从此凿破天真，实非所愿。”哥笑应之。将就寝，才涕云：“儿本世家子，幼为匪人所诱，作此贱役，一朝玷辱，九族蒙羞。”言毕泣不能仰。哥大笑不禁，入帷代解袜履，童犹撑拒，咿哑之声达于户外。师闻而呵之曰：“儿勿如此。我日以珍羞之味、绮罗之衣供汝，非欲汝守贞节光门户而建贞童坊也。区区囊中物，皆诸郎博得来。

    Explanation

    312

    The protagonist, feeling depressed and without a plan, daily just relaxed and dispelled boredom. In the east of the entertainment district, he met a young, talented official, and they chatted. The official blushed on the cheeks, and they entered the room together. Unable to avoid, he reluctantly engaged in pleasurable activities. The protagonist bribed the official's teacher with substantial benefits, and the teacher instructed the official to serve the young man of noble status in bed. The official frowned and said, "Grateful for the great kindness, I know very well. But at seventeen, I am still a virgin. From now on, breaking the innocence, it is not what I desire." The protagonist laughed and responded. As they were about to go to bed, the official wept and said, "I am originally from a noble family. I was lured by wicked people at a young age and became this lowly performer. Once I am defiled, the entire family will be disgraced." After finishing, he could not look up. The protagonist couldn't help but laugh, entered the room to help take off socks and shoes, and the official still resisted, with the sounds of struggle reaching outside the room. The teacher heard and reprimanded, "Don't behave like this. I daily provide you with delicious food and fine clothes, not to make you guard your chastity and bring glory to the family by building a monument to your purity. These small things in my bag are all obtained by the young men."

    313

    汝坐食于此，于心安乎？如再抗违，鞭楚从事矣。”才见逼，默不敢语，听其所为。哥抱于怀，曰：“儿勿悲，吾为尔择一佳妇，鬻尔出籍，而愿之乎？”才不语。再问之，才云：“但乞稍全体面，于愿已足，尚敢有过望乎？”及扪其体，肤腻如脂，戏握其阳曰：“好男儿！身有此物，何唏嘘学小女子耶？”才战慄惊悸，心戚戚然，默念鲁阳之戈，不知何以锋利。屏息既久，而按兵不动，心益惊疑，汗流浃背。伪睡以待。哥乘其睡，潜与同枕。片刻，哥亦朦胧。才私扪其阴，则坦坦平途，双峰对峙，大骇。哥笑曰：“可放胆矣。

    Explanation

    313

    You sit here eating without contributing, is your heart at ease? If you continue to resist, I will have you whipped and punished!" Seeing the pressure, he remained silent, unable to speak, and listened to what would happen. The brother embraced him, saying, "Do not be sad, my child. I will find you a good wife and release you from your register; would you like that?" He remained silent. When asked again, he said, "I only ask for a bit of dignity, and that would be enough; how could I dare to have any other expectations?" When he touched his body, his skin was smooth as fat, and the brother playfully held his penis, saying, "Good man! You have this thing, why are you sighing like a little girl?" He trembled with fear and anxiety, his heart filled with unease, and silently wondered at the sharpness of the sword of Lu Yang, not knowing how it could be so sharp. Holding his breath for a long time, he remained motionless, his heart filled with more doubt and fear, his sweat pouring down his back. He feigned sleep to wait. The brother, taking advantage of his sleep, secretly shared a pillow with him. After a moment, the brother also fell into a drowsy state. He privately touched his private parts and found a smooth path and two peaks standing opposite each other, greatly shocked. The brother laughed and said, "Now you can relax."

    314

    ”才亦笑曰：“公子丰于财，何致穷无此物耶？”哥曰：“无此尚破尔胆，若有，断尔魂矣。”哥以实告。才甚喜，倒戈相向，大肆发挥。哥畅然满志，谓才曰：“今而后切宜秘密，予不负尔。”由是往来无间。越两月余，才请其鬻身出籍，愿常侍左右。哥从其请，商诸其师。师素以此儿执拗，屡欲驱逐，但怜其艺，未忍遽绝。闻公子言，如脱重累，不争其直，使从公子去。至杜家，随诸僮服役，慧过群仆，遂宠擅专房。杜翁亦喜，饰以女子妆，为爱哥纳谙小星，因蓄发箝耳，居于内室。人佥谓公子喜南风，而不知阿翁选东床也。

    Explanation

    314

    "才亦笑曰："公子丰于财，何致穷无此物耶？"哥曰："无此尚破尔胆，若有，断尔魂矣。"哥以实告。才甚喜，倒戈相向，大肆发挥。哥畅然满志，谓才曰："今而后切宜秘密，予不负尔。"由是往来无间。越两月余，才请其鬻身出籍，愿常侍左右。哥从其请，商诸其师。师素以此儿执拗，屡欲驱逐，但怜其艺，未忍遽绝。闻公子言，如脱重累，不争其直，使从公子去。至杜家，随诸僮服役，慧过群仆，遂宠擅专房。杜翁亦喜，饰以女子妆，为爱哥纳谙小星，因蓄发箝耳，居于内室。人佥谓公子喜南风，而不知阿翁选东床也。"

    Translation:
    "才 also laughed and said, 'The young nobleman is wealthy, so why would he lack this item?' The elder brother replied, 'Even without it, it would still scare you, but if he had it, it would break your spirit.' The elder brother revealed the truth. 才 was very pleased, turned his weapons against them, and made a grand display. The elder brother was content and said to 才, 'From now on, we must keep this a secret, and I will not betray you.' Thus, they interacted without any barriers. After more than two months, 才 requested to sell himself out of his current status, wishing to always serve by the young nobleman's side. The elder brother agreed and consulted with his teacher. The teacher had always found this child stubborn and wanted to drive him away, but he pitied his talent and could not bear to reject him immediately. Hearing the young nobleman's words, the teacher felt as if a heavy burden had been lifted, and he did not argue about the price, allowing 才 to go with the young nobleman. Upon arriving at the 杜家, 才 followed the servants in their duties, being more intelligent than all the other servants, and soon became favored, occupying the entire room. The 杜翁 was also pleased, dressing 才 in a woman's attire, and taking him in as a concubine for his beloved son. Because of this, 才 grew his hair and bound his ears, living in the inner chamber. People all said that the young nobleman favored southern culture, but they did not know that the old man had chosen the right person for his daughter."

    315

    王御史闻婿纳妾，大愤，促其速娶。翁不得已，备青庐为哥完婚。哥谓才曰：“今而知优孟衣冠，不可以久。倘王氏女来，何以待之？”才曰：“是不难。迎娶之事，卿自任之，床第之私，予代之可也。”公子闻之，大悦，从其计。王女入门，虽夭夭少好，而悍妒非常，合卺后见夫宿妾房，大不平，犹拘为新妇，不肯遽发。因循半载，从无当夕事。归宁时，涕泣不食，忿欲觅死。父母询其故，不答。访诸婢媪，始知其故。御史大怒，登其门诮让之。翁惧，迎妇归。妇迫令出妾。

    Explanation

    315

    The magistrate heard that his son-in-law had taken a concubine and was greatly angered, urging him to marry quickly. Reluctantly, he prepared a green tent to finalize the marriage. The young master said to his concubine, "Now I understand that imitating others' roles cannot last long. If the W family's daughter comes, how shall we treat her?" The concubine replied, "This is not difficult. The matter of marriage, you take care of it. Private matters of the bedroom, I can take care of them." The young master heard this, greatly pleased, and followed his plan. The W family's daughter entered the household, though she was young and pretty, she was extremely jealous and quarrelsome. After the wedding night, seeing her husband sleeping in the concubine's room, she was greatly upset, still treating the concubine as a new wife, unwilling to dismiss her immediately. For half a year, there was no night when the husband spent with her. When she returned home to her parents, she was crying and not eating, filled with anger and wanting to die. Her parents asked her the reason, but she did not answer. They asked the maidservants, and finally knew the reason. The magistrate was greatly angry and went to his door to scold him. The young master was frightened and brought the wife back. The wife forced him to dismiss the concubine.

    316

    公子向妇婉言知悔，妇怒曰：“妾王御史之女，三从四德，无不备晓，有何失德于汝，而竟不以伉俪视之？”公子笑而不答。女红晕于颊，怒容可掬。公子曰：“得毋谓我偏爱妾耶？今夜即来伴新娘子。”妇益羞怒，语侵翁姑，翁姑以好言慰之，其忿始解。随即唤妾至而痛挞之。公子无如何，与之约曰：“今夜遣妾来，以明我不二。”至晚，才至妇室，妇怒逐之。才抱衾强卧，妇怒欲觅杖，其仆媪止之，私谓曰：“娘子勿尔，倘遂妾去而公子不来，是为渊驱鱼，为丛驱兽也。公子使妾来，是激娘子之怒，逐之，中其计矣。不如纳之。”妇以为然。妾曲意承迎，妇终不悦，曰：“郎不自来，而遣汝塞责，是奚落我。再作态，当以赤铁烙尔口。

    Explanation

    316

    The young master spoke gently to his wife, expressing regret. The wife became angry and said, "I am the daughter of a former censor. The Three Obediences and Four Virtues, I understand them all. What wrong have I done to you that you no longer treat me as your wife?" The young master smiled and did not answer. The wife's anger was evident. The young master said, "Is it not because you think I favor my concubine? Tonight I will come to accompany the new bride." The wife became even more ashamed and angry, speaking disrespectfully to her in-laws. Her in-laws tried to comfort her, and her anger subsided. The young master then called the concubine and severely beat her. The young master had no choice but to promise, "Tonight I will come, to prove that I do not favor her." That evening, the young master arrived at the wife's room, but the wife angrily chased him away. The concubine clung to the bedding and tried to sleep, but the wife was furious and wanted to find a stick to beat her. The servants stopped her and privately said, "Madam, please do not do that. If you drive the concubine away and the young master does not come, you will be driving fish into deep water and beasts into thickets. The young master sent the concubine to provoke your anger and now you have chased her away, which is exactly what he wanted. It is better to let her stay." The wife agreed. The concubine tried to please her, but the wife was still unhappy, saying, "If the young master does not come himself, but sends you to make up for it, it is mocking me. If you act that way again, I will brand your mouth with red-hot iron."

    317

    ”妾曰：“今夜自有郎来，使娘子尽欢。”妇益怒，又欲操杖，为媪劝解始免，遂悒悒卧寝。妾潜就妇低声曰：“娘子勿怒，召和而缓至，何伤也？郎君寂寂寞寞，清夜难堪，不知垂多少涎耳。”女以为谤己，气冲发指，赤身起扑之。才乘势颠倒。妇猝不及防，昂然直入矣。咋其口，笑曰：“郎来也，郎来也，娘子勿怒矣。”稠云密雨，曲尽绸缪。妇转怒为喜，骇而问故，才曰：“公子不敢见娘子者，缘公子亦娘子也。妾，公子之内嬖，实公子之丈夫也。以予一身周旋于两间，应无不可。

    Explanation

    317

    The maid said, "Tonight, there will be a man coming to make the lady happy." The woman became even more angry and wanted to take a stick, but was persuaded by an elderly woman to stop, and then lay down in a state of deep sadness. The maid approached the woman quietly and spoke softly, "Lady, do not be angry. The man is coming slowly, what harm is there? The young master is lonely and finds the night unbearable, how many droolings (of desire) does he not have?" The woman thought the maid was insulting her, became very angry, and attacked her. The maid took advantage of the situation and had sex with her. The woman was caught off guard and was penetrated. The maid bit her mouth and laughed, "The man has come, the man has come, lady, do not be angry anymore." They engaged in a passionate and intimate act. The woman's anger turned to joy, and she was startled and asked why. The maid said, "The young master is reluctant to see the lady because he is also a woman. I am the young master's favored concubine, and I am actually the young master's husband. With me navigating between the two, there should be no problem."

    318

    然公子丑如鬼，而娘子艳如仙，辟如择羊，岂效甄宇，娘子岂不知之？”妇喜甚，抚才曰：“好男儿貌胜莲花，与卿愿为并蒂。”才恚曰：“初会面即便挞楚，令侬难堪。至今棒疮尚痛，明日请退避三舍。”妇力白其悔，痛自挝。才遽止之。妇乃矢誓旦旦，愿期白首。才笑相许。月余，情如胶漆。哥不耐，至妇所唤妾，妾不应。妇曰：“公子光明磊落，不偏厚于妻妾，使我辈二女同居，共不得志，以表公子无私。今何作此态耶？”公子词穷，自思以美果让人，作事颠倒，悔已无及。从此饮食不甘。父母为之调停，使妻妾各以半月伴寝，遂遣妾随哥去。至半月，当入妻室，哥畏葸不往。

    Explanation

    318

    The man was as ugly as a ghost, while the woman was as beautiful as a fairy. It was like choosing a sheep—how could one follow the example of Zhen Yu? The woman, too, should know this well!" The woman was very pleased and patted Cai, saying, "A good-looking man whose appearance surpasses that of a lotus flower, I wish to be like twin blossoms on the same stem with you." Cai angrily said, "Right after our first meeting, you beat me up, making me feel embarrassed. The bruises from the beating are still painful. I ask you to keep your distance from me tomorrow." The woman strongly expressed her regret and painfully struck herself. Cai quickly stopped her. The woman then swore earnestly, wishing to be together until old age. Cai smiled and agreed. After a month, their feelings were as inseparable as glue and lacquer. The elder brother became impatient and went to the woman's place to call the concubine, but the concubine did not respond. The woman said, "The gentleman is upright and honorable, does not favor one over the other. Making us two women live together, both of us unable to achieve our desires, to show the gentleman's impartiality. Why are you acting this way now?" The gentleman was at a loss for words. He thought to himself that giving up the beautiful fruit was a mistake, a foolish act, and now it was too late to regret it. From then on, he lost his appetite. The parents mediated and arranged for the wife and concubine to take turns sleeping with him for half a month each. Then they sent the concubine away with the elder brother. When it was time for him to sleep with the wife, the elder brother was afraid and did not go.

    319

    父母苦劝之，不得已从其命。妻知其伪，固诱之淫，哥衣不解带。将近十余日，妇不耐，曰：“闻郎君身无长物。信然乎？”公子不答，及期始伴妾去，严禁妾不与妇通，会面更无日也。妇恚甚，招公子来，留于室，夜谓公子曰：“尔之伎俩吾已勘破，不实言，吾将遍延亲党来，以扬其丑。如明言之，有妾同享，外人勿得知也。”公子伪睡不答。妇怒沈半晌，出其不意，捋裤而手探之，则蓬蓬须发，满口于思，大笑曰：“今日尚惺惺假作态乎？明日即遍传播。

    319

    Final Translation

    1. 父母苦劝之，不得已从其命。

      • The parents urged him persistently, but he reluctantly followed their command.

    2. 妻知其伪，固诱之淫，哥衣不解带。

      • The wife knew it was false, so she deliberately tempted him to indulge in sexual relations, and he remained dressed without changing clothes.

    3. 将近十余日，妇不耐，曰：“闻郎君身无长物。信然乎？”

      • For nearly ten days, the wife became impatient and said, "I've heard that the young man has no valuables. Is that true?"

    4. 公子不答，及期始伴妾去，严禁妾不与妇通，会面更无日也。

      • The young man did not answer. Only at the appointed time did he accompany his concubine away, strictly prohibiting her from having relations with the wife, and there would be no more meetings.

    5. 妇恚甚，招公子来，留于室，夜谓公子曰：“尔之伎俩吾已勘破，不实言，吾将遍延亲党来，以扬其丑。如明言之，有妾同享，外人勿得知也。”

      • The wife was very angry, summoned the young man, and kept him in the room. At night, she said to him, "I have already seen through your tricks. If you don't tell the truth, I will invite all my relatives and friends to expose your shame. If you admit it, we can share the concubine, and no one else will know."

    6. 公子伪睡不答。

      • The young man pretended to sleep and did not respond.

    7. 妇怒沈半晌，出其不意，捋裤而手探之，则蓬蓬须发，满口于思，大笑曰：“今日尚惺惺假作态乎？明日即遍传播。

      • The wife remained angry for a long time. Unexpectedly, she pulled down her pants and touched him. His beard and hair were full, and he was filled with thoughts. She laughed loudly and said, "Are you still pretending to be awake and acting now? Tomorrow, I will spread it everywhere."

    END

    320

    ”公子泣曰：“予非故为妖异，父母老而无子，以我代之，作此不明之事，幸人不之知。既已如此，背之非孝。卿亦人子也，若伤二老之心，则不孝之罪，不独我一人也。且予已有娠矣，尚赧颜争夕乎？”妇曰：“公子何以置我？”哥再拜曰：“予分娩时佯为患病，烦卿坐褥一月，伪为生子。果男也，共抚之，两全其美，仁孝在其中矣。此后阳台请君独据，予将雌伏矣。”言毕泪下如雨，妇感而许之。又月余，公子举一男，潜闭内斋，屏绝诸务，延医诊视，医云：“气亏阳痿，日进参附。”公子弃诸药，市产后之剂服之，得无恙。妇蓬首垢面坐帷中，锦衾蔽体，居然生子。公子遣妾来侍妇寝，又雇乳姆哺儿，母子均得舒泰。

    320

    The young master wept and said, "I did not intentionally create something strange. My parents are old and have no children, so I replaced them, doing such an ambiguous thing, fortunately without people knowing. Having done it, turning away from it is not filial. You are also someone's son; if you hurt the hearts of the two elders, then the sin of filial impiety is not just mine alone. Moreover, I am already pregnant; should I still blush to compete for the night?" The woman said, "How can the young master leave me behind?" He bowed twice and said, "I pretended to be ill during childbirth, troubling you to sit on the birth couch for a month, pretending to give birth to a son. If it is a boy, we will raise him together, achieving the best of both worlds, with benevolence and filial piety in it. After this, the 'Yangtai' (a euphemism for the bedroom) will be exclusively yours, and I will retreat and be submissive." After speaking, tears fell like rain, and the woman was moved and agreed. Another month later, the young master gave birth to a boy, secretly closing in the inner chamber, setting aside all other matters, inviting a doctor for diagnosis. The doctor said, "There is a deficiency in qi and yang, take ginseng and fuzi daily." The young master discarded the other medicines and took postpartum remedies, recovering without harm. The woman sat in the curtain with unkempt hair and a dirty face, covered with a brocade quilt, indeed giving birth to a son. The young master sent a concubine to attend the woman at night and hired a wet nurse to feed the child, and both mother and child were comfortable.

    321

    于是贺者盈门，王母亦喜，备仪物甚丰。视其儿方面大耳，卧锦绣襁褓中，洵是福相。越三旬，夫妇并起，登堂拜其父母。翁欣然有含饴之乐，视儿如掌上珠，命名曰“福生”。福生者，父所生也，而他人不解其意。公子从此遣妾常在女所，女嬖之，稍离之勿悦也。妾亦怜妇美，两情欢恰，与公子绝迹矣。公子抑郁成疾，红潮为之不来。医揣公子体虚，率进兴阳固肾之剂，愈服愈危。又有凝公子为疡症入内者，更以攻毒之品投之，于是病致不起。公子泣谓父母曰：“鱼目混珠，终非长策，今幸有子，宜善遇之。王女不可恃也。

    Explanation

    321

    Those who came to congratulate filled the door, and the princess was also pleased, preparing abundant ceremonial gifts. She saw the child with a square face and large ears, lying in a luxurious swaddling cloth, indeed a sign of good fortune. After three weeks, the husband and wife both stood up, went to the hall to pay respects to their parents. The grandfather was delighted with the joy of holding sugar in his mouth, treating the child as a pearl on his palm, and named him "Fusheng." Fusheng, born by his father, was not understood by others. The prince then sent his concubine to stay with the princess, who favored her, and she was unhappy if she left. The concubine also pitied the beauty of the wife, and they were both happy, keeping their relationship hidden from the prince. The prince became depressed and fell ill, with his complexion turning pale. The doctor, sensing the prince's weak condition, prescribed tonics to strengthen his yang and kidneys, but the more he took, the more dangerous it became. Some believed the prince had an internal ulcer and further treated him with toxic substances, leading to his inability to get up. The prince wept and said to his parents, "Mixing fish eyes with pearls is not a long-term strategy. Now that I have a son, you should treat him well. The princess cannot be relied upon."

    322

    儿为二老者至矣，今寿不永，可为奈何？彼亏我之短而有负心，予必夺其魄。儿死即遣之嫁，万勿久留，恐出意外之丑。彼才优者，恨未能生啖其肉耳。”言毕而逝。父母痛如割心，以丈夫礼葬于祖茔之侧。妇不为之缞绖。翁劝之，妇曰：“我与彼妯娌也。妯娌无是服制，不敢违。”翁辞窘，不敢强。妇遂炫装敷粉，日与妾戏。妾亦不安于女子装，一仍其旧。翁忿挞之。妇与才并怒，共起挝翁，且曰：“我尔家之婿也。尔女既亡，我有嫡妻。如不相容，我便携眷去，不受尔羁绊也。”翁怨忿填胸，恐其败露，转善言安抚之。才每私出夜游，翁已无奈，渐渐白日亦然，继为狭邪游。

    Explanation

    322

    My son has come for the two elders. Now his life is not long, what can be done about it? He has wronged me by his shortcoming and has a disloyal heart. I will surely take his soul. If my son dies, I will send her away to marry, do not keep her for long, fear that an unexpected disgrace will occur. That person of superior talent, I regret that I cannot eat his flesh alive." After speaking, he died. The parents were as pained as if their hearts were being cut, and they buried him with the rites of a man beside the family tomb. The wife did not wear mourning clothes. The old man persuaded her, and she said, "I am his sister-in-law. There is no mourning ritual for sisters-in-law, I dare not disobey." The old man was at a loss for words and dared not force. The wife then put on makeup and cosmetics, she played with the concubine every day. The concubine also did not feel comfortable in the attire of a woman, she continued to dress as before. The old man angrily beat her. The wife and the man were both angry, they both beat the old man together, and said, "I am your family's son-in-law. Your daughter is already dead. I have a legitimate wife. If you do not accept me, I will take my family away, and will not be bound by you anymore." The old man's resentment and anger filled his chest, fear that it will be exposed, he then spoke kindly to soothe her. The man often went out privately at night, the old man was already helpless, gradually, even during the day, he did so, and then engaged in immoral activities.

    323

    无赖子每出入其门，翁恨欲死，乃对灯堕泪。忽举首，公子立灯下。翁大骇。公子曰：“父勿惊惧，此来正为父与儿也。明日唤乳妇抱福生于母所，朝夕善守之，防彼毒手，儿自有术以报之。儿一生暗昧不明，致遭奇辱，因生平暴殄天物，故夭寿命。然所恃者孝行不亏耳。幸得请于帝矣，许斩此淫佞。”言毕不见。翁疑为梦，然闻此言，姑含忍之。一夕，家人呼有盗，于是群起操戈相逐，见墙阴一人攀壁方登，有健仆猛刺之，颓然而倒，缚视乃其邻子，呼云：“予本不愿为此，尔家才儿约我来，许我瓜分。盗魁在汝家，与我何尤？”翁闻言，令解其缚而释之。

    Explanation

    323

    Every time the troublemaker entered or left the door, the old man was so angry he nearly died, and he would cry by the lamp. Suddenly, he raised his head and saw the young master standing by the lamp. The old man was greatly startled. The young master said, "Father, do not be afraid. I have come specifically for you and your son. Tomorrow, call the wet nurse to take your child to your wife's room, and watch over him day and night to protect him from harm. I will have my own way to repay you. My life has been obscure and unclear, leading to strange humiliations because of my wasteful and reckless behavior, which shortened my lifespan. However, what I rely on is my filial piety. Fortunately, I have asked the emperor for permission, and he has granted me the authority to behead this lewd and deceitful person." After speaking, the young master disappeared. The old man doubted whether it was a dream, but upon hearing these words, he bore it with patience. One night, the family called out that there was a thief, and everyone armed themselves and chased after him. They saw a man climbing the wall to enter, and a strong servant thrust at him, causing him to fall down and collapse. When they tied him up and looked, it turned out to be his neighbor's son. The son cried, "I did not originally want to do this. Your clever son invited me to come and promised me a share. The leader of the thieves is in your house. What do I have to do with you?" Upon hearing this, the old man ordered that the son be untied and released.

    324

    次日，以厚资遣才去。妇见才去，假归宁，意欲与才野合耳。而王御史家法森严，内外隔绝，妇与才会面殊难。计无所出，乃使其心腹婢托买丝于门外，得遇才，告以秘计。才归，夜呼其党逾重垣入，思劫女掠财。方至前庭，见爱哥立廊左，才大惊，猛斫之，遂倒。王仆群至，见有持刀者，众先擒之，乃才官也。于是众盗奔窜，王家多健仆，争缚之，无一脱者。烛视所斫之尸，乃即妇也。盖妇阴嗾才行劫，乘间与共遁。是夜闻声即出，不期竟为才所杀也。王御史始知其女非良，痛恨之，送盗于有司，尽置于法。后福生长成，联捷登第，奉其祖以终老焉。

    Explanation

    324

    The next day, with a generous gift, Cai was sent away. The woman, seeing Cai leave, pretended to return to her parents' home, intending to meet him secretly. However, the household of Wáng Yùshǐ was known for its strict discipline, with a clear separation between the inner and outer parts of the house, making it extremely difficult for the woman and Cai to meet. With no other options, she sent her trusted maid out to pretend to buy silk, where she met Cai and told him her secret plan. Cai returned that night, calling his accomplices to jump over the high wall and enter, planning to kidnap the woman and steal her wealth. Just as they reached the front courtyard, they saw Aigē standing on the left side of the corridor. Cai was greatly surprised and suddenly attacked her, causing her to fall. The master's servants arrived in a group and, seeing someone with a knife, immediately captured him. It turned out to be Cai. The bandits then fled in panic, but the household had many strong servants who struggled to bind them, and not one escaped. By candlelight, they examined the body of the person who had been struck down and found that it was the woman. It turned out that the woman had secretly incited Cai to commit the robbery, taking the opportunity to escape together. That night, when she heard the noise, she came out immediately, only to be unexpectedly killed by Cai. Wáng Yùshǐ only then realized that his daughter was not virtuous, and he feared and hated her deeply. He sent the bandits to the authorities, and they were all punished according to the law. Later, Fu grew up and passed the imperial examinations consecutively, achieving official rank, and honored his ancestors, living out his days in peace.

    325

    醉茶子曰：掩耳盗铃，未有不暴露者。况为之易装娶室，岂假者竟能真哉？自立于必败之地而欲求全，其可得乎？杜翁可谓愚矣。若爱哥始则放荡，后则知悔，无如阴阳反背，事终不济，天所以始弃之而终怜之矣。夫事不可以矫揉作也。尝见柏舟操节，暗偷韩掾之香；柳陌游春，浪掷卫车之果。或明或暗，终被人知者，丑之所在，弥缝难也。乡有翁媪，老而无子，三女皆待字。共议守贞养亲，而幼女终默默，二姊咸唾之。父母聘其幼女。后父母终，家道寒苦，其婿出赀殓殡之，众叹其幼女贤也。其二姊守贞，于亲无补，惟闭门茹苦，虽族中侄辈俱莫睹其面。后皆病癫狂。长者先死，次者病十余年方死。其平昔癫语甚丑，皆其心事。噫，矫情有何益哉！

    Explanation

    325

    The Drunken Tea Master said: Plugging one's ears while stealing a bell, there has never been one that was not exposed. How much more so, then, to disguise oneself and marry into a family—could the fake become real? To set oneself up in a place of certain failure and yet seek to preserve oneself, how could it be possible? The Old Man Du can be called foolish. If Brother Ai started off wayward, later he came to regret it, but alas, the yin and yang were reversed, and the matter ultimately failed. Heaven abandoned him at first but later pitied him. Matters cannot be artificially forced. I have seen those who maintained their chastity like a boat on the river, secretly stealing the fragrance of Han's official; those who strolled in the willow-lined paths in spring, recklessly throwing away the fruits of Wei's carriage. Whether openly or secretly, they were all eventually known, for where ugliness lies, it is hard to patch up. In the village, there was an old man and woman who had no children, and their three daughters were all unmarried. They agreed to maintain chastity to support their parents, but the youngest daughter remained silent, and the two elder sisters both spat at her. The parents arranged a marriage for the youngest daughter. After the parents died, the family fell into poverty, and her husband spent money to bury them. Everyone praised the youngest daughter as virtuous. The two elder sisters maintained chastity, but it did not help their parents; they only shut themselves away and endured hardship, and even the nephews of the family never saw their faces. Later, they both became mad. The elder sister died first, and the second one fell ill for over ten years before dying. Their former mad words were very ugly, all expressions of their inner thoughts. Ah, what good is pretense!

    326

    狐师
    　　宫生斯和，浙人。父贸易于津，娶北村赵氏女，遂家于是焉。生从垫师读书于萧寺，因冒籍赴通州应考，院试被黜，意颇不平。与二三窗友自州归，离村半里许，有演外台戏者，众相聚观。生见一女姿容绝代，拥挤众中，粉污交下。未几至台前众健儿丛中，摩肩相立，众俱若未之睹。生心怪之，归告诸友，佥云未见。至晚，生归寝室，挑灯独坐。举首则女子立几前，生拒之，女不去。生曰：“此塾也，非女郎所宜到处，若为人见，无颜甚矣。君纵不畏人言，吾欲避嫌瓜李。请速行，友且至矣。”女曰：“予狐仙也，予之形唯君独见之。

    326

    Fox Teacher
    Sheng, a man from Zhejiang, had a father who traded in Tianjin. He married a woman from North Village, Zhao family, and settled there. Sheng studied under a tutor at Xiang Temple and took the exam in Tongzhou under a false registration, but he failed the provincial exam and felt quite aggrieved. With a few classmates, he returned from the prefecture. On the way, there was a performance of an outdoor play, and the crowd gathered to watch. Sheng saw a woman of extraordinary beauty in the midst of the crowd, where dust and dirt were mixed together. Soon, she reached the front of the stage among the strong men, shouldered each other standing, but everyone seemed not to notice her. Sheng found it strange and told his friends upon returning, but all said they did not see her. By evening, Sheng returned to his room, lit a lamp, and sat alone. Lifting his head, he saw a woman standing before the table. Sheng tried to push her away, but she did not leave. He said, "This is a study place, not suitable for a lady to be here. If anyone sees her, it would be very shameful. Even if you do not fear what others might say, I want to avoid suspicion. Please go quickly, my friends are coming." The woman replied, "I am a fox spirit. My form is only visible to you."

    327

    ”生曰：“市上遨游，非子也耶？毋托狐仙以诳我。”女曰：“君勿多疑。君试思市上热闹之场，塾中幽僻之地，何物女儿，能独往独来，而使众耳目如聋聩者？”生恍然曰：“君诚仙人，此来何意？”女曰：“实告君，君前生为山右富室，每于猎人手买禽兽救生。彼时予未成道，误为猎者所获，幸君救免，兹来特报此德。”生曰：“此尔辈之故智耳，非云有缘，即云报德，其实意在苟合。”女正色曰：“此何言也？予所谓报德者，非床第之爱，乃衣钵之传。君有夙根，前程甚远，顾质美未学，犹璞玉未琢。自揣学问固陋，然教君尚觉有余。君肯师我，当竭力诲之。”生踯蹰有难色。女云：“满招损，谦受益。

    Explanation

    327

    Sheng said, "Wandering in the market, are you not the one I saw? Do not pretend to be a fox spirit to deceive me." The woman replied, "Do not doubt me too much. Consider this: in the bustling market, and in the secluded school, what kind of girl could come and go alone, making everyone around her as deaf and dumb as if they were not there?" Sheng suddenly realized, "You are indeed a divine being. What is your purpose in coming?" The woman said, "Let me tell you honestly. In your previous life, you were a wealthy person from Shanxi, often buying animals from hunters to save their lives. At that time, I had not yet achieved enlightenment and was mistakenly captured by a hunter. Fortunately, you saved me. I come specifically to repay this virtue." Sheng said, "This is just your old trick. Whether you say it's fate or repayment of virtue, in reality, you are after something more." The woman spoke sternly, "What are you saying? The virtue I wish to repay is not about physical intimacy, but about passing on knowledge. You have a strong foundation and a bright future ahead. However, your qualities are excellent but lack in learning, like an unpolished jade. Although my knowledge is limited, I still feel capable of teaching you. If you agree to learn from me, I will do my best to instruct you." Sheng hesitated and looked uneasy. The woman said, "Pride brings loss, while humility brings gain."

    328

    今汝趾高气扬，宜其一芹尚不能采。”生闻言，怒曰：“衡文者无目，于我何尤？”女曰：“不怨胜己者，当反求诸己。君不知责己，徒詈主司何为？”生曰：“此老生常谈也。予何尝不知责己，所以不平者，以他人非能胜己耳。”女云：“何也？”生云：“如某之功名以贿赂得，某之功名以夹带得，某则以关节得，某则文本不佳以侥幸得。我何有其一？不过但凭文耳。”女曰：“此正人之所以胜己也。彼以贿赂，君当自怨无钱；彼以夹带，君当自怨无胆；彼以关节，君当自怨无门径；彼以侥幸，君当自怨无命运。数者并无，然则可凭者文而已。

    Explanation

    328

    "Your arrogance is evident, yet you cannot even pick a single celery," the woman remarked. Upon hearing this, the young man became angry and retorted, "The examiners are blind; what fault is there with me?" She replied, "Do not blame those who surpass you; instead, look inward and examine yourself. You do not know how to take responsibility for yourself, so why merely curse the examiners?" He said, "This is just trite talk. I am well aware of the need to take responsibility for myself, but I feel justified in my anger because others have succeeded through unfair means." She asked, "Why is that?" He explained, "Some have gained their success through bribery, others through cheating, some through connections, and still others through the sheer luck of a poorly written text. I have none of these advantages; I can only rely on my own writing." She responded, "This is precisely why others have surpassed you. If they succeeded through bribery, you should blame yourself for lacking money; if they cheated, you should blame yourself for lacking courage; if they used connections, you should blame yourself for lacking influence; if they succeeded through luck, you should blame yourself for lacking fortune. None of these apply to you, so the only thing you can rely on is your own writing."

    329

    君平心而论，文果佳乎？”曰：“佳！”女曰：“如文之某处，何以云佳？”历指其疵，宛如目睹。生大惊曰：“君未见吾文，何以指谬如是之不爽耶？君真神人，敢不拜服！”言毕再拜，执弟子礼。女曰：“君服我之神耶，抑服我之识耶？若服我之神，是我以术取；若服我之识，是我以学胜也。圣人之道，不过平易近人，愿君勿以奇异视我。则子之学日益进矣。”生曰：“今而后敬聆师训。”女喜曰：“孺子真可教，从此呼为姊弟可也。”因呼为“胡姊”，遂独居静室，夜为生讲贯，剖疑析义，问无不知。生偶有荒怠，辄加督责，懔若师保。每夜来，将晓即去，卒不及乱，而色厉词严，亦不敢犯之也。尝谓生曰：“读书之道，当取其精而遗其粗。

    329

    "Let's discuss your writing impartially, is the writing truly good?" he asked. "Yes!" she replied. "How can you say such and such a part of the text is good?" she asked, pointing out each flaw as if seen with one's own eyes. The student was greatly surprised and said, "You haven't seen my writing, how can you point out the errors so accurately? You are truly a divine being, how dare I not bow in submission!" After saying this, he bowed twice, adopting the etiquette of a disciple. She said, "Do you admire my divinity or my knowledge? If you admire my divinity, it is because I use magic; if you admire my knowledge, it is because I have won through learning. The way of the sage is nothing more than accessible and approachable, I hope you do not see me as extraordinary. Then your studies will gradually improve." The student said, "From now on, I will respectfully listen to your teachings." She was pleased and said, "This child is truly teachable, from now on, we can call each other siblings." So she called herself "Sister Hu," and then lived alone in a quiet room. At night, she taught the student, clarifying doubts and analyzing meanings, there was no question she couldn't answer. When the student occasionally neglected his studies, she would immediately reprimand him, as strict as a teacher and guardian. Every night, she came and left just before dawn, she never allowed any disorder, her expression was stern and her words were severe, he dared not defy her either. She once said to the student, "The way of reading, one should take the essence and discard the superficial."

    330

    古人所谓观其大略者，非疏忽也，其用心不在寻章摘句耳。今君之案头所陈者，不过讲章一卷，时文数百艺而已，其识见果安在哉？夫博览经史、诸子百家烙化于胸中，固亦大难，第学问长进，不可不阔眼界。今之时艺最足缚人才思，并令人无暇更读他书。然诸书与古诗文，亦不可不着意也。”生曰：“朱注可尊乎？可驳乎？”女曰：“晦翁《四子章句》一书，如暗室逢灯，夜行见炬，洵属有功于世。然其中亦有不可过拘泥者。如『获罪于天』，天谓即理也。意以为于理不合，即于天不合，故谓天为理。若谓理即是天，天即是理，似乎误矣。

    Explanation

    330

    The ancients who spoke of observing the general outline were not being negligent; their focus was not on searching for phrases and picking out sentences. What you have laid out on your desk now consists of no more than a single scholarly text and hundreds of contemporary writings—where, then, lies your true insight? To thoroughly read the classics, histories, and the works of the hundred schools of thought, and to have them deeply internalized, is indeed a great challenge. Yet, for the advancement of learning, it is essential to broaden one’s horizons. The contemporary arts, however, are most capable of confining one’s thoughts and leaving no time to read other books. Nevertheless, one should still pay attention to various books and ancient poetry and prose. The student asked, “Are Zhu Xi’s commentaries to be respected? Can they be criticized?” The woman replied, “Zhu Xi’s Four Books Commentary is like finding a lamp in a dark room or seeing a torch in the night—it truly benefits the world. Yet, there are aspects within it that should not be overly rigidly adhered to. For instance, in the phrase ‘to incur the wrath of Heaven,’ Heaven is said to be synonymous with reason. The idea is that if something does not conform to reason, it does not conform to Heaven, hence Heaven is called reason. If one were to say that reason is Heaven, and Heaven is reason, it seems to be in error.”

    331

    果如晦翁之言，则『天厌之』，何不云理厌之？『天将以夫子为木铎』，何不云理将以夫子为木铎？并『理丧予』、『吾之不遇鲁侯理也』、『夫理未欲平治天下也』等句，皆改成理字，可能讲乎？予不必多赘。如此等处，当独具眼力，不可为古人所愚。非必一知半解，强词夺理，便欲压倒古人。夫取其意，何可摘其非；尊其言，不必护其短。此平心之论，何必先存一尊之驳之之见于胸中耶？至朱子《诗经注》，则多有模棱语。如《郑风》率指为淫奔，且如『此疑亦淫奔之词』，既疑何注？拘泥处如『惧其嫁不及时，而有强暴之辱。』何其迂阔！夫辱不辱在人，受辱不受辱在己。

    Explanation

    331

    If it is indeed as Mr. Hui's words suggest, then why does it say "heaven disapproves of it" rather than "reason disapproves of it"? Why does it say "heaven will make you a bell for the people" rather than "reason will make you a bell for the people"? And in phrases like "reason has abandoned me," "it is because I did not meet the Duke of Lu's reason," and "reason does not wish to govern the world," why not change "天" to "理"? I do not need to add more commentary. In such places, one should have independent judgment and not be deceived by the ancients. It is not necessary to have a superficial understanding and force arguments to dominate ancient texts. One should take the meaning of the text without nitpicking and respect the words without defending their flaws. This is a fair and balanced view. Why must one preconceive notions of respect or criticism in their heart? As for Zhu Xi's annotations on the Book of Songs, they often contain ambiguous statements. For example, in Zheng Feng, he often interprets them as referring to promiscuous behavior, and phrases like "this is also likely a word of promiscuous behavior," but if it is doubtful, why make a note? Such rigid interpretations, like "fearing that she will not marry in time and be subjected to violent dishonor," are so impractical! Whether one is dishonored or not depends on the person, and whether one is dishonored or not depends on oneself.

    332

    焉有嫁不及时，遽来强暴之辱？遂令贞信自守者以此为可惧。吾想极乱之世尚不致如此，况二南被化之国乎？此等处可以不必死于句下也。”生闻言，如晨钟暮鼓，入耳豁然，遂悦服遵教。每塾中课题，女先一日即知，为生讲析研究，俾成佳作。漏尽女自去，生始就枕。如是年余，生文思大进，塾师不能易一字。同学者共猜之，然不料其有是也。一夜，有同学生起溺，闻生室中有人声，舐窗私窥，见生孤坐灯下，言犹未已。叩扃穷诘，生不能隐，具以实告。同学生曰：“妖由人兴，不早与绝，是自求祸。伊谁见古来名儒，有受业于狐者？学品果优，避之不暇，况公然敢犯函丈而居师位乎？”盖阳虽规诫，而阴怀忌妒。

    Explanation

    332

    There is no situation where a woman is forced into an unwanted marriage so soon after her marriageable age, thus making those who are virtuous and self-disciplined fear this situation. I think even in the most chaotic times, such a situation would not occur, let alone in a country influenced by the virtues of the Two Nans. Such situations do not necessarily warrant death by the letter of the law. The young man heard these words and was enlightened as if by the sound of morning and evening bells, and thus willingly obeyed the teachings. Every day, the girl knew the school assignments a day in advance, explained and studied them, helping him to produce excellent work. When the night watchman's bell rang, the girl left, and then the young man went to bed. After a year or so, his literary talent greatly improved, and the schoolmaster could not change a single word. His classmates guessed together, but they did not expect such a thing. One night, a classmate got up to urinate and heard voices in the young man's room. Peering through the window, he saw the young man sitting alone by the light of a lamp, still speaking. He knocked on the door and pressed for answers, and the young man could not hide it, so he told the truth. The classmate said, "Evil comes from people. If you do not sever ties early, you will bring disaster upon yourself. Who has ever seen a famous scholar in ancient times who learned from a fox? If one's academic qualities are truly excellent, they would avoid it, let alone openly dare to take the place of a teacher." Although he appeared to be advising him, he secretly harbored jealousy.

    333

    因与众约，次日各持利刃，突入生室，左右乱击。生见女怫然而去，乃谓众曰：“诸君不必恶作，彼已去矣。”众乃挽生同榻。生忿未眠，闻窗外女呼曰：“宫生学业已成，拾青紫如抬芥，好自为之，毋荒旧业可也。予与君缘分已满，即无诸君此举，予亦不久留。尚烦寄语诸君，如某某与我有一面缘，五年后会于石桥，志之勿忘，予去也。”众聆言不胜悔惧，急开户出视，但见星月皎洁，银河在天，而女已渺。后生与众友同车入都，应春闱试。至通州，经石桥，见一女子姿容绝代，自对面来。众目送之曰：“美而艳！”生审视曰：“是胡师也！”下车即不复见。众亦惊叹久之。是科宫高捷南宫，其友亦同榜登第。

    Explanation

    333

    Thus, he made an agreement with the group, and the next day, each holding a sharp blade, they suddenly entered the room of the male and attacked wildly from all sides. The male saw the female leave angrily and then told the group, "Gentlemen need not be afraid; she has already left." The group then persuaded the male to share the bed. The male was angry and unable to sleep, and he heard a female calling from outside the window: "Palace Male's studies are already completed, and obtaining high office is as easy as picking up mustard seeds. Do well on your own, and do not neglect your old profession. My缘分 with you is already fulfilled; even without this action of yours, I would not stay for long. Please convey this message to the gentlemen: if someone has met me once, we will meet at the stone bridge in five years. Remember this and do not forget, I am leaving." The group, hearing the words, were filled with regret and fear, and quickly opened the door to look outside. But they saw only bright stars and moon, and the Milky Way in the sky, and the female had vanished. Later, the male and his friends entered the capital together in a carriage to take the spring imperial examination. Arriving at Tongzhou, they passed by the stone bridge and saw a female with extraordinary beauty coming from the opposite direction. The group watched her go and said, "Beautiful and charming!" The male, upon closer inspection, said, "It is Teacher Hu!" After getting out of the carriage, she was no longer seen. The group also marveled for a long time. In that year, Palace Male passed the imperial examination and ranked high, and his friends also passed the imperial examination in the same batch.

    334

    伍明伦
    　　伍明伦，渤海人，家无恒业，为盐商伙友。偶失事，穷困无聊，或荐之于豫商。至豫，则肆中人溢于事，商使之居市后闲院，月给以青蚨千许，以俟有缺补用。伍愁闷欲死，思归故乡，而又无资斧，遂隐忍之，大遭诸役白眼，饮馔或呼与之。一夜，独坐廊下玩月，仰视月中隐隐有红霞一朵，直降庭际，光彩射目，一红裳女子艳丽如仙。伍惊起，女子笑曰：“勿怖勿怖，何前日心愿，今遽忘耶？”伍不解，不觉膝为之屈。女曳之并坐，询其起居。伍诺诺。女隐以钩蹑其胫，间以游语，遂相狎昵。

    Explanation

    334

    Wu Minglun

    Wu Minglun was a native of Bohai, a man without a fixed occupation, serving as an assistant to a salt merchant. Once he got into trouble, he became poor and aimlessly depressed, and someone recommended him to a merchant from Henan. Upon arriving in Henan, the business was already full of people, so the merchant made him live in a quiet courtyard behind the market, giving him a thousand copper coins per month to wait for a vacancy to be filled. Wu was so depressed that he wanted to die, thought of returning to his hometown, but had no money for travel expenses, so he endured it silently. He was treated with contempt by the servants and sometimes called upon to share meals with them. One night, he sat alone under the eaves playing with the moon, looking up, he saw a faint red cloud in the moon, directly descending into the courtyard, its light shone brightly into his eyes. There was a beautiful woman in red clothes, lovely as a fairy. Wu was startled and got up, the woman said, "Do not be afraid, do not be afraid, why have you forgotten your wish from before?" Wu did not understand, unconsciously, his knees bent. The woman pulled him to sit beside her, asked about his daily life, Wu nodded in response. The woman secretly hooked her foot around his leg, and spoke to him in a playful manner, they became intimate with each other.

    335

    伍问：“卿自月中来，得毋嫦娥也耶？”女曰：“嫦娥月殿仙人，妾不过宫中之侍婢耳。君前身亦月中人，捣药仙翁是君大父，君其子孙行也。妾在殿前为仙人取药，君忽以眉目送情，仙人知之，怒欲刑责，为捣药翁苦求得免，于是降谪人间。不图一染尘浊，前日事茫不记忆。妾此来亦是私奔，聊报君之痴情耳。”问：“嫦娥知之奈何？”曰：“彼由人世奔入月中，妾由月中奔入尘世，其奔一也，乌得禁之？虽然，亦须慎密，彼若觉之，甚无益也。每望月满，妾当自来。”天将晓，飘然而去，至期果来。如是年余，依恋最笃。

    Explanation

    335

    Wu asked, "Did you not come from the moon? Are you not Chang'e?" The woman replied, "Chang'e is a goddess of the moon palace, but I am merely a servant in the palace. Your former self was also from the moon; the immortal who grinds medicine is your grandfather, and you are his descendant. I fetched medicine for the immortals in front of the palace, and you suddenly conveyed affection with your gaze. The immortals knew and were angry, wanting to punish you. The immortal who grinds medicine pleaded hard, and you were spared. Thus, you were demoted to the mortal world. I did not expect to be tainted by the dust of the mortal world; the events of the past are now unclear in my memory. I am coming here to elope, just to repay your foolish affection." Wu asked, "What if Chang'e finds out?" She said, "She ran from the mortal world into the moon, and I ran from the moon into the mortal world. Our elopements are the same, how can they be forbidden? However, we must be careful. If she discovers it, it will be of no use. Every time the moon is full, I will come to you." As dawn approached, she left lightly, and as expected, she came. For over a year, their attachment was very deep.

    336

    伍以贫窘，浼其援助，女曰：“妾无能为力，君家捣药翁有奇术，可博取富贵。君于十六夜设香案拜祷，当有效。”如其教，商果遣伍司会计。又半载，肆中掌橐钥者故，商使补其缺。于是衣履饮馔，较前悬殊，即众役亦奔走逢迎，供驱使焉。又久之，积有金帛，遂为狭邪游，歌童舞妓，不离左右，商颇厌恶。女私谓伍曰：“君薄有赢余，便不安其分，居停颇不耐之，可为奈何？”伍爽然自失，求计于女。女曰：“君家翁自有妙术，明日妾浼其同来，君自哀之。”次夕果偕至，皤皤然一白须老翁也。伍拜尽礼，翁喜曰：“阿儿勿忧，有老夫衣钵在，保无虞也。

    Explanation

    336

    Wu, distressed by his poverty, asked her for help. The woman said, "I have no power to assist, but your master who makes medicine has a unique skill that can bring wealth and status. On the sixteenth night, set up an altar and pray to him, and it should be effective." Following her advice, the merchant indeed sent Wu to manage the accounting. After half a year, the person in charge of the purse strings in the shop died, and the merchant appointed Wu to fill the vacancy. Thus, Wu's clothing, shoes, food, and drink were significantly better than before, and even the other servants ran around and catered to him, serving him as if he were their master. After a long time, Wu accumulated wealth and silk, and he began to frequent the brothel district, surrounded by young male singers and female dancers. The merchant grew quite displeased with this. The woman privately told Wu, "With just a little surplus, you are already dissatisfied with your position, and the host is very dissatisfied with it. What can be done about this?" Wu felt a sudden sense of loss and sought advice from the woman. She said, "Your master has his own special skill. Tomorrow, I will ask him to come with me, and you should beg him." The next evening, they indeed arrived together, and there was an old man with a full white beard. Wu bowed to him with all due respect, and the old man smiled and said, "Do not worry, my son. I have passed down my skills to you, and you will be without worry."

    337

    ”携手至密室，授其术。伍从此挟其术以悦豫商，商惑焉，衣履易著，食同座，寝同床，金钱任伍自取勿过问。伍颇快意，益感翁，朔望必设祭。豫之缙绅官长结交为昆仲，皆以得友伍为荣。众中有鲠直者，伍以其术惑之，罔不颠倒；向之薄待己者，设计惩创之。家蓄多金，颇置良产。自顾生平于愿已足，乃购某家废园，将建月华楼为迎女也。工未兴，女忽至，谓伍曰：“好事者白于仙子，仙子甚恚，不令妾离跬步。今此一别，永无见期，君亦宜自敛抑。盈满为灾，悲于死丧，慎之。”泪滴红裳，乱如雨落。伍亦泣不能抑。未几，霞光满室，照灼如火，众役佥至，而女已渺。

    Explanation

    337

    Holding hands, they went to a secret room where the technique was imparted to Wu. From then on, Wu used the technique to please and delight Shang, who was bewitched by it. Shang's clothes and shoes changed easily, they ate together at the same table, slept together on the same bed, and money could be taken freely by Wu without asking. Wu felt quite pleased and even more grateful to the old man, offering sacrifices on the first and fifteenth day of each month. The officials and dignitaries of Yu formed friendships with Wu as brothers, all considering it an honor to have Wu as a friend. Among the crowd, there were some who were honest and upright, but Wu used the technique to bewitch them, and none remained unchanged. Those who had treated him poorly in the past, he devised plans to punish them. The family accumulated much wealth and acquired good property. Looking back on his life, Wu felt that his wishes were fulfilled, so he bought a certain family's abandoned garden to build the Moonlight Pavilion to welcome the girl. The work had not yet begun when the girl suddenly arrived and told Wu, "Someone told the fairy, and the fairy was very angry. She did not allow the maid to leave her side even a step. Now this parting, we will never meet again. You should also restrain yourself. Fullness brings disaster, more sorrowful than death. Be cautious." Tears dripped onto her red dress, falling like rain. Wu also wept, unable to control himself. Soon, the room was filled with rosy light, shining brightly like fire. All the workers came together, and the girl had vanished.

    338

    醉茶子曰：天下之恶习染人深者，莫过于盐务。故昔人云：“务者，误也。”言误人也。其充商者姑勿论，即如盐伙，其劣尤甚，故又谓之“盐毒”。毒者，深入膏肓，不可救药，较诸游幕曰“幕习”、作官曰“官派”为尤甚也。所以然者，皆众小人谄谀所致。为商人总司其事者，群呼为“总台”，作镇曰“镇台”，其尊号直与督抚藩臬并驾齐驱矣。其至引地也曰“回店”，于是店役跪接于道左，巡役护送于途中。甫一登程，安车早备。才经回店，盛馔早开。其获盐犯以送官，俨若献俘；其结盐案以达商，宛如报捷。种种恶习，难以枚举。及其失事也，嗒焉丧志，皇皇然如丧家之犬。始也贪囊尚存私蓄，聊以糊口；久则质物典衣，无所不至，萧然四壁，徒叹奈何。向之气焰蒸蒸者，至是一扫而净矣。呜呼，天下谋食之道甚多，何必为此哉！

    Explanation

    338

    Drunk Tea Master said: Among all the evil habits that deeply corrupt people, none is more so than the salt trade. Therefore, ancient people said, "Business is a mistake." They meant that it leads people astray. As for those who engage in commerce, let us not discuss them; but consider the salt traders, whose corruption is even worse, hence they are also called "salt poison." Poison, which penetrates deep into the vitals, is incurable, more so than the habits of those in the literary world called "literary customs" or officials called "official manners." This is due to the flattery of many small people. Those who manage the business for merchants are collectively called "total platform," and those who act as governors are called "governor platform." Their titles are on par with those of governors, provincial inspectors, and vice-governors. When they return to their base, it is called "return to the store," and thus the store servants kneel to greet them on the side of the road, and the guards escort them along the way. As soon as they start their journey, a comfortable carriage is ready. As soon as they return to the store, a lavish meal is already prepared. When they capture salt smugglers and send them to the authorities, it is like presenting captives; when they resolve salt cases and report to the merchants, it is like reporting a victory. Various evil habits are too numerous to list. When they fail, they lose their spirit and behave like a homeless dog. At first, they still had some private savings to survive; over time, they pawn their belongings and sell their clothes, until they are destitute and can only sigh in despair. Those who were once full of arrogance are now completely gone. Alas, there are many ways to make a living, why choose this one?

    339

    鼠友
    　　江苏乙，寄寓直隶客店，传者忘其何县何驿，独卧吸烟。有大鼠如猫，伏床边俯嗅烟气，迥不畏人。乙曰：“尔亦瘾耶？”戏喷之。鼠受之以鼻，半晌，徐徐自去。如是数日，率以为常。乙本长随，时失事，资斧匮乏，愁闷无策，向鼠叹曰：“囊罄粮绝，困惫待毙，恐不能与君常吸此味矣。”言毕，唏嘘久之。鼠闻之，双目灼灼，若有所思，返身遂去。须臾复返，衔洋钱一元置枕旁。乙欢然捡取，出购芙蓉膏就灯烧吸，大肆吞吐。鼠亦酣畅淋漓，饱其所闻而去。次日复至，衔洋元三饼。

    Explanation

    339

    Rat Friend

    Jiangsu’s Second Gentleman, lodging temporarily at a guesthouse in Zhili, was forgotten by the narrator as to which county or post station it was. He lay alone, smoking. A large rat, the size of a cat, crouched by the bed, sniffing the smoke, not at all afraid of people. Second Gentleman said, “Are you also addicted?” and playfully puffed smoke at it. The rat accepted it through its nose, for a while, then slowly left on its own. This happened for several days, and he treated it as a regular occurrence. Second Gentleman was originally a long-term servant, and at times he lost his job, leaving him with scarce funds and no resources, filled with worry and despair. He sighed to the rat, “My purse is empty and my provisions exhausted; I am exhausted and waiting for death. I fear I won’t be able to enjoy this flavor with you anymore.” After speaking, he sighed for a long time. The rat, hearing this, had its eyes shining brightly, as if deep in thought, then turned around and left. Soon it returned, carrying a silver dollar and placing it beside the pillow. Second Gentleman joyfully picked it up, went out to buy opium, and smoked it by the lamp, indulging in smoking. The rat also enjoyed it thoroughly and satisfyingly, satisfied with the smell and left. The next day, it returned, carrying three silver dollars.

    340

    乙益喜，喟然曰：“知管仲者鲍叔牙君，知我贫而厚施于我，是我之叔牙也，谁谓鼠无牙哉！”自此呼鼠为朋友。鼠辄至，饮食与共，吸烟则伏其旁，夜则共寝，人畜相安，不啻莫逆。鼠每出必衔洋元一二枚，日三四返。积半载，囊橐充牣，衣食俱丰，甚德鼠。逆旅主人窥其状，颇涉疑惧，细察之，昼无所营，夜扃户寝。吸烟外无事不出户庭，又不滥交友，决其非盗也，益奇之。乙有旧主某太守，将之任所，适寓此店。见乙询其何往，乙对以闲居将谋栖止。时太守乏干仆，令其随任，乙欢然乐从。次晨束装就道，行数日，已越数百里。

    Explanation

    340

    乙更加高兴，感慨地说：“了解管仲的人是鲍叔牙，了解我的贫困而慷慨地帮助我，这就是我的叔牙，谁说老鼠没有牙齿呢！”从此他称老鼠为朋友。老鼠总是来，一起吃饭，吸烟时就在旁边趴着，晚上一起睡觉，人和动物相处得很和谐，不仅仅是莫逆之交。老鼠每次出去都叼着一两枚洋元，每天三四次往返。积累半年，袋子装得满满的，衣食都很丰盛，非常感激老鼠。客栈的主人观察他的状况，非常怀疑和害怕，仔细观察，发现他白天无所事事，晚上关门睡觉。除了吸烟外，没有其他事情不出门，又不滥交朋友，断定他不是小偷，更加觉得奇怪。乙有旧主某太守，即将前往任职的地方，正好住在这家店。太守见到乙询问他要去哪里，乙回答说闲居将要寻找住处。当时太守缺乏干练的仆人，命令他随行，乙高兴地答应。第二天早晨收拾行李上路，走了几天，已经走了几百里。

    341

    饭后吸烟，忽忆及鼠，失声曰：“我友危矣。”同人诘其为谁，不答，慨然诣主请假。主人询其故，亦讳不言，但叩首请赏假。太守许之。乙驱车昼夜兼行，至客店，顾谓主人曰：“我居之屋有人寓否？”曰：“未也。”急令发其钥，尘土满榻，诸物如故。乙张皇四顾，若有所失。主人骇而问故，不答，急遣主人去。主人方出户，乙大呼朋友者再。主人以为唤己也，急入，乙又遣之。主人益疑，立窗外潜窥之。见乙遍搜室内，揭席得大鼠，扪之，气绝而体已僵矣，失声大痛，抱于怀，号泣甚悲。

    Explanation

    341

    After the meal, he smoked, suddenly recalling the mouse, and exclaimed, "My friend is in danger." The others asked who it was, but he did not answer, and went to the master to request leave. The master asked the reason, but he also avoided speaking, and knelt to request leave. The prefect granted it. B drove the carriage day and night, arriving at the inn, and looked at the owner and said, "Is there anyone staying in the house I live in?" "No," the owner replied. B quickly ordered the key to be brought, and found the bed covered in dust, with everything as before. B looked around in panic, as if something was missing. The owner was startled and asked why, and B quickly sent the owner away. The owner was just about to leave when B called out to his friend twice. The owner thought B was calling him, and quickly went in, but B sent him away again. The owner became even more suspicious, and stood outside the window and peered in secretly, seeing B searching the room thoroughly, uncovering the mat and finding a big rat, feeling it, and finding it dead and stiff. B cried out in great pain, held it in his arms, and wept bitterly.

    342

    主人诘之，不答，藏鼠于怀，出门去，市购衣衾棺木甚美，使舁于店中，怀出死鼠，殓而哭奠之。祭毕，向主人备述颠末，且云：“我困苦欲死，非鼠无以有今日。不图一时疏忽，致令戕其生。鼠非我杀，实由我而死。九泉之下，负此良友，我何以为情哉？”泣涕不止。主人疑团冰释，浩叹久之。初，鼠之盗洋元也，肆中失物者凡数家。有一家失去尤多，随处严察，终未获盗。物议纷腾，连累颇众。至是市人佥闻之，而亦无如何。乙买田数亩葬鼠，封树立碑而后去。归白太守，太守叹曰：“不忘德，仁也；不负心，义也。鼠尚不负，宁负我哉!”重用之。

    Explanation

    342

    The owner questioned him, but he did not answer. Instead, he hid the dead mouse in his bosom and left. He then went to the market to purchase very fine clothing, shrouds, and a coffin. He had the items carried to the shop, and from his bosom, he took out the dead mouse, laid it to rest, and mourned and offered sacrifices to it. After the rites were completed, he told the owner the full story, saying, "I was so destitute that I was on the verge of death. Without the mouse, I would not have had today. I did not expect that a momentary lapse of attention would lead to its death. The mouse was not killed by me, but it died because of me. In the netherworld, I would be in debt to this good friend. How could I live with myself?" He wept uncontrollably. The owner's doubts melted away, and he sighed deeply for a long time. Initially, when the mouse had stolen the foreign currency, several shops had lost items. One shop in particular had lost a great deal, and thorough investigations were conducted everywhere, but the thief was never found. Public opinion was divided, and many were implicated. By this time, the townspeople had all heard of the matter, but there was nothing they could do about it. The man bought several acres of land to bury the mouse, erected a tombstone, and then left. Upon returning, he reported the incident to the prefect. The prefect sighed and said, "To remember kindness is benevolence; to remain true to one's heart is righteousness. If even a mouse is not betrayed, how could I be betrayed?" He then gave the man a prominent position.

    343

    醉茶子曰：人当困苦之际，箪食豆羹之惠，其德不忘，况千金之赠乎！厚报死鼠，固其宜也。盗泉之水，虽渴不饿，此充类至义之行，求之古人尚难之，况庸人乎？原其心，悲其遇，可矣。昔人云：“屋内吸烟，蝇鼠等俱有瘾。”由是观之，洵不诬也。然为瘾所迫，至使鼠亦盗金，则人之荡检逾闲者，更不奇矣。顾得之则生，勿得则死，鼠终以此毕命。洋烟之患，流毒及于庶类，可胜叹哉!予有《烟赋》，录子左。其词曰：“绵邈春愁，用何物兮解忧？有芙蓉膏之异味，非鲁璜越璧之难求。花名莺而色艳，烟号鸦而味稠。种自分乎墨白，用无间乎春秋。灯惟贵乎太谷，斗必取乎寿州。故使嗜其味者，俾昼作夜，化刚为柔。方其始也，无事萦身，有文在手。或伴青娥，或寻红友。趣觅灯前，事添饭后。食之以时，尝焉亦偶。一钱二钱，七口八口。谓夫宿疾全消，精神顿有。非戚将军之临阵，何用灰瓶；非范大夫之当筵，胡持玉斗。

    Explanation

    343

    The Tea Drinker said: When people are in distress, they do not forget the kindness of a simple meal or a bowl of porridge, let alone a gift of a thousand gold pieces! To repay a small favor with great gratitude is indeed appropriate. Even if one is thirsty, one should not drink from the water of the Thieves' Spring, for this is a righteous act that extends a principle to its logical conclusion, and it is difficult even for ancient sages, let alone ordinary people. One can understand their hearts and pity their plight. As the ancients said, "Even flies and mice in a house can develop habits." From this, it is indeed true that even small creatures can develop dependencies. However, when driven by addiction, even a mouse will steal gold, and people who exceed moral boundaries are even more extraordinary. If one gains what they desire, they live; if they do not, they die. The mouse ultimately meets its end in this way. The scourge of opium, spreading its poison to all kinds of people, is truly lamentable! I have an essay on opium, which I will record below. The words go: "The distant and lingering sorrow of spring, what can relieve my worries? There is the unique flavor of opium, not as rare as the precious stones of Lu and Yue. The flower is named after the oriole and is brightly colored; the smoke is called after crows and is thick. The varieties are distinct, and it is used without regard to the seasons. The lamps are most valued from Tai Gu, and the opium is always sought from Shouzhou. Thus, those who crave its flavor make day into night, turning strength into weakness. At first, they are not preoccupied, and they have writing in hand. They may accompany young women or seek companionship. They search for the lamp before meals and add to their activities after meals. They consume it regularly, sometimes once, sometimes twice, seven or eight times. They claim that all old illnesses are cured, and their spirits are suddenly renewed. If it is not like General Cao Cao on the battlefield, why would one need a lamp? If it is not like Fan Li at a banquet, why would one hold a jade cup?"

    344

    仙殊弄玉，横吹翡翠之箫；人岂陶潜，高卧烟霞之薮。则有枕高灯矮，床小屏遮，消闲岁月，吞吐云霞。招众民于广土，系荡子于名花。首尾都精，镶柱头于美玉；细微必达，隔斗柄以轻纱。对枕谈心，尽倾肝鬲；向灯呵欠，徐动齿牙。味酿胸中，顿醒襄陵之酒；香留舌本，犹浇顾渚之茶。又有花浓野馆，春满藤床，结风月偶入温柔乡。解醒汤无容更饮，相思草可许同尝。香桔干而为斗，甘蔗老而作枪。

    Explanation

    344

    An Extraordinary Woman, Fei Changfang, plays the jade-green flute with divine skill; how could a person be like Tao Yuanming, reclining carefree in a land where clouds and mist gather? There are high pillows and low lamps, a small bed screened off, leisurely years spent, inhaling and exhaling the mist and clouds. Gathering many people in a vast land, tying down a wandering soul with the allure of beautiful flowers. Everything is refined, adorned with column heads of beautiful jade; even the smallest details are conveyed, separated by the delicate gauze of the celestial handle. Facing the pillow and talking heart-to-heart, pouring out deep emotions and feelings; facing the lamp and yawning, slowly moving the teeth and gums. Brewing flavors in the chest, suddenly awakening to the clarity of the wine from Xiangling; the fragrance remaining on the root of the tongue, still pouring the tea from Guzhu. There is a flower-rich rural guesthouse, a woven bed filled with the warmth of spring, forming a bond with the wind and moon and entering a romantic and intimate setting. A drink to sober up no longer allows for more drinking, a grass of longing can be shared together. Fragrant dried tangerines are used as dice, old sugarcane is made into a spear.

    345

    迷清梦于灯前，微酣蝴蝶；薰浓香于被底，羡煞鸳鸯。苟浅尝辄止，即多食无妨。至若锦绣人才，貔貅武士，即贪恋而旷功，亦因循而废事。萦烟气于寸衷，粘烟痕于两指。凌晨未起，久疏囊萤之功；晌午犹眠，定减射雕之技。欲奋勉以求名，胡颓靡而若此。迨夫囊钱告罄，贫无立锥，已成大隐，无有止期。色枯槁而类鬼，项瑟缩而如龟。身战战兮骨立，目汪汪兮泪垂。幸余劫后之灰，火能重炼；纵有淋旁之土，水难再追。如兹丑态，是谓穷吹；苟不力戒，亦奚以为。奉劝隐者，已而已而。”

    Explanation

    345

    Lost in a dream before the lamp, slightly intoxicated like a butterfly; enveloped by a strong fragrance beneath the quilt, envying the harmonious mandarin ducks. If one only tastes it lightly, even eating a lot won't cause harm. As for talented and refined individuals, valiant warriors, if they are greedy and neglect their duties, they will also become complacent and abandon their tasks. Enveloped by the smoke in their hearts, with the marks of smoke on their fingers. Not rising at dawn, long having neglected the diligence of studying by candlelight; still sleeping at noon, surely reducing their skill in archery. Wanting to strive hard to gain fame, why are they so decayed? When the money in their bag is exhausted, living in extreme poverty with no place to stand, they have become great recluses, with no end in sight. Their appearance is withered, resembling a ghost, their neck hunched like a turtle. Their body trembles, bones protruding, their eyes teary, tears streaming down. Fortunately, the ashes after a disaster can be relit with fire; once water has been poured, it cannot be retrieved. Such a pitiful state is called extreme poverty and despair; if not strongly admonished, what is the point? I advise the reclusive person, stop, stop.

    346

    阿菱
    　　王生愿，齐之世家子。步年轩轾，迥非常俦。父名司理，训诲綦严，日闭馆中课之读，虽亲戚不使通庆吊。时村中有演戏者，生私出观之。剧终天晚，恐归受责，徘徊道左，远望家门而零涕焉。未几，信步出村，东行里许，路旁一老姥谓曰：“若个好男儿，天已如许晚，尚踟蹰于此，将何往耶？”生以情告。姥云：“归必受楚，不如随我去便佳。寒舍不远，当不使郎君露处。”生收涕大悦，遂同行。至一村，短篱周匣，中有柴门如窦，携生手俯身入。中殊宽厂。

    Explanation

    346

    阿菱
    　　王生愿，是齐国的世家子弟。他容貌俊美，远超常人。他的父亲名叫司理，对他的教育极为严格，每天把他关在书房里读书，即使是亲戚也不允许送喜讯或吊唁。有一次村里有人演戏，他偷偷出去观看。戏演完天色已晚，他害怕回家后会受到责罚，便在路边徘徊，远远望着家门，不禁流下了眼泪。不久，他信步走出村子，向东走了几里路，路旁有一位老妇人对他说：“你这样的好男儿，天色已这么晚了，还在这里徘徊，打算去哪里呢？”他把自己的情况告诉了老妇人。老妇人说：“你回去一定会受责罚，不如跟我走吧，我的家不远，不会让你露宿在外。”他擦干眼泪，非常高兴地跟着老妇人一起走。到了一个村子，村子周围有矮矮的篱笆，中间有一个柴门像洞穴一样，老妇人牵着他的手弯腰进去。里面非常宽敞。

    347

    行数武，有老屋数楹，石枕竹床，颇觉雅洁。秉烛命坐，见几上棋枰书籍，不类农家，问：“老姆精于诸艺乎？”姥云：“幼时亦颇娴习，惜未能精。老身皮姓，有一甥女，自幼失怙恃，寄居寒家，针黹之暇，令其释闷。此儿颇慧，乳名阿菱，待老身唤来，伴公子戏。”俄去复返，引一女郎至，双鬟垂耳，娇艳动人，立灯下，秋波微睨，笑态盈盈。生魂魄飞越，应对屡乖。姥云：“菱儿暂陪公子，予去作馔来食。”姥去，生问女郎年几何，女云：“十四。”生云：“小我一岁。

    Explanation

    347

    After walking a few steps, there were a few old buildings, with stone pillows and bamboo beds, giving a quite elegant and clean atmosphere. The old woman lit the candle and invited him to sit, and he saw a chessboard and books on the table, which were not typical of a farmer's household. He asked, "Is the old lady skilled in various arts?" The old woman replied, "In my youth, I was quite proficient, but unfortunately, I could not become an expert. My name is Pi. I have a niece who lost her parents at a young age and lives in my humble home. In her spare time from sewing, I let her relieve her boredom. This girl is very clever, and her pet name is Aling. I will call her to entertain the young master." She went away and returned, bringing a young lady. The young lady had twin braids hanging down, was beautiful and charming, stood by the lamp, her eyes glanced sideways, and had a smiling face. The young man's soul flew away, and his responses were often inappropriate. The old woman said, "Aling, please entertain the young master while I go prepare food for us to eat." The old woman left, and the young man asked the young lady how old she was. She said, "Fourteen." The young man said, "I am one year older than you."

    348

    ”女云：“小一岁便如何？”生云：“此后好呼唤耳。”女云：“谁是尔婢子，辄便呼唤？”生云：“称呼耳！”女云：“何以相称？得毋夜郎自大耶？”生云：“不敢不敢，卿须呼我为郎。”女笑云：“我以为兄也。侬最怕狼，不便相呼。”生云：“不呼郎呼我为甚？”女掩口云：“不呼尔为狼则呼为犬。”生云：“无故奚落人，当罚尔。”遽前夺其帕。女笑声嗤嗤，掷帕于地，生急俯拾，而女早抬起。

    Explanation

    348

    The female said, "What difference would it make if I were a year younger?" The male replied, "Then it would be easier to call out to you afterward." She asked, "Who do you think you are to call me like that?" He responded, "It's just a form of address!" She questioned, "How should I address you? Are you being overly proud?" He humbly said, "I dare not, you must call me '郎'." She laughed and said, "I thought you were my older brother. I'm most afraid of wolves, so it's not convenient to call you that." He asked, "Why won't you call me '郎'?" She covered her mouth and said, "If I don't call you a wolf, then I'll call you a dog." He said, "You're teasing me without reason, you should be punished." He quickly moved forward to take her handkerchief. She laughed and threw it on the ground. He quickly bent down to pick it up, but she lifted it up before he could.

    349

    生持其腕而复夺之，姥揭帘入，左手执盘，右手持匕箸，堆置几上，谓生曰：“此儿自幼娇养，有多慢客，幸公子谅之。”生极赞其慧。姥云：“如此痴憨，不畏郎君笑耶？可入厨去，悉将物来。”无何，女将杯盘罗列盈几。姥云：“我家固无男子，亦无仆婢，蔬食不堪奉客。”生谦让后，食味俱甘脆，而莫能指名。询姥何处得此佳味，姥云：“郎君日居富贵之乡，列八珍，食万羊，此等山肴野蔌，不常食，转以为奇耶？”生食之甘，益询其名。姥云：“盘中红丝白理者，紫驼之峰也；肉腴骨脆者，黑熊之膰也。饭乃红莲之稻，汤乃碧糁之羹。

    Explanation

    349

    The protagonist held the child's wrist and then took it back. The elderly woman entered by lifting the curtain, holding a plate in one hand and a knife and chopsticks in the other, and placed the objects on the table. She said to the protagonist, "This child has been spoiled from a young age and is often rude to guests. Please forgive her, noble sir." The protagonist highly praised the child's intelligence. The elderly woman said, "She is so naive and innocent that she doesn't fear being laughed at by you. She can go to the kitchen and bring everything." Soon, the daughter arranged cups and plates on the table. The elderly woman said, "Our household has no men and no servants, so the simple food is not fit for guests." After the protagonist humbly declined, the food tasted sweet and crisp. He asked the elderly woman where she got such delicious food. She replied, "You live in a wealthy place, enjoying the finest delicacies. These simple dishes from the mountains and fields are not often eaten, so they are considered strange." The protagonist found the food delicious and asked for its name. The elderly woman said, "The red threads and white patterns in the dish are the peak of purple camel. The tender meat and brittle bones are the shoulder of a black bear. The rice is from red lotus and the soup is from green grains."

    350

    市远殊无兼味，但祈强饭为佳。”生赞不已。姥云：“今日既屈嘉宾，愿行一酒令以佐饮，公子愿乎？”生急请命。姥云：“取骰子四枚一掷成点，当拈古诗一句与色子相合，否则罚以金谷酒数。”女一掷，得“三三一六”，云：“三山半落青天外。”生掷得“一么三三”，云：“月点波心一颗珠。”女云：“何不云『万绿丛中一点红』？”姥云：“郎君思亦巧矣。”生请姥掷，姆掷得“两么两六”，云：“双悬日月照乾坤。”令毕，各饮一杯而止。

    Explanation

    350

    The market is far away, and there are no luxurious dishes; one can only hope for a hearty meal to be good." The young man praised without end. The old lady said, "Today, since we have the honor of hosting guests, I would like to carry out a drinking game to accompany the drinking. Would you like to participate, young master?" The young man quickly asked for the rules. The old lady said, "Take four dice and throw them to form points. One should pick an ancient poem line that matches the dice. Otherwise, be punished by the number of jingu jiu." The girl rolled the dice and got "three, three, one, six," and said, "Three mountains half fall into the blue sky." The young man rolled the dice and got "one, one, three, three," and said, "The moon is like a pearl in the center of the waves." The girl said, "Why not say 'A single red dot in a sea of green'?" The old lady said, "Young master, your thoughts are also clever." The young man asked the old lady to roll the dice, and she rolled "two, two, six, six," and said, "The sun and moon hang side by side, illuminating the universe." After the game was over, each person drank one cup of wine and then stopped.

    351

    席终，姥扫榻展卧具，止生宿。生云：“独居胆怯，愿姥遣人伴寝。”姥云：“生醉矣，此处无虎狼，保不衔生去。”乃留烛携女同去。生辗转终夜，思欲直言，恐不遂，或遭其辱。天将晓，闻对户房中老姥与阿菱唧唧不休，恍惚似议婚。晨起姥来，生问阿甥有婿家无，云：“未也。”生云：“择婿如何等人？”姥云：“性情温和，品貌秀雅者方许之。”生云：“若小生者如何矣？”姥沉吟云：“公子甚佳，老身也颇倾慕。俟商之于甥，如其愿也，则今夕良辰，即备青庐，无劳亲迎焉。”生大喜。

    Explanation

    351

    The banquet concluded, and the old lady swept the bed and spread the bedding, inviting the guest to stay overnight. The guest said, "I am afraid to stay alone; I wish the old lady would send someone to keep me company." The old lady replied, "You are drunk; there are no tigers or wolves here, I promise they won't carry you away." She then left the candle and took the girl with her. The guest turned over all night, wanting to speak directly but fearing it wouldn't go well, or he might be humiliated. When dawn approached, he heard the old lady in the room opposite and her granddaughter talking incessantly, vaguely discussing marriage. When the old lady came in the morning, the guest asked if her granddaughter had a fiancé. She said, "Not yet." The guest asked, "What kind of person is chosen for a husband?" The old lady said, "Only someone with a gentle disposition and fine appearance is allowed." The guest said, "What about someone like me?" The old lady hesitated and said, "The young man is very good, I also admire him greatly. After consulting with the granddaughter, if she agrees, then tonight is a good time, we will prepare the wedding pavilion, no need for a formal wedding." The guest was very pleased.

    352

    姥去，生伏窗听之，女云：“生年少浮动，春风邂逅，秋扇弃捐，儿何以堪耶？况彼无媒妁之言、父母之命哉！”姥云：“儿误矣，生至情人也。我辈混迹人寰，所欲皆其自择。仙凡路隔，谁见有尘世人为狐仙作蹇修者？媒妁可勿计，我将使彼自达其父母焉。”女云：“虽然，不可太急。”姥云：“前缘夙定，非老身之强为也。”女云：“红鸾未动，速则有灾。以儿卜也，当迟之两年，否恐不利。”姥云：“成事在天，儿勿虑也。”出谓生曰：“以公子才貌门第，有何不可。

    Explanation

    352

    The old woman left, and the young man crouched by the window listening. The girl said, "Young man, you are fickle and impulsive, having met in the spring breeze, only to be abandoned like a fan in autumn. How can I bear such treatment? Besides, he has not had the proper introductions from a matchmaker or the approval of his parents!" The old woman replied, "You are mistaken. He is a true lover. We, being mixed among mortals, choose our desires ourselves. The divide between the divine and the human is vast; who has seen a mortal act as a matchmaker for a fox spirit? We can disregard the matchmaker; I will make him reach out to his parents on his own." The girl said, "Even so, it should not be rushed." The old woman said, "Our fate is predetermined, not something I am forcing. The Red Phoenix has not yet moved; rushing could bring misfortune. Based on my divination, it should be delayed for two years, otherwise it might not be advantageous." The old woman said, "Success depends on fate, do not worry, child." She then went out and told the young man, "With your talents, appearance, and family background, what is there to be concerned about?"

    353

    第恐不能自专，归烦禀命尊人。如其协也，予择吉送女去。实告君，我辈狐仙也，与君有前缘焉。”生云：“区区之意，姥所深知。顾此言何以告父母？”姥云：“不妨。蓄有一物，归而献之，为言求婚，尊人必愿。如有回音，可于某日俟我于城东之石桥。”乃出一绢，包缄固甚密，赠生藏诸怀。导生出门，嘱云：“所约之期勿忘也。”返身遂去。生视之，松楸夹路，并无居庐。寻途而返归，不敢隐，实告其父。父异之，启其缄，重纸包裹，中一铜尺，其铭云：“尺非长，寸非短，宜子孙，垂久远。”洵汉代物，不禁咤异。

    Explanation

    353

    Firstly, I am afraid I cannot act on my own, and returning home, I will have to consult my elders. If it is in agreement, I will choose a good day to send my daughter away. Truly, I am telling you, we are fox spirits, and we have a predestined connection with you. The young man replied, "My humble intention, Grandmother is well aware of this. But how can I inform my parents about this?" The grandmother said, "It is not a problem. I have something stored, and upon returning home, you will present it to them, saying you are seeking a marriage alliance. Your elders will surely agree. If there is a response, you can wait for me at the stone bridge in the east of the city on a certain day." She then took out a piece of silk, tightly wrapped it, and gave it to the young man to keep in his bosom. She led him out the door and instructed him, "Do not forget the date we agreed upon." Turning around, she left. The young man looked around and saw pines and cypresses lining the road, with no dwelling in sight. He returned home by retracing his steps and did not hide the matter from his father. His father was surprised and opened the wrapping, finding it contained a copper ruler, inscribed with the words, "The ruler is not long, nor is the inch short; it is suitable for descendants and will last for generations." Indeed, it was a Han dynasty artifact, and he could not help but marvel.

    354

    先是生父司理，曾游咸秦刘太守幕。守酷好古玩，凡民间有古器，多方罗致之。有某生蓄一铜尺，守知之，设计强夺去。生忿，控诸抚，抚恶其贪，将坐赃免。守惧，遣人央抚，请以尺献，抚许之。盖抚好古之癖，更甚于守。守不得已割爱，启箧取尺，先一夕为其戚林某窃去，怀物遁归，至中途忽失去。守无所措，上台催索甚紧，而某生亦讼不休。守乃遣人寻觅，许以千金。固疑是林，而已遁去。知司理与林同里，来函乞司理搜求，言词哀恳。

    Explanation

    354

    Before his father, the magistrate, once served in the entourage of Governor Liu in the region of Xi'an. The governor was passionately fond of ancient artifacts and would go to great lengths to acquire any ancient objects from the people. There was a scholar who owned a copper ruler, which the governor learned about and devised a plan to forcibly take it. The scholar, angered by this, appealed to the governor, who disliked the governor's greed and was about to convict him of embezzlement and dismiss him. Fearing this, the governor sent someone to plead with the higher authorities, offering to present the ruler as a gift, which they agreed to. It turned out that the higher authorities had an even greater passion for antiquities than the governor. Unable to part with the ruler, the governor opened a chest to retrieve it, but it had been stolen the previous night by his relative Lin, who fled home with the object. On the way, Lin suddenly lost it. The governor was at a loss, as the higher authorities were pressing hard for its return, and the scholar continued to sue. The governor then sent someone to search for it, offering a thousand gold pieces as a reward. He suspected it was Lin, but Lin had already fled. Knowing that the magistrate and Lin were from the same hometown, the governor sent a letter asking the magistrate to search for it, using earnest and pleading words.

    355

    司理在幕府，知之甚悉，故今得此物，喜出望外，深感其情，欲结婚，且将诣咸阳谒守，指日登程。时有其内兄余某者，力阻之，云：“匹夫无罪，怀璧其罪，赃物之来，行踪谲异，留之不详，不如售之以去累。且狐仙亦不可为眷属，亟宜绝之。”司理信其言，以他词答守，藏尺于箧，不践仙盟。生愁思无聊，忽忽至半月，私至城东。姥果在，喜云：“我固知郎君不失信，奈尊翁决绝何？老身以千金相赠，何妄信邪说？窃恐祸至，阿菱已许人，郎君宜再寻佳丽可也。”生挽袂，大痛失声。

    Explanation

    355

    The judicial official, while serving in the military or administrative office, knew the matter very well. Upon obtaining the object, he was overjoyed beyond expectation and deeply felt the gratitude. He intended to marry the woman and was about to go to Xianyang to pay respects to the governor, setting off on the day. At that time, his brother-in-law, named Yu, strongly opposed him, saying, "A common man has no fault, but holding a precious jade invites blame. The stolen goods have strange and suspicious movements. Keeping them would be inauspicious; it would be better to sell them to get rid of the burden. Moreover, a fox spirit cannot be a spouse. One should immediately cut off the relationship." The judicial official believed his words and replied to the governor with other words, hiding the letter in a chest and not fulfilling the promise to the fox spirit. The young man was filled with grief and boredom, and suddenly, after half a month, he secretly went to the east of the city. The old woman was indeed there and said with joy, "I knew for sure that the young man would not break his promise. What to do with your father's decisive and final decision? I will give you a thousand gold pieces. Why believe blindly in heretical or false doctrines? Secretly fear that disaster will come. The woman named Aling has already been promised to someone. The young man should seek another beautiful woman." The young man wrung his hands and cried out in great pain.

    356

    姥云：“郎君收涕，予戏言耳。有一策，能从之尚可为。君家后院楼，屏人独居，俟夜有红灯，予即送女至。”如其教，果有灯贴槛际。就之，见阿菱坐楼上，光艳胜于初见时，遂成夫妇，栖于别业，俨然伉俪，至晓始去。于是凭肩望月，交臂谈心。女恒若不快，生怪诘之，女云：“凡事强合者必不能久，况婚姻大事乎！予两人恐不能常聚。”生以他辞掩之，女叹亦止。于是和诗猜谜，颇觉雅趣。一夕，灯前猜谜，生云：“昔人有禽字，猜为会少离多，颇确切。

    Explanation

    356

    The old woman said, "Young master, please stop crying; I was just joking. Here is a plan, if you can follow it, we can still be together. In your family's rear courtyard, seclude yourself in the small building, wait until there is a red light at night, and I will send the girl to you." Following her advice, indeed there was a red light attached to the railing. Approaching it, he saw Ailing sitting on the upper floor, her beauty surpassing what he had seen before. They then became husband and wife, residing in a separate estate, appearing as a proper couple, until dawn before leaving. Thus, they leaned on each other while gazing at the moon, talked intimately with arms around each other. The woman always seemed unhappy, and the man asked her curiously. She said, "Anything forced will not last long, especially in the case of marriage! We two are likely not to be together often." The man covered it up with other words, and the woman sighed and stopped. Then they composed poetry and played word games, finding it quite refined and interesting. One evening, by the light of the lamp, they guessed riddles. The man said, "There was an ancient character for bird, guessed as meeting little and parting often, which is quite accurate."

    357

    ”女闻之，愀然曰：“不如手到擒来为佳。”如是星离月会，约有半载，渐泄其事。生父忧之。适余至，生谋诸余。余为延术士粘朱符于楼窗，女大不怿，谓生曰：“妾言验矣。幽期密约，胡可以长？不如从此去，否则定遭奇祸。”生苦挽留，女终不乐。未几，术士作法于庭，仿佛有清烟自楼内出。术士云：“妖被擒矣。”举家相贺，女自此不再至。生神思迷惘，若有所失，独坐空斋，对影成两，乃赋一诗云：“楼上红灯不再过，凄凉孤月泛金波。从来佳丽尘寰少，自古书生薄命多。枕底香盟余绣履，奁中遗物剩青螺。

    Explanation

    357

    The woman heard this and looked solemn, saying, "It would be better if it were easily achieved." Thus, they had frequent meetings and separations for about half a year, gradually revealing their secrets. The father of the man was worried. Just as I arrived, the man consulted me. I arranged for a magician to place red talismans on the window of the tower. The woman was very unhappy and told the man, "What I said was true. Secret and intimate agreements cannot last long. It would be better to leave now, otherwise, we will surely face an unusual calamity." The man tried hard to persuade her to stay, but she was still unhappy. Soon after, the magician performed a ritual in the courtyard, and there was a faint mist coming from inside the tower. The magician said, "The demon has been captured." The whole family celebrated, and the woman no longer came. The man felt mentally confused and disoriented, as if he had lost something. He sat alone in an empty study, seeing his own shadow as if it were another person, and then composed a poem:

    "The red lantern will never pass again,
    A solitary moon reflects on the golden waves.
    From ancient times, beautiful and virtuous women are rare,
    Since ancient times, scholars have often been unfortunate.
    The remnants of a love vow under the pillow,
    The remaining items in the boudoir, the jade hairpin."

    358

    伤心最是黄昏后，独掩空帷涕泗沱。”反复吟咏，忧闷欲死。生父以为可庆，而余亦以功自诩，往来益密。因求铜尺，暂假观玩。司理出尺示之。未几二人痛饮，司理醉，余乘间窃去。及醒始知，急往寻余，余已买舟诣秦矣。盖欲持此以要太守之金也。司理急追之，及至秦，余已先到谒刘守，及探其行囊，则铜尺乌有矣，遂与守言：“铜尺在司理手，彼悍不欲与。”于是守衔之。司理至秦谒守，守有怼词，因述其情，守勿信，遣人招余，余已归矣。司理遂息于逆旅。

    Explanation

    358

    The saddest time is after dusk, alone closing the empty curtain, tears streaming down. Repeatedly reciting, feeling so distressed that one almost dies. My father thought this was a cause for celebration, and I also boasted of my own achievements, our interactions becoming more frequent. I then asked for the copper ruler, temporarily borrowing it to observe and play with. The official took out the ruler and showed it to me. Soon after, the two of us drank heavily, and the official became drunk. I took the opportunity to steal the ruler. Upon waking, he realized what had happened and hurriedly went to find me, but I had already bought a boat to go to Qin. He intended to use this to extort money from the governor. The official then stayed at an inn.

    359
