When placed in a narrow alley, it always returned. After several years, it gradually grew, and the family became accustomed to it and no longer found it strange. When given food, it could eat it. Now it is several feet long and as thick as a gourd, and it seems to have some awareness. The woman later gave birth to a child, and whenever she went out, she called the maggot to guard it. The maggot lay quietly on the side of the chest, and when the child crawled, the maggot blocked it. Whenever friends or relatives visited, the maggot would hide itself, and when they left, it would come out again. An old woman once saw it, and it still exists today, and it can appear and disappear mysteriously.

    401

    陈差官
    　　军械所差官陈某，目能视鬼，所言与纪文达公笔记略同。尝言：“日正午无鬼，余时皆有，夜则尤多。邑城内水月庵前后，至夜则坐者、卧者皆鬼也，遇人来则避之。或猝不及躲，为人冲倒，则其形散而不复能聚。”有某军门之子病笃，邀陈往视。陈云：“公子魂已离舍，恐不能愈，请为备后事可也。”未几果卒。或询其故，云：“凡人病甚，则魂必出其躯壳，恒在左右，离身不远。若离床则病笃，出房舍则必毙矣。”又云：“每清明、中元节、年终，鬼必还家取纸钱。尝见其家贫有不焚者，则鬼徘徊门左，状甚凄楚，至有零涕者。”问纸钱有益乎？云：“鬼所持者如真钱也。凡世所焚车马舆轿箱柜刍灵等物，至冥中俱化为真。”陈每天晚则厌出，自言每逢恶鬼，亦颇畏恶。故声明夜不当差。

    Explanation

    401

    Chen Cha Guan, an official from the Arsenal, named Chen, has the ability to see ghosts. His statements are similar to those recorded by Ji Wenda Gong. He once said: "At noon, there are no ghosts; at other times, there are ghosts, and at night, there are even more. In the city, in front of and behind the Water Moon Temple, at night, those who sit and those who lie down are all ghosts. When people come, they avoid them. If they cannot dodge in time and are knocked down by people, their form disperses and they cannot reassemble." A son of a certain military commander was gravely ill, and invited Chen to visit. Chen said: "The young master's soul has already left his body, and it is feared he will not recover. Please prepare for the afterlife." Soon after, the young master died. Someone asked him why, and he said: "When a person is very ill, their soul must leave their body, usually staying close by, not far from the bed. If they leave the bed, their condition worsens; if they leave the house, they will definitely die." He also said: "Every Qingming Festival, Ghost Festival, and Chinese New Year's Eve, ghosts must return home to collect paper money. I have seen that in poor families where the paper money is not burned, the ghosts wander around the gate, looking very sad, and some even shed tears." He was asked if burning paper money is beneficial. He said: "The ghosts hold the paper money as if it were real money. All the items burned as offerings, such as carriages, horses, sedan chairs, boxes, cabinets, and straw figures, turn into real things in the afterlife." Chen hates to go out at night, saying that every time he encounters evil ghosts, he is very afraid and disgusted. Therefore, he declares that he should not be on duty at night.

    402

    不得已夜出，则提灯低首疾行，目不邪视，言墙隅宅中阴暗密无人处，鬼多聚之。寻常所遇者大抵皆枯瘦愁惨多戚容，体胖容舒者甚少。又见有奇形怪状，头巨如丘，身高盈丈，青面朱发，如世塑之夜叉、魍魍极其丑恶者，不知人死后胡以化为此态，莫明其故也。每劝人夜行必须执炬，则鬼见火光自避。察其所言，似非尽诬。军械所与水月庵相连，庵前放花爆，陈戒其友人勿出视。是夕人众拥挤，压死数人，盖陈预见有厉鬼也。一日与友同往西沽，途中见一家双闭其门，陈曰：“是将有凶死者。”其友不信。未几有二人从内奔出，神色惊惶，访之其家，果有缢死者。友询陈何知，曰：“吾见有缢鬼入，故知其有变也。”友云：“既见鬼觅代，勿能救与？”曰：“不敢多事。”昔罗两峰目能视鬼，其所言者亦类此。然则无鬼论可勿广矣。

    Explanation

    402

    If one has no choice but to go out at night, they should carry a lamp, bow their head, and walk quickly, not looking sideways. They say that ghosts tend to gather in the dark and secluded corners of houses and alleys. The ghosts one usually encounters are mostly emaciated, sorrowful, and with many sad expressions; few are plump and relaxed. One also sees strange and unusual shapes, with heads as large as hills and heights of a full zhang (about 3 meters), blue-faced and red-haired, like the nightmares and goblins from the world's sculptures, extremely ugly. It is unknown why people transform into such states after death, and the reason is not known. Chen often advises people to carry a torch when walking at night, and ghosts will avoid the light of the torch. Considering what he says, it seems not entirely false. The military arsenal and the Shuiyue Temple are connected, and fireworks were set off in front of the temple. Chen warned his friends not to go out to see. That night, a crowd of people caused several deaths due to overcrowding, it seems that Chen had foreseen the presence of malevolent ghosts. One day, he went to Xigu with a friend. On the way, he saw a house with both doors closed. Chen said, "There will be a fatal death." His friend did not believe him. Shortly after, two people ran out from inside, with frightened expressions. When he visited the house, he found that there had indeed been a hanged death. His friend asked Chen how he knew, and Chen replied, "I saw a hanged ghost enter, so I knew there would be a change." His friend said, "If you see a ghost looking for a substitute, couldn't you save them?" Chen replied, "I dare not meddle." Formerly, Luo Liangfeng could see ghosts with his eyes, and what he said was also similar. Therefore, the theory that there are no ghosts should not be widely promoted.

    403

    樊英
    　　樊英者，狐女也。邑南村有刘氏子，劫孤。弱冠，美丰姿，读书颇慧。诣城内就师读，偶游街市，遇一女子，绿鬟垂鬓，绛纱短衫，裙下双翘如笋，爱好之，目不斜瞬。女微觉，红涨于面，掩袖而过。刘归，冥想废读，托故归家，眠餐顿减。忽一夕，月色昏黄，有叩扉者。出视，则向所遇女子也。延之入，倚灯坐，羞涩不语。刘惊喜，不知所措。半晌不言去。刘出视，家人皆将就寝，遂归斋，掩其扉，与共寝处。天将明，女自去。如是月余，人无知者。二人情好益笃。生穷其家室，语殊含混。生以爱故，亦不穷究。隐为蓄甘旨，女笑云：“无须，我能致之。”探袖中出果饵及肴酒，罔不精美。生每有所欲，应念辄至，因知其为狐仙。母为生议婚，力阻不愿。后为家人所窥，母令绝之，生不忍。

    Explanation

    403

    Fan Ying
    Fan Ying was a fox spirit. In a village to the south of the town, there was a Liu family son who had robbed orphans. At the age of twenty, he was handsome and intelligent. He went to the city to study under a teacher, and one day while wandering through the streets, he met a woman with green hairpins and black hair hanging down, wearing a red silk short jacket, with her skirt flaring out like bamboo shoots. He fell in love with her and did not look away. The woman noticed and blushed, covering her face with her sleeve and passing by. Liu returned home, lost in thought and neglecting his studies, claiming an excuse to go back home, and his sleep and meals decreased. One evening, the moon was dimly lit, and there was a knock on the door. He went out to see, and it was the woman he had met before. He invited her in, and she sat by the lamp, shy and silent. Liu was overjoyed but at a loss for what to do. She stayed for half an hour without saying goodbye. Liu went out to see, and all the family were about to go to bed, so he returned to his room, closed the door, and slept with her. Just as dawn was breaking, the woman left on her own. For over a month, no one knew about it. The two of them grew even more attached to each other. Liu asked about her family, and she spoke in a very vague manner. Because of his love for her, Liu did not press her for more information. She secretly provided him with delicious food, and she smiled and said, "It's not necessary, I can provide it for you." She took out fruits, snacks, and wine from her sleeves, all of which were exquisite. Whenever Liu had a desire, she would fulfill it immediately, and thus he knew she was a fox spirit. His mother arranged for him to marry, but he strongly resisted. Later, his family found out, and his mother ordered him to break off the relationship, but Liu could not bear to do so.

    404

    又数载，生呕血病危，女扶持倍谨，恒终日不去，家人佥呼为娘子。未几生亡，女披麻哭泣尽礼，稽首向母曰：“母请勿忧，此后奉养，新妇之责也。”生殡后，女去，留一巨布囊，云：“如有所需，探此可得。”母晨炊取米，果不爽。由是饥求食，寒索衣，皆仰给此囊，以故家稍裕，而女去如黄鹤矣。有邻村某氏子，乃杭人寓居者，相传得美妇，闻其状，颇类狐女。母因往视，新妇则樊英也。母怒，责其失节，且戒其家速绝，不然狐蛊亦类吾儿。樊英泣曰：“儿三岁为妇，未敢失德，且报母不为不至。夫死再醮，亦常之事。身虽嫁而犹养姑，以视悻悻出门视翁姑如寇仇者，毋将何以责之？”母语塞，盖隐刺刘姥也。母归，疑女与绝，及探囊，依然得物，始喜。终母之世，供给勿衰，母死始罢。或云樊英诚贤，惜其未明大节也。

    Explanation

    404

    After several years, the husband fell seriously ill and began coughing up blood. The daughter-in-law took care of him with great diligence, staying by his side all day, and the family began to call her "mother-in-law." Soon after, the husband passed away. The daughter-in-law wore mourning clothes, wept, and performed the necessary rituals, then bowed formally to the mother-in-law and said, "Mother, please do not worry. From now on, it is my duty to take care of you." After the funeral, the daughter-in-law left, leaving behind a large cloth bag, and said, "If you need anything, explore this and you can obtain it." Every morning, the mother took rice from the bag, and it always provided what she needed. As a result, the family became somewhat prosperous, and the daughter-in-law left mysteriously, like a yellow crane.

    There was a son from a neighboring village, who was from Hangzhou and currently living elsewhere, and who obtained a beautiful wife. When he heard her story, he found her quite similar to a fox girl. The mother went to see the new wife, who turned out to be Fan Ying. The mother was angry and criticized her for being unfaithful, warning her family to break off the relationship, or else the fox spirit would harm her son. Fan Ying cried and said, "I have been a wife for three years and have not dared to lose my virtue. I have taken care of my mother-in-law, and it is a common thing for a woman to remarry after her husband's death. Even though I am married, I still take care of my mother-in-law. Compared to those who leave angrily and treat their in-laws as enemies, how can you blame me?" The mother was speechless, realizing that she was indirectly criticizing Old Lady Liu. The mother returned home, suspecting that the daughter-in-law had left, but when she explored the bag, she found that it still provided what she needed, and she was relieved. She continued to provide support until her death, and some people said that Fan Ying was indeed virtuous, but it was regrettable that she did not understand the major principles.

    405

    醉茶子曰：明大节，人且难之，况狐耶？尝见某仆妇，与其夫伉俪颇笃。夫死家贫，瘗诸荒冢。越数载，自买新阡数亩，求其夫柩，不可辨识。或教以长绳系马鞍，缚绳腰际，曳之哭且行，有所阻，就其处掘之，果得夫柩。遂迁葬焉。噫！可谓贤矣。后晚年不贞，其主人有二童，先后私之。又某氏妇容貌娴雅，夫妇甚和。夫死不嫁，与某私，生子，伪为讨螟蛉者，以延夫嗣。之二妇者，可谓深于情而越于礼矣。一以夫骨为重，而必致之安土，但知瘗夫为义，不知贻夫耻为大不义也。其初心可取，特欲心炽，力不能遏，以至晚节不终。一以无后为负夫，不知犯淫为更负夫。然究其初心，未必真为夫嗣计。纵使真为夫嗣计，异姓螟蛉犹有乱宗之嫌，而乃辱身乞种，岂复有人理哉？特淫焰炽盛，居然生子，又不忍割爱，遂日留而养之，所谓机变之巧，无所用耻焉。总之，女德无极，第见其工貌胜人，即许为贤，奚可哉？前妇愚蠢，此妇淫而多机警矣。

    Explanation

    405

    The Tea-Drunk One says: Emphasize the great principles, even humans find it difficult, how much more so foxes? I once saw a servant woman who was deeply devoted to her husband. After her husband died and they were poor, she buried him in a desolate grave. After several years, she bought a new plot of land, and sought her husband's coffin, but could not identify it. Someone taught her to tie a long rope to a horse's saddle, fasten the rope around her waist, drag it while crying and walking. If it encountered an obstacle, she would dig at that spot, and indeed found her husband's coffin. She then moved it to a new burial place. Ah! She can be called virtuous. Later in her old age, she became unfaithful. Her master had two young men who had sexual relations with her, one after the other. Another woman, of a certain family, was elegant and refined, and her husband and she were very harmonious. After her husband died, she did not remarry, and had sexual relations with someone else, and had a child. She pretended to adopt a child as her husband's heir. These two women can be said to be deeply emotional but went beyond proper conduct. One valued her husband's remains and insisted on burying him in a proper place, knowing that burying her husband was right, but not realizing that bringing shame upon him was a great wrong. Her initial intentions were commendable, but her desires were intense, and she could not control them, leading to her later actions being dishonorable. One considered not having descendants a betrayal of her husband, but did not realize that committing adultery was an even greater betrayal. However, examining her initial intentions, she may not have truly been considering her husband's lineage. Even if she truly was considering her husband's lineage, adopting a child of a different surname is still suspicious of causing family disorder. And yet she dishonored herself by seeking a child. How could this be considered human behavior? Her lust was intense, and she actually had a child, and she could not bear to give it up, so she kept it and raised it. This is what is meant by cunning and resourcefulness making her ashamed of nothing. In summary, female virtue has no limit, but if one only sees that a woman is skilled and beautiful, and immediately considers her virtuous, how can that be right? The former woman was foolish, while this one was promiscuous but clever.

    406

    役夫
    　　辛卯夏，有挖河之役。有役夫数十人，共往宁河兴工之所。中有一夫，相失在后。日将暮，渴甚，而旷野无人，去驿亭绝远，徘徊道左。忽见禾黍深处有房舍，就之，闬闳壮丽，类世家。不敢遽入，蹲踞门旁，以待阍者。俄一女子红妆颇艳，自内出，询其何为。役夫言其始末，并求赐饮。女子笑云：“易耳。”乃延入，云：“我家固无男子，俟侬为尔煮茶。”俄而玉碗金壶，酌以佳茗。役夫饮毕，方欲辞行，女云：“日将坠矣，得毋饥乎？”役夫得茶已属望外，闻欲赐食，欢甚，急应以唯唯。

    Explanation

    406

    During the summer of the year Xin Mao, there was a labor project to dig a river. Among the dozens of laborers, one man became separated from the group and lagged behind. As the day was drawing to a close, he was extremely thirsty, and the vast wilderness was deserted, far from the nearest驿站. He wandered along the roadside, uncertain of where to go. Suddenly, he saw a grand house in the midst of fields of millet and rice. Approaching it, he found the gate and entrance to be magnificent, resembling those of a wealthy family. Daring not to enter immediately, he sat squatting by the door, waiting for someone to come out. Soon, a woman dressed in red, very beautiful, emerged from inside and asked him what he was doing. The laborer explained his situation and requested some water. The woman smiled and said, "It's easy." She then invited him in and said, "My family has no men, so I will brew tea for you." Soon, she served him fine tea in a jade bowl and a golden pot. After finishing the tea, the laborer was about to leave, but the woman said, "The sun is setting soon, are you not hungry?" The laborer, already surprised by receiving the tea, was overjoyed to hear that she would offer him food. He quickly agreed, responding with eager assent.

    407

    女子乃招至寝室，其中金玉锦锈，备极辉煌，所陈设之物，多不能指其名。于是坐立几不能自主。女子出厨间物列几上，罔不甘旨，役夫大肆饕餮。饭罢，女治枕栖，止之宿。役夫生平未谙此乐，自惭形秽，踌躇不安。女笑曰：“勿尔迂拘，苟愿永好，老于是乡，足尔一生温饱矣。”役夫居数日，见女子神气举止不类常人，转自恐，因云：“家有老母妻子，须身自作苦，以供赡养。若久留于此，八日冻馁矣。请暂去复来。”女云：“去则去耳，再来难矣。”乃送之出门去。役夫至工所，诸人怪其来晚。役备述其事，众夫共往，至其处，禾黍油油，迥无屋宇，诧异而返，未知是鬼是狐。

    Explanation

    407

    The woman then invited him to her bedroom, where gold, jade, brocade, and embroidery adorned the space, all shining with unparalleled brilliance. The objects displayed, most of which could not be named, filled the room. He could hardly stand or sit on his own, overwhelmed by the opulence. The woman brought out food from the kitchen, arranging it on the table. Everything was delicious, and the laborer indulged in a feast. After the meal, the woman prepared a bed for him and invited him to stay the night. The laborer, who had never experienced such pleasure before, felt ashamed of his own appearance and was hesitant and uneasy. The woman smiled and said, "Do not be so bound by convention. If you wish to stay forever, age here in this place, and it will suffice for your entire life's comfort." After staying for several days, the laborer noticed that the woman's demeanor and actions were unlike those of ordinary people, and he began to feel fear. He explained, "I have an elderly mother and a wife and children at home. I must work hard to support them. If I stay here for too long, I would freeze and starve in eight days. Please let me leave for now and come back later." The woman replied, "If you leave, you leave; but returning will be difficult." She then escorted him out the door. When the laborer returned to his workplace, his colleagues were surprised by his late arrival. He recounted the events, and they all went to the place he described. There, they found lush crops and no buildings, and they returned in astonishment, unsure whether it was a ghost or a fox.

    408

    醉茶子曰：闻之役夫蠢然一村汉耳，而倚翠偎红，竟得数日艳福，亦殊出人意外。古人云：“作得一日神仙，虽死无憾。”其役夫之谓乎？顾役之愚浊，不能终享清福，固无足论；而为仙子者，亦漫不加察，冒然纳之，陡然舍之，是果何心哉？世岂无翩翩美少，而适逢此蠢蠢者，遽纳之东床，想真仙断不出此，此必采补之流耳。
    浙生
    　　浙江某生，传者逸其姓氏。入北闱乡试。场后登车将返津门，途次遇雨。半里外有兰若，往投止焉。僧延入一斗室，颇雅浩。夜息灯寝，忽有啜泣声在承尘上，知为狐，姑听之。忽又作笑声，然稍止，辄复呜呜咽咽，声甚悲，旋又大笑。士大骇。未几，笑止而哭，哭止而笑。细辨之，乃出一人，心益不解。至天晓，始不复闻。忽有一人在旁问曰：“尔良夜不眠，忽哭忽笑，其意何居？”答曰：“我炼形拜月，吐纳采补，数百年来，甘苦备尝。

    Explanation

    408

    醉茶子曰：闻之役夫蠢然一村汉耳，而倚翠偎红，竟得数日艳福，亦殊出人意外。古人云：“作得一日神仙，虽死无憾。”其役夫之谓乎？顾役之愚浊，不能终享清福，固无足论；而为仙子者，亦漫不加察，冒然纳之，陡然舍之，是果何心哉？世岂无翩翩美少，而适逢此蠢蠢者，遽纳之东床，想真仙断不出此，此必采补之流耳。

    Translation:
    The Drunken Tea Master said: I have heard that a laborer, a simple village man, managed to enjoy the company of beautiful women for several days, which is quite surprising. An ancient saying goes: "Even if one becomes a god for a day, dying without regret." Is this what the laborer meant? Considering the laborer's lack of wisdom and inability to fully enjoy such pleasures, it is not worth discussing; but as for the fairy, she also acted carelessly, rashly accepting him and then abruptly discarding him. What was her intention? In the world, there are many handsome young men, yet she happened upon this simpleton and hastily accepted him as her future husband. It seems that a true fairy would not do such a thing; she must be a practitioner of alchemy or Taoist practices.

    浙生
    　　浙江某生，传者逸其姓氏。入北闱乡试。场后登车将返津门，途次遇雨。半里外有兰若，往投止焉。僧延入一斗室，颇雅浩。夜息灯寝，忽有啜泣声在承尘上，知为狐，姑听之。忽又作笑声，然稍止，辄复呜呜咽咽，声甚悲，旋又大笑。士大骇。未几，笑止而哭，哭止而笑。细辨之，乃出一人，心益不解。至天晓，始不复闻。忽有一人在旁问曰：“尔良夜不眠，忽哭忽笑，其意何居？”答曰：“我炼形拜月，吐纳采补，数百年来，甘苦备尝。

    Translation:
    A Scholar from Zhejiang
    A scholar from Zhejiang, whose name has been omitted in the records, entered the provincial examination in the northern capital. After the exam, he boarded a carriage to return to Tianjin. On the way, he encountered rain. Half a mile away, there was a Buddhist temple, and he went there to take shelter. The monk invited him into a small but elegant room. That night, as he extinguished the lamp and went to sleep, he suddenly heard weeping sounds above the ceiling. He knew it was a fox, so he decided to listen. Suddenly, the sound turned into laughter, but it soon stopped, and then the weeping began again, sounding very sad. Soon after, it turned into loud laughter. The scholar was greatly startled. Shortly after, the laughter stopped and weeping began, and then the weeping stopped and laughter began again. Upon closer examination, he realized that a person had appeared, and he was even more puzzled. By dawn, the sounds had stopped. Suddenly, someone appeared beside him and asked, "Why did you not sleep last night, crying and laughing? What was your intention?" The scholar replied, "I have been practicing physical transformation, moon worship, and breathing exercises for several centuries, experiencing both sweetness and bitterness."

    409

    至今日支体官骸，与人无异，大丹将成，此一乐也。倘顿遭雷劫，则夙昔功苦一朝瓦解，尽归灰烬，殊觉可惜。思量至此，不觉肝为之摧，心为之酸，潸焉出涕，几不知泪之所自来也。转思幸免雷殛，则从此升碧霄，登紫府，优游自得，与黄石、赤松辈共朝夕，将入王母之宫，而听董双成之雅乐，许飞琼之妙音，餐沆瀣，咽琼浆，食仙桃，长生不老，俯视尘世之冠冕，真若泥涂富贵，直如幻梦，不亦乐乎，又不禁为之狂笑。”一人曰：“尔休矣。凡修道之人，心宜淡定。尔私欲撞扰，倾刻万念，甚非法也。

    Explanation

    409

    Up to this day, my limbs and body are no different from those of others, and the great elixir is about to be completed, which is one joy. If I were to suddenly face a thunderbolt calamity, then my past efforts and hardships would disintegrate in a moment, all returning to ashes, and it would be truly very regrettable. Thinking to this point, I cannot help but feel my liver crushed and my heart pained, tears streaming down involuntarily, almost not knowing where the tears come from. Turning to think of being fortunate to avoid the thunderbolt, then from now on I will ascend to the blue sky, enter the purple palace, live in ease and satisfaction, sharing daily life with Yellow Stone and Red Pine and others, about to enter the Queen Mother's palace, and listen to the elegant music of Dong Shuangcheng, enjoy the exquisite sounds of Xu Feiqiong, eat the mist and dew, swallow the jade liquor, eat the immortal peach, live forever without aging, looking down on the crowns of the world, truly like mud and mire, just like a dream, isn't that joyful? And I cannot help but laugh uncontrollably." One person said: "You stop. All those who practice the Dao, the mind should be calm. Your selfish desires disturb, in a moment countless thoughts, very wrong.

    410

    ”其人又刺剌强辩。浙人闻之叱曰：“此等细事，值得悲喜交集？絮絮聒聒，扰我清眠。再不止，我便觅天师符印至。”再听之，则寂然。生暗喜狐之畏己也，方欲安眠，忽闻大呼曰：“某人，尔责我而不自责。汝前科乡试出场后，在京寓候榜，忽作得意想，则手舞足蹈，云。『我今秋折桂，明春攀杏，从此高车驷马，将华丽其衣服，夸耀夫乡里，高步青云，俯视一切，乐何如之！』于是拊掌大笑。

    410

    The man continued to nag and argue insistently. A Zhejiang native, upon hearing him, shouted, "Is such a trivial matter worth a mixture of sorrow and joy? Your incessant chatter is disturbing my peaceful sleep. If you do not stop, I will go fetch the talisman and seal of the Celestial Master." Upon hearing this, the man fell silent. The narrator secretly rejoiced that the fox feared him, just as he was about to fall asleep, suddenly heard a loud shout saying, "Mr. Someone, you blame me but do not blame yourself. After you left the provincial examination hall last year and awaited the results in the capital, you suddenly felt elated, dancing with joy and exclaiming, 'This autumn I will achieve success in the imperial examination, and next spring I will pass the higher exams. From now on, I will ride in a luxurious carriage, dress in fine clothes, show off in the village, rise to high positions, and look down on everything. How delightful it will be!' And with that, he clapped his hands and laughed heartily.

    411

    尔以我未之闻耶？俄而飞骑报入，尔神色张皇，坐卧不定，非喜非惧，非乐非忧，我口直不能形容其状。既而红箓无名，孙山被落，尔则顿足痛骂，涕泗交流。仆来则咤叱泄忿，店主人至则迁怒挥拳，指桑詈槐，丑态百出。尔以我未之见耶？今亦诮我耶？我亦自知忽悲忽乐厌人听闻，然不至如尔之令人难堪也。”生闻之益怒，奋身即起，闻承尘上嗤嗤笑声，云：“修道之人与读书之士，皆宜平心静气。仆自此闻命矣。一语奉赠，至重阳后勿复作前态。”再听则寂然矣。天明晴霁，登车就道，回思狐言，不觉哑然自笑。到津为友人述之，无不哄堂。

    Explanation

    411

    "Do you think I haven’t heard of it?" Suddenly, a messenger on horseback arrived, and your expression became panicked, unable to sit or lie down calmly, neither happy nor fearful, neither joyful nor sorrowful. I couldn’t describe it with words. Later, you failed the exam, and you stamped your feet and cursed vehemently, tears and snot flowing together. When I came, you shouted to vent your anger, and when the innkeeper arrived, you vented your anger on him with punches, blaming the wrong person and displaying all sorts of ugly behaviors. Do you think I haven’t seen it? Are you also mocking me now? I also know that I sometimes feel sad and sometimes happy, which is annoying to hear, but it’s not as bad as what you do, which is really uncomfortable. The man heard this and became even more angry, standing up immediately. He heard a mocking laugh from the ceiling, and the fox said, "Those who practice the Way and those who study should be calm and composed. From now on, I will follow your advice. I give you one piece of advice: after the Double Ninth Festival, don’t act like this anymore." After that, there was silence again. The next day, the sky cleared, and he got into his carriage and started his journey. Reflecting on the fox’s words, he couldn’t help but laugh silently. When he told his friends about it in the city of Jin, they all burst out laughing.

    412

    醉茶子曰：荣辱得失之心，虽仙不免。当局者苦于不自知，所难堪者旁观之匿笑耳。若以禅理论，万事皆空，不但雪窗萤火为多事，即炼形吐纳亦多事。果如是，成何世界耶？所以人当尽心竭力，以尽人事，成败富贵，一听诸天可也。
    树怪
    　　乙酉夏，移居城南第。庭有枯树，百余年物也。众以为钱神所凭，颇祈祷之。是岁除夕，邻家子见有白须叟踞树巅，予诗以嘲之。诗云：“恐予志怪少新奇，树露精灵树有知。暴客谅非来陋巷，老翁何故上枯枝。送穷愧我无长策，祈福嗤人枉费词。”“是鬼是仙还是盗，眼前傀儡太支离。皓首庞眉恍露身，无端惊喜动居邻。世间宁有摇钱树，天下何多妄想人。尔欲保身须匿迹，我将纵斧即摧薪。松能不老椿能寿，浪说仙灵恐未真。”

    Explanation

    412

    醉茶子曰：The heart that worries over honor, shame, gain, and loss, even immortals cannot escape. Those caught up in the situation suffer from not knowing themselves, and what is most difficult to bear is the silent laughter of onlookers. If we consider it from the perspective of Zen philosophy, all things are empty—diligent study by the light of snow and fireflies is a mere bother, and even Taoist practices of cultivating the body and breathing exercises are a bother. If that is the case, what kind of world is this? Therefore, one should give one's utmost effort, to fulfill one's human duties, and success or failure, wealth or poverty, should be left to the heavens.

    Tree Spirit
    In the summer of the year of the Fire Ox, I moved to a residence in the southern part of the city. There was an old, withered tree in the courtyard, a hundred years old. The people believed it was inhabited by a spirit of wealth, and they prayed to it quite often. On the eve of that year, a neighbor's child saw an elderly man with a white beard sitting atop the tree, and I composed a poem to mock him. The poem reads: "I fear my interest in strange things lacks novelty, but this tree reveals its spirit, showing that it is indeed sentient. A violent guest would not come to a humble lane, so why does an old man climb a withered branch? To send away poverty, I feel ashamed to lack a long-term strategy, and to pray for good fortune, I mock those who waste their words." "Is it a ghost, a immortal, or a thief? The puppet before my eyes is too fragmented. His white hair and broad eyebrows suddenly appear, and for no reason, he surprises and startles the neighbors. Is there truly a money tree in the world? How many deluded people are there in the world? If you wish to protect yourself, you must hide your presence, and I will let loose the axe and destroy the tree. The pine can remain young, and the cypress can live long, but it is said that immortals and spirits are probably not real."

    413

    夜游神
    　　夜游神，往往为人所遇。以予所闻者言之，一在邑东关外崇宁宫前，有王某夜行，见墙阴一物如袱，俯视乃巨靴，长约三尺许。举头则眉际复一靴，大亦相等。仰望一巨人，坐檐际，高约数丈，迭腿而坐。踌踌间，忽有一人提灯笼而来，巨人抬其足，其人若未之见，匆匆遂过。王亦欲随之过，巨人仍以足挡之。相持数刻，始不见。归家后，不数日而亡。殆衰气所感，鬼神揶揄之也。又某宦寓河北客舍，好樗蒲。正月间，访友人赌戏，归店时已三鼓，月色微明。至北关浮桥，见钞关东有巨人坐屋上，高以丈计，其服制仿佛纱帽宽袍，气象雄阔。某骇，几不能步。视所提之灯，光小如豆，踯蹰不前，俄而不见。某归后亦无恙。

    Explanation

    413

    Night Spirits

    Night spirits, often encountered by people. According to what I have heard, one such encounter occurred at the eastern gate outside the Chongning Temple. A man named Wang was walking at night and saw something like a cloth under the wall. He looked down and saw a large shoe, about three feet long. Looking up, he saw another shoe at the brow level, of similar size. He then saw a giant sitting on the eaves, about several meters tall, with legs crossed. As he hesitated, suddenly a person carrying a lantern came by. The giant lifted its foot, and the person seemed not to notice, passing by quickly. Wang also wanted to follow, but the giant blocked him with its foot. They stood there for a while before the giant disappeared. Wang returned home, and shortly after, he died. It seems that his declining health and the mockery of the spirits led to his death.

    Another official stayed in a guesthouse in Hebei and enjoyed playing a game of chance. One evening, he visited a friend to gamble and returned to the guesthouse at the third watch of the night. As he passed the northern gate bridge, he saw a giant sitting on a house east of the customs office, about several meters tall. The giant's attire resembled a wide-sleeved robe and a scholar's cap, with a majestic and expansive presence. The official was terrified and nearly unable to walk. He looked at the small lantern he was carrying, which seemed like a bean-sized light, and hesitated. Soon, the giant disappeared. The official returned home unharmed.

    414

    张桂
    　　邑有张桂者，寓御河北岸钞关东。其邻杨氏，与对门居。张偶夜归，见杨氏门前立一人，身极削瘦，凉帽青衣，肩披搭橐，中露公文数角，仿佛公人。张怪而就视，见其面白如粉，双目微圆如豆。悟其为鬼，急奔入家，闭其户。乃登梯再视，则乌有矣。是夜杨氏归卒。殆阴曹之勾役也。
    双头豕
    　　邑城中有某氏，产小豕两头，一身八足双尾者，群以为异。然不足异也，盖畜类如豚犬等，皆一产数子。猪之双首双尾一身八足者，乃其胎未分，粘合为一。如桃香瓜果中，有并蒂孪子者，亦二子未分，长而为一也。猪亦类是。若鹅之两头者，乃两雄共一雌，雄气杂也。或问犬先产者身小，后产者身转大，此何以故？曰：此最易解。先产者其后坐之胎也，后产者其先坐之胎也。胎先成者故大，后成者故小。及生时则后者在先，先者在后。此非大小之明验欤？近来颇讲格致之学，识者当不以吾言为河汉。

    Explanation

    414

    Translation

    Zhang Gui
    There was a man named Zhang Gui in the county, who lived east of the customs office on the northern bank of the Yellow River. His neighbor, the Yang family, lived opposite him. One night, Zhang happened to return home and saw a person standing at the Yang family's door. The person was extremely thin, wearing a cool hat and blue clothes, with a shoulder bag revealing several corners of official documents, resembling an official. Zhang was startled and went closer to look. He saw that the person had a face as white as powder, with small, round eyes like beans. Realizing it was a ghost, Zhang rushed into his house and closed the door. He then climbed a ladder to look again, but the person was nowhere to be seen. That night, the Yang family died. It was likely a messenger from the underworld.

    Two-Headed Pig
    In the county town, a certain family gave birth to a pig with two heads, one body, eight legs, and two tails. Everyone found this strange. However, it is not so strange, as animals like pigs and dogs often give birth to multiple offspring at once. The pig with two heads, two tails, and eight legs was simply a case where the fetuses had not separated and stuck together as one. It is similar to the phenomenon of twin fruits or flowers growing together, where two seeds have not separated and grow into one. Pigs are also like this. For example, a two-headed goose is the result of two males sharing one female, with mixed male energy. Someone asked why the first-born dog is smaller, while the later-born dog is larger. The reason is simple: the first-born dog is the one that sat behind, while the later-born dog is the one that sat in front. The earlier-formed fetus is larger, while the later-formed fetus is smaller. When they are born, the later-formed fetus comes out first, and the earlier-formed fetus comes out later. This is not a clear proof of size differences? Recently, there has been much discussion of the study of natural sciences, and those who understand will not consider my words as mere exaggeration.

    415

    杜醒山
    　　邑人杜醒山妻病，夜出延医，觉有拍其肩者。回顾阒其无人。去数武，遇其亡友王某，怪而呼之。不应。或先或后，随行半里许，旋失所在。俄有红旗绕足旋舞，俯拾则无。甚恶之，以为妻寿不永也。归家就寝。后闻有扣门者。出视，则其亡友赤身无衣，势将扑捉。一惊而寤，顿觉身冷如渥冰水。自此患病，遂至不起，而妻竟无恙。
    张绅士
    　　邑绅张某寝疾，夜有巨鬼鞠躬倒入其室，臀塞寝门几满。旋伸臂于床前，拳大如斗，以签示病者，票四月十四日。张大惊，鬼亦自去。时二月也，计其期尚余百日。乃至闰二月十四日而卒，始悟四月乃两月之隐语也。光绪庚寅年事。

    Explanation

    415

    Translation

    Du Xingshan
    The townsman Du Xingshan's wife was ill, so he went out at night to invite a doctor. He felt someone tapping his shoulder. When he looked back, there was no one there. After walking several steps, he met his deceased friend Wang, who was strange and called out to him. Wang did not respond. Sometimes ahead, sometimes behind, Wang followed him for about half a mile before suddenly disappearing. Soon, a red flag began to swirl around his feet, but when he bent down to pick it up, it was gone. He found this very ominous and thought his wife's life would not be long. He returned home to sleep. Later, he heard someone knocking on the door. When he went out to see, it was his deceased friend, naked and without clothes, as if about to grab him. He woke up in a start, immediately feeling his body as cold as ice water. From then on, he fell ill and eventually did not recover, while his wife remained unharmed.

    Zhang Scholar
    The town scholar Zhang was ill in bed, and at night a large ghost bowed and entered his room, its buttocks blocking the bedroom door almost completely. It then extended its arm in front of the bed, with a fist as large as a wine jar, showing a ticket to the sick man with the date April 14th. Zhang was greatly alarmed, and the ghost left on its own. At that time, it was February, and calculating the remaining time, there were still about a hundred days left. He died on the 14th of the second month in the leap year, and only then did he realize that "April" was a coded message for two months. This was during the year Gengyin of the Guangxu era.

    416

    陈某
    　　邑陈某入山买果品，路行失道。见荒郊一野寺，入投止焉。四面墙垣俱圮，似久无僧。正殿残破过半，东廊灰尘几满。窗下停一柩，若新殓者。乃趋西廊，展衾神座右。二更许，万籁无声，忽东廊柩爆然作响，侦视棺上坐一女子，模糊莫辨容色。大恐，乃曳衾匿案下，伏身屏气以听。半晌，有二健夫入，各携酒肴，踞地饮饫。其一劝饮甚殷，对坐者饮颇豪。未几，颓然醉卧，劝饮者遽抽刀决其首，尸倒案侧，头碌碌然滚神座下。旋至东廊柩前，呼曰：“我为尔报厥仇矣！”出门遂去。陈心胆俱碎。忽闻棺响，女子从棺内出，至西廊尸前，伏闻其首，旋起立大笑，声烈烈如鸮。陈毛发森竖，缩项不敢呼息。俄女子去。片刻棺响，女复来，如前状，旋又去。陈视天色已明，悄无人迹，遂狼狈而遁。

    Explanation

    416

    Chen of the County

    Chen, a man from a certain county, entered the mountains to buy fruit products but got lost on his way. He saw a wild temple in the remote wilderness and decided to take refuge there. The walls and fences around the temple were all collapsed, suggesting it had been without monks for a long time. The main hall was more than half ruined, and the eastern corridor was almost entirely covered in dust. Under a window, there was a coffin that seemed to contain a recently interred body. Chen then rushed to the western corridor and unfolded a quilt on the right side of the god's seat. Around the second watch of the night (around 2 AM), everything was silent, and suddenly, the coffin in the eastern corridor made a loud noise. He looked closely and saw a woman sitting on the coffin, her appearance blurry and indistinguishable. Frightened, he dragged the quilt and hid under the table, crouching down and holding his breath to listen. After a long while, two strong men entered, each carrying wine and food, and sat on the ground to drink and eat to their fill. One of them encouraged drinking very enthusiastically, and the one sitting opposite drank quite boldly. Shortly after, they fell asleep drunk. The one who encouraged drinking suddenly drew a knife and cut off the head of the other, who fell beside the table. The head rolled under the god's seat. They then went to the coffin in the eastern corridor and called out, "I have avenged your grudge for you!" They left the temple after that. Chen's heart and courage were shattered. Suddenly, he heard a noise from the coffin, and the woman emerged from inside. She went to the body in the western corridor, placed her head on it, and then stood up, laughing loudly like an owl. Chen's hair stood on end, and he shrank back, not daring to breathe. Soon, the woman left. A moment later, the coffin made a noise again, and the woman came out again, repeating the same actions, and then left again. Chen saw that the sky was already bright, and there were no signs of human activity. He fled in a state of panic.

    417

    郭氏妇
    　　邑人郭姓妇某氏，与其夫反目。诟谇之际，怒云：“如此相逼，我便自缢！”盖气愤语也。其邻陈姓者，目能视鬼，与郭妻遇，问曰：“嫂因何事而欲轻生？”氏言无之。陈云：“适见缢鬼相随，何便云无？”氏始悟前日语，而鬼因其隙而乘之也。

    Explanation

    417

    A Woman from the Guo Family

    A woman from the Guo family in the town, whose surname was Guo, quarreled with her husband. In the midst of the quarrel, she angrily said, "If you press me like this, I will hang myself!" This was said in a fit of anger. A neighbor surnamed Chen, who could see ghosts, encountered her and asked, "What is it that makes you want to take your own life, Sister Guo?" She denied it. Chen replied, "I just saw a hanging ghost following you. How can you deny it?" She then realized her previous statement, and understood that the ghost had taken advantage of her words.

    418

    醉茶子曰：十目十手，指视维严。言一出而鬼即乘之，以是知祸福之来，求之自己，言可妄发乎哉！
    分水箭
    　　海河滨有菜园，圃中结一瓠，长白异常。有术人口操南音，向老圃购此瓠。老圃云：“瓠老不堪食，留作种者。客买是奚为？”术士出重价，意在必售。老圃因其出重价也，靳不与。术士愈增价，老圃知其有异，云：“客不明告以故，虽千金弗易也。”术士不得已告曰：“津中三岔河底有分水箭，众流汇海而直下者恃有此箭，否则成巨浸矣。若得此宝，值千金。第有老龙看守，必以术取。今得此瓠，骑此可以下水，与老龙战。兹即实告，且有所烦：某日子夜与君同往，授君五色旗。待予入水后，俟有手出水上，见何色手，以何色旗与之，万匆惊骇。与毕静候片刻，予得宝出，当厚酬君。

    Explanation

    418

    The Drunken Tea Drinker said: "A hundred eyes and hands scrutinize with utmost rigor. Once words are spoken, ghosts immediately seize them; thus, one knows that calamities and blessings come from within oneself. Can words be spoken carelessly?"

    The Water Dividing Arrow

    There was a vegetable garden by the sea. In the garden, a gourd grew, unusually long and white. A man with a southern accent who practiced magic approached the old gardener to buy this gourd. The old gardener said, "This gourd is too old to eat; I kept it as seed. What use does the guest have for it?" The magician offered a high price, determined to buy it. The old gardener, seeing the high price, refused to sell it. The magician increased the price even more, and the old gardener, sensing something unusual, said, "You did not tell me the reason. Even if you offered a thousand gold pieces, I would not sell it." The magician, having no choice, explained, "In the Three Rivers where they meet at the sea, there is a water dividing arrow. The many streams flowing into the sea rely on this arrow to maintain their course; otherwise, they would form a vast flood. If you obtain this treasure, it is worth a thousand gold pieces. However, it is guarded by an old dragon, and you must use magic to retrieve it. Now, with this gourd, you can ride it into the water and fight the old dragon. I am telling you the truth, and I also have a favor to ask: on a certain night at midnight, I will accompany you. I will give you a five-colored flag. After I enter the water, wait until a hand appears on the surface. When you see what color the hand is, give the corresponding colored flag to it. Do not be startled. After I have obtained the treasure and come out, I will generously reward you."

    419

    ”老圃许之，乃摘瓠去。越数日，夜已交子，月明如昼，术士驾小舟邀老圃往。至则披发跣足，投瓠于水，跨之而下。旋见波浪翻沸，一巨手出水面，大如箕，色正赤，老圃急以赤旗与之。又数刻，出一黑手，大如前状，又以黑旗与之。旋见水波汹涌，高出于岸，小舟飘荡欲翻。老圃暗想：宝所以镇河，一方福也，为彼将去，水灾立至，吾其鱼矣。俄出一白手，圃以黄旗与之。倏水星迸沸，白手复出，圃又与以青旗。是时小舟摆拨欲沉，老圃急划船至岸，跃下观之。见白手出水上，半晌不退。四外水立如山，忽訇然震响如雷，波涛渐平，见术士尸浮上，身首异处，顺流而下。老圃自返。

    Explanation

    419

    The elderly gardener agreed, and then picked the gourd and left. After several days, at midnight, when the moonlight was as bright as daylight, the magician sailed a small boat and invited the old gardener to come with him. Upon arrival, the magician was barefoot and with long hair loose, threw the gourd into the water, and leaped down onto it. Immediately, they saw the waves churning violently, and a huge hand emerged from the water, as large as a winnowing basket, and was a deep red color. The old gardener quickly gave it the red flag. After another few moments, a black hand appeared, as large as before, and he gave it the black flag. Suddenly, they saw the water waves surging, higher than the shore, and the small boat was swaying and about to capsize. The old gardener thought to himself secretly: "The treasure is used to stabilize the river, a blessing for one area. If it is taken away, a water disaster will immediately follow, and I will become a fish." Suddenly, a white hand appeared, and the gardener gave it the yellow flag. Suddenly, the water stars burst forth, and the white hand reappeared, and the gardener gave it the blue flag. At that time, the small boat was swaying and about to sink, and the old gardener quickly rowed the boat to the shore, jumped down to observe, and saw the white hand emerging from the water, persisting for a long time without retreating. The water around stood like mountains, and suddenly there was a loud rumbling like thunder. The waves gradually calmed, and they saw the magician's body floating up, with head and body separated, floating downstream. The old gardener returned on his own.

    420

    土偶
    　　邑城隍祠两廊中，所塑土偶最精。凡油鼎刀山诸狱，削肠剖腹等形，无不神采生动，宛肖其真。庚寅岁重修，好事者塑一产亡鬼，翠蛾愁锁，红粉娇啼，两鬓蓬飞，七窍丹渍，背负雨伞蒲团，犹活泼类生。三月间，有某姓妇，患产难，危在旦夕。忽闻门外有号泣声，其家人出视，见一妇倚门呜咽，酷似庙中所塑者，惊疑间即失所在。旋闻产妇大呼有鬼，转瞬即气绝矣。后往往深夜辄出为祟，遂毁其像，乃安。盖物太肖人形，感异气即足为怪，况工人聚精凝神之作乎？

    Explanation

    420

    Clay Idols

    In the two side aisles of the city god temple in the district, the clay idols were the most exquisitely crafted. All depictions of various hells, including cauldrons of oil, mountains of knives, and the forms of cutting out intestines and opening abdomens, were all vivid and lifelike, exactly resembling the real thing. In the year of Gengyin, the temple was rebuilt, and a well-meaning person carved a clay figure of a woman who died during childbirth. She had green eyebrows furrowed with worry, red lips crying delicately, disheveled temples, and seven orifices stained with red. Carrying an umbrella and a cushion on her back, she still seemed lively like a living being. In March, a woman of a certain surname suffered from a difficult childbirth and was in imminent danger. Suddenly, she heard sobbing sounds outside the door. Her family went out to see and saw a woman leaning against the door, weeping bitterly, who was very much like the clay figure in the temple. Astonished and doubtful, they saw her disappear immediately. Soon after, the woman in labor shouted loudly that there was a ghost, and she stopped breathing in an instant. Later, the figure often appeared at night to cause mischief. They then destroyed the clay figure, and peace was restored. Because the object too closely resembled human form and was affected by strange energy, it was enough to be considered strange, how much more so, then, for the work of an artisan who concentrated all his energy and spirit?

    421

    剃发匠
    　　王某者，昔充营卒，曾从军江浙。后流落津门，为人剃发，与同寓三人俱患疫不起。见病者口鼻出血即毙，心知其异。觉己鼻口中血似欲滥，强忍而抑之。未几，神昏瞆，见二役持签提锁，势甚猛厉，将欲加之锁。一役曰：“姑待姑待，彼阳光尚旺，俟其血出，勾之不迟。”王闻之，心益惴惴。未几，胸如油沸，热不能遏，遂哇之，血出，顿清爽，觉魂一缕如烟，自顶上出。回顾二役，不知所往。乃出门信步行，飘飘然身轻如叶。欻至一处，道旁大席棚高于楼阁，棚中人来往纷杂甚伙。

    Explanation

    421

    The Barber

    Wang Mou, once a soldier who had served in Jiangsu and Zhejiang, later wandered to Tianjin, where he worked as a barber. He lived with three others in the same lodging, and all four fell ill with an epidemic that caused them to bleed from the mouth and nose before dying. Wang noticed that the symptoms were unusual. He felt as if blood was about to gush from his nose and mouth, and he forced himself to hold it back. Soon after, his mind became confused and dim, and he saw two officials holding a sign and a lock, approaching with a fierce and severe manner, as if they were about to put the lock on him. One official said, "Wait, wait, his vitality is still strong. Wait until the blood comes out, it won't be too late to take action." Hearing this, Wang's heart became even more anxious. Soon after, his chest felt like boiling oil, and the heat could not be contained. He vomited, and the blood came out, suddenly feeling refreshed. He felt his soul as a wisp of smoke, rising from the top of his head. Looking back at the two officials, he did not know where they had gone. Then he went out and walked leisurely, feeling light and airy, as if his body were as light as a leaf. Suddenly, he arrived at a place where a large thatched shed by the road was higher than a pavilion, and people inside the shed were bustling and numerous.

    422

    窥探间，二役忽至，猝以锁系其项，曰：“随我见官去。”王急与争。二人强曳之棚内，见北面有暖阁，捽之使跪，云：“听大人吩咐。”王仰视，见黑烟笼罩，暗不辨物。阁上责之曰：“尔何敢抗违公差，逗留不至？”王云：“小人非敢抵差，只因有未了之公事，是以迟迟。”问何事不了，曰：“方今中外交涉，难保必无事端，将为朝廷效死命耳。千把之职虽微，尽忠则一。但此热念不自能已，倘假数年，小人之愿也。如其数尽，又何敢违？”神默然，命左右稽其册，果以军功曾授千总不诬。神嘉其忠，赐寿一纪，遣之还。霍然遂苏。光绪庚寅三月事。今其人尚存。

    Explanation

    422

    In the act of spying, two guards suddenly appeared and quickly locked his neck with a chain, saying, "Come with me to see the officials." Wang hurriedly objected. The two men forcibly dragged him into the shed, where they saw a warm pavilion to the north. They roughly made him kneel and said, "Listen to the instructions of the great person." Wang looked up and saw a thick black smoke, making it too dark to see anything. The person on the pavilion reprimanded him, saying, "How dare you resist the public officials and delay your arrival?" Wang replied, "I do not dare to resist the order, but I have unfinished official business, which is why I have been delayed." They asked what business he had not completed, and he said, "At present, dealing with foreign affairs, it is difficult to guarantee that there will be no incidents. I will serve the court with my life. The position of a hundred-man commander is humble, but the duty of loyalty is the same. But this fervent desire cannot be suppressed. If given a few more years, it is the humble servant's wish. If my time is up, how dare I disobey?" The god remained silent and ordered the attendants to check the records. It was indeed true that he had been granted the rank of thousand-man commander for military merit. The god praised his loyalty, bestowed an additional twelve years of life, and sent him back. Suddenly, he woke up. This event occurred in the third month of the year Gengyin during the Guangxu era. The person is still alive today.

    423

    醉茶子曰：听其谲词，遽增其寿，神几近悠悠矣。虽然，言为心声，侃侃之论，竟出于剃发者之口，以是知无是心者无是言也。善则必赏，所以劝恶，神岂妄哉！
    蜈蚣
    　　邑西南村郊外有大蜈蚣，长约五尺余，宽半尺，出没不测。夜则有光如炬，照灼数步。村人患之，集众持梃冥搜，得于草莽中，盘伏如带。众击以梃，夭矫难制，众惧奔避。有二健夫奋力乱击始毙，归觉肢体麻术。视其二人之臂，均黑肿如墨。盖物最毒，虽未着手，而从梃递及。是知怪物不可以力敌也。

    Explanation

    423

    Translation:

    Drunken Tea Child said: Listening to his deceptive words, one suddenly feels their lifespan extended, and the divine seems almost vague. Yet, words are the voice of the heart, and this earnest discourse comes from the mouth of a shaved one. Thus, one knows that one who lacks such a heart will not speak in such a way. Good deeds must be rewarded, so as to encourage the wicked. How could the divine be false?

    Centipede

    In the outskirts of the southwest village of the town, there was a large centipede, about five feet long and half a foot wide, appearing and disappearing unpredictably. At night, it had a light like a torch, illuminating several steps. The villagers were troubled by it and gathered to search for it with sticks in the dark, finding it coiled and lying like a belt in the grass. They struck it with sticks, but its twisting movements made it difficult to control. Fearing it, they fled. Two strong men struck it with all their might until it died, and when they returned, they felt their limbs numb. When they looked at the arms of the two men, they were both black and swollen like ink. The creature is the most poisonous, even though it did not touch them, its poison spread through the sticks. Thus, one knows that monsters cannot be fought with brute force.

    424

    疟童
    　　苏州李氏妇，寄寓于津，患疟疾。昏乱中见一物如猫，跃登其榻。细视乃一小童子，绿衣红裤朱履，头绾双髻。向之笑，辄寒热交作，至昏昏睡去，则不知何作矣。如是数夕，悟其为疟鬼，欲驱之，而无术也。一夕，甫登床，作退缩状。妇返顾，见窗上有剖瓜刀一柄，因思必其所畏。次日以刀置枕畔，果不敢近。妇取以掷之，物吱吱嗥叫而遁。自是病愈。
    草偶
    　　邑梁氏，营造墓田极奢侈，旁建房舍亦宏壮。是村有富户某姓，秋获积粟盈仓，往往亏歉。怪而察之，殊无盗迹。疑仆，诮责之。仆冤甚，夜伺之。见二巨人头大如釜，身高丈余，月光下金铠辉煌，手持画戟，越垣过，直至仓房。门自启，鞠躬入，俯掬菽麦，饱饫而去。仆遥尾之，入梁氏殡宫，遂不见。次早白诸主人，乃集多人共往搜之，有送葬刍灵，方弼、方相二草偶，酷似所见。破其腹，得菽麦数十斛。火之，怪遂绝。

    Explanation

    424

    Malaria Demon
    A woman of the Li family from Suzhou, residing in Tianjin, fell ill with malaria. In her confused state, she saw something like a cat leap onto her bed. Upon closer inspection, it was a small boy with a green robe, red pants, and red shoes, his head tied in two buns. Whenever he laughed, she would alternately experience chills and fever, until she fell into a deep sleep, not knowing what was causing it. For several nights, she realized it was a malaria demon, wanting to drive it away but having no method. One night, as she was about to get into bed, she made a retreating gesture. The woman looked back and saw a knife for slicing melons on the window, thinking it must be what the demon feared. The next day, she placed the knife by her pillow, and the demon indeed did not dare to approach. She picked up the knife and threw it at the demon, which squeaked and howled and fled. After this, her illness healed.

    Grass Figures
    The Li family of the town constructed a cemetery plot extremely lavishly, with houses built nearby that were also grand. In this village, there was a wealthy family whose name is not specified, who had a bountiful harvest, filling their granaries with grain, but often suffered losses. They found this strange and investigated, but found no signs of theft. Suspecting their servant, they scolded him. The servant was very aggrieved and waited for them at night. He saw two giants with heads as large as cauldrons and heights over ten feet, their golden armor shining brilliantly in the moonlight, holding painted halberds, jumping over the wall and reaching the granary. The door opened itself, and they bowed and entered, bending down and scooping up beans and wheat, satisfied and leaving. The servant followed them from a distance and saw them enter the funeral hall of the Li family, where they disappeared. The next morning, he reported to the owners, who gathered many people to search the area. They found two straw figures, Fangbi and Fangxiang, used in funerals, which closely resembled what he had seen. They opened their stomachs and found dozens of shi of beans and wheat. After burning them, the strange occurrences ceased.

    425

    缸怪
    　　邑西关内民家有投缸毙者，时见怪异。一夕月明，院内有声如碌碡。转视之，一巨瓮中有人倒矗，竖其双足，旋转不休，犬吠随之。人见者辄病。其宅几易数主，居皆不安。有夏姓者寓是宅，拆房重修，于大门楼粱间得一术匣，中盛小泥缸长寸许，一小泥人倒竖其中，宛如昔之所见。方悟工师之妖术，致使居者遭凶祸焉。
    　　醉茶子曰：世所患之邪祟，大抵皆类此，不必真鬼魅也。顾工师镇魇之术，致使人投缸而毙，则邪术亦可畏也已。

    Explanation

    425

    Translation

    Strange Phenomenon in a Jar

    In a household within the western gate of the city, there were those who died by being thrown into a jar, and strange occurrences were often seen. One moonlit night, a sound like a threshing sledge was heard in the courtyard. Turning to look, a large jar was found with a person standing upright and inverted inside, with their feet upright, continuously rotating. Dogs barked in response. Those who saw it fell ill. The house changed hands several times, and all residents were uneasy. A man surnamed Xia lived in this house and decided to tear it down and rebuild it. While doing so, he found a magical box in the beam of the main gate. Inside the box was a small clay jar about an inch long, with a small clay figure standing upright and inverted inside, exactly like what had been seen before. It was then realized that the craftsman's witchcraft had caused the inhabitants to suffer misfortune.

    Drunk Tea said: The evil spirits that plague the world are mostly like this, not necessarily true ghosts or demons. However, the craftsman's binding and subduing technique, which caused people to die by being thrown into jars, shows that even evil magic is to be feared.
